public section

namespace Lean.Elab.Command

@[builtin_command_elab moduleDoc] def elabModuleDoc : CommandElab := fun stx => do
  let some range ← Elab.getDeclarationRange? stx
    | return  -- must be from partial syntax, ignore

  match stx[1] with
  | Syntax.atom _ val =>
    if getMainVersoModuleDocs (← getEnv) |>.isEmpty then
      let doc := String.Pos.Raw.extract val 0 (val.rawEndPos.unoffsetBy ⟨2⟩)
      modifyEnv fun env => addMainModuleDoc env ⟨doc, range⟩
    else
      throwError m!"Can't add Markdown-format module docs because there is already Verso-format content present."
  | Syntax.node _ ``Lean.Parser.Command.versoCommentBody args =>
    let docSyntax := args.getD 0 .missing
    if docSyntax.getKind == `Lean.Doc.Syntax.parseFailure then
      -- Report parser errors without attempting elaboration
      runTermElabM fun _ => reportVersoParseFailure docSyntax
    else
      runTermElabM fun _ => do
        addVersoModDocString range ⟨docSyntax⟩
  | _ => throwErrorAt stx "unexpected module doc string{indentD <| stx}"

private def addScope (isNewNamespace : Bool) (header : String) (newNamespace : Name)
    (isNoncomputable isPublic isMeta : Bool := false) (attrs : List (TSyntax ``Parser.Term.attrInstance) := []) :
    CommandElabM Unit := do
  modify fun s => { s with
    env    := s.env.registerNamespace newNamespace,
    scopes := { s.scopes.head! with
      header := header, currNamespace := newNamespace
      isNoncomputable := s.scopes.head!.isNoncomputable || isNoncomputable
      isPublic := s.scopes.head!.isPublic || isPublic
      isMeta := s.scopes.head!.isMeta || isMeta
      attrs := s.scopes.head!.attrs ++ attrs
    } :: s.scopes
  }
  pushScope
  if isNewNamespace then
    activateScoped newNamespace

private def addScopes (header : Name) (isNewNamespace : Bool) (isNoncomputable isPublic isMeta : Bool := false)
    (attrs : List (TSyntax ``Parser.Term.attrInstance) := []) : CommandElabM Unit :=
  go header
where go
  | .anonymous => pure ()
  | .str p header => do
    go p
    let currNamespace ← getCurrNamespace
    addScope isNewNamespace header (if isNewNamespace then Name.mkStr currNamespace header else currNamespace) isNoncomputable isPublic isMeta attrs
  | _ => throwError "invalid scope"

private def addNamespace (header : Name) : CommandElabM Unit :=
  addScopes (isNewNamespace := true) (isNoncomputable := false) (attrs := []) header

private def popScopes (numScopes : Nat) : CommandElabM Unit :=
  for _ in *...numScopes do
    popScope

def withNamespace {α} (ns : Name) (elabFn : CommandElabM α) : CommandElabM α := do
  addNamespace ns
  let a ← elabFn
  modify fun s => { s with scopes := s.scopes.drop ns.getNumParts }
  popScopes ns.getNumParts
  pure a

private def innermostScopeName? : List Scope → Option Name
  | { header := "", .. } :: _ => none
  | { header := h, .. }  :: _ => some <| .mkSimple h
  | _                         => some .anonymous -- should not happen

private def checkEndHeader : Name → List Scope → Option Name
  | .anonymous, _ => none
  | .str p s, { header := h, .. } :: scopes =>
    if h == s then
      (.str · s) <$> checkEndHeader p scopes
    else
      some <| .mkSimple h
  | _, _ => some .anonymous -- should not happen

@[builtin_command_elab «namespace»] def elabNamespace : CommandElab := fun stx =>
  match stx with
  | `(namespace $n) => addNamespace n.getId
  | _               => throwUnsupportedSyntax

@[builtin_command_elab «section»] def elabSection : CommandElab := fun stx => do
  match stx with
  | `(Parser.Command.section| $[@[expose%$expTk]]? $[public%$publicTk]? $[noncomputable%$ncTk]? $[meta%$metaTk]? section $(header?)?) =>
    -- TODO: allow more attributes?
    let attrs ← if expTk.isSome then
      pure [← `(Parser.Term.attrInstance| expose)]
    else
      pure []
    if let some header := header? then
      addScopes (isNewNamespace := false) (isNoncomputable := ncTk.isSome) (isPublic := publicTk.isSome) (isMeta := metaTk.isSome) (attrs := attrs) header.getId
    else
      addScope (isNewNamespace := false) (isNoncomputable := ncTk.isSome) (isPublic := publicTk.isSome) (isMeta := metaTk.isSome) (attrs := attrs) "" (← getCurrNamespace)
  | _                        => throwUnsupportedSyntax

@[builtin_command_elab InternalSyntax.end_local_scope] def elabEndLocalScope : CommandElab := fun _ => do
  setDelimitsLocal

/--
Produces a `Name` composed of the names of at most the innermost `n` scopes in `ss`, truncating if an
empty scope is reached (so that we do not suggest names like `Foo.«».Bar`).

If `n` is not specified, will use all scopes in `ss`.
-/
private def nameOfScopes : (ss : List Scope) → (n : Nat := ss.length) → Name
  | _, 0 => .anonymous
  | [], _ => .anonymous
  | s :: ss, n + 1 =>
    if s.header == "" then
      .anonymous
    else
    .str (nameOfScopes ss n) s.header

/--
Returns the first suffix of `base` that begins with `seg`, if one exists.

Note: this uses a naive, O(m*n) implementation for simplicity; we assume repeated partial overlap of
name components to be relatively uncommon, so that practical performance is closer to linear.
-/
private def findSuffixWithPrefix (base : Name) (seg : Name) : Option Name :=
  findSuffixMatch base seg true
where
  /--
  Helper for `findSuffixWithPrefix`. If `allowOffset` is `false`, then `seg` must be a suffix of
  `base`, not just a prefix of a suffix.
  -/
  findSuffixMatch : (base : Name) → (seg : Name) → (allowOffset : Bool) → Option Name
  | _, .anonymous, _ => some .anonymous
  | .anonymous, _, _ => none
  | .num p n, seg@(.num p' n'), allowOffset => do
    if n == n' then
      if let some nm := findSuffixMatch p p' (allowOffset := false) then
        return .num nm n
    if allowOffset then
      return .num (← findSuffixMatch p seg allowOffset) n
    else
      none
  | .str p s, seg@(.str p' s'), allowOffset => do
    if s == s' then
      if let some nm := findSuffixMatch p p' (allowOffset := false) then
        return .str nm s
    if allowOffset then
      return .str (← findSuffixMatch p seg allowOffset) s
    else
      none
  | .str p s, seg, allowOffset =>
    if allowOffset then
      return .str (← findSuffixMatch p seg allowOffset) s
    else
      none
  | .num p n, seg, allowOffset =>
    if allowOffset then
      return .num (← findSuffixMatch p seg allowOffset) n
    else
      none

private def throwNoScope : CommandElabM Unit :=
  throwError m!"Invalid `end`: There is no current scope to end"
    ++ .note m!"Scopes are introduced using `namespace` and `section`"

private def throwMissingName (name : Name) : CommandElabM Unit := do
  let hint ← liftCoreM <| MessageData.hint m!"To end the current scope `{name}`, specify its name:"
    #[← `(end $(mkIdent name))] (codeActionPrefix? := "Add scope name: ")
  throwError "Missing name after `end`: Expected the current scope name `{name}`{hint}"

/--
Produces a hint message with a suggestion to replace the name following `end` at the current ref
with the name given by `scopes` if there is only one active scope; otherwise, returns `none`.

Rationale: When there is only one active scope, only one valid `end` command is possible, so we
suggest it; if there are multiple, then it is harder to determine with confidence which the user
intended to end.
-/
private def mkSingleScopeReplacementHint? (scopes : List Scope) := do
  -- Recall that there is always an anonymous topmost scope, so `scopes.length = 2` when there is
  -- only one active scope:
  if scopes.length == 2 then
    let name := nameOfScopes scopes
    some <$> MessageData.hint m!"Use current scope name `{name}`:" #[(← `(end $(mkIdent name)))]
      (codeActionPrefix? := "Replace scope name: ")
  else
    return none

private def throwTooManyScopeComponents (header : Name) (scopes : List Scope) : CommandElabM Unit := do
  let hint ← liftCoreM do
    if let some hint ← mkSingleScopeReplacementHint? scopes then
      pure hint
    else
      let scopesName := nameOfScopes scopes
      pure <| MessageData.hint' m!"The name after `end` must be `{scopesName}` or some suffix thereof"
  throwError m!"Invalid name after `end`: `{header}` contains too many components" ++ hint

private def throwScopeNameMismatch (header : Name) (scopes : List Scope) (endSize : Nat)
    : CommandElabM Unit := do
  let correspondingScopes := nameOfScopes scopes endSize
  let allScopes := nameOfScopes scopes
  let help ← liftCoreM do
    if let some hint ← mkSingleScopeReplacementHint? scopes then
      pure hint
    else if let some suffix := findSuffixWithPrefix allScopes header then
      let hintMsg := m!"If you meant to end the outer scope(s) `{header}`, you must end all the \
        intervening scopes `{suffix}`:"
      MessageData.hint hintMsg #[← `(end $(mkIdent suffix))]
        (codeActionPrefix? := "Add intervening scopes: ")
    else if correspondingScopes != allScopes then
      pure <| .note m!"The current scopes are `{allScopes}`"
    else pure .nil
  throwError m!"Invalid name after `end`: Expected `{correspondingScopes}`, but found `{header}`" ++ help

private def throwUnnecessaryScopeName (header : Name) : CommandElabM Unit := do
  let hintMsg := m!"Delete the name `{header}` to end the current unnamed scope; outer named scopes \
    can then be closed using additional `end` command(s):"
  let hint ← liftCoreM <| MessageData.hint hintMsg #[← `(end)] (codeActionPrefix? := "Delete name: ")
  throwError m!"Unexpected name `{header}` after `end`: The current section is unnamed" ++ hint

@[builtin_command_elab «end»] def elabEnd : CommandElab := fun stx => do
  let `(end $[$header? $[.%$trailingDotTk?$_]?]?) := stx
    | throwUnsupportedSyntax
  let header? := header?.map (·.getId)
  let danglingDot := trailingDotTk?.join.isSome
  let endSize : Nat := match header? with
    | none   => 1
    | some n => n.getNumParts
  let scopes ← getScopes
  let numScopes := scopes.length
  if numScopes == 1 then
    throwNoScope
  match header? with
  | none        =>
    addCompletionInfo <| .endSection stx none false <| scopes.map (·.header)
    if let some name := innermostScopeName? scopes then
      throwMissingName name
  | some header =>
    if endSize >= numScopes then
      throwTooManyScopeComponents header scopes
    else
      addCompletionInfo <| .endSection stx header danglingDot <| scopes.map (·.header)
      let scopesName := nameOfScopes scopes endSize
      if scopesName != header then
        if scopesName == .anonymous then
          throwUnnecessaryScopeName header
        else
          throwScopeNameMismatch header scopes endSize
  modify fun s => {s with scopes := s.scopes.drop endSize }
  popScopes endSize

private partial def elabChoiceAux (cmds : Array Syntax) (i : Nat) : CommandElabM Unit :=
  if h : i < cmds.size then
    catchInternalId unsupportedSyntaxExceptionId
      (elabCommand cmds[i])
      (fun _ => elabChoiceAux cmds (i+1))
  else
    throwUnsupportedSyntax

@[builtin_command_elab choice] def elabChoice : CommandElab := fun stx =>
  elabChoiceAux stx.getArgs 0

@[builtin_command_elab «universe»] def elabUniverse : CommandElab := fun n => do
  n[1].forArgsM addUnivLevel

@[builtin_command_elab «init_quot»] def elabInitQuot : CommandElab := fun _ => do
  liftCoreM <| addDecl Declaration.quotDecl

@[builtin_command_elab «docs_to_verso»] def elabDocsToVerso : CommandElab := fun stx => do
  let xs := stx[1].getArgs
  for x in xs do
    if x.getKind == identKind then -- skip commas
      let declName ← liftCoreM <| realizeGlobalConstNoOverload x
      runTermElabM <| fun _ => withRef x <| makeDocStringVerso declName
      -- Add the info afterwards so the hover shows the updated docstring
      addConstInfo x declName

@[builtin_command_elab «export»] def elabExport : CommandElab := fun stx => do
  let `(export $ns ($ids*)) := stx | throwUnsupportedSyntax
  let nss ← resolveNamespace ns
  let currNamespace ← getCurrNamespace
  if nss == [currNamespace] then throwError "invalid 'export', self export"
  let mut aliases := #[]
  for idStx in ids do
    let id := idStx.getId
    let declName ← resolveNameUsingNamespaces nss idStx
    if (← getInfoState).enabled then
      addConstInfo idStx declName
    aliases := aliases.push (currNamespace ++ id, declName)
  modify fun s => { s with env := aliases.foldl (init := s.env) fun env p => addAlias env p.1 p.2 }

@[builtin_command_elab «open»] def elabOpen : CommandElab
  | `(open $decl:openDecl) => do
    let openDecls ← elabOpenDecl decl
    modifyScope fun scope => { scope with openDecls := openDecls }
  | _ => throwUnsupportedSyntax

open Lean.Parser.Term

private def typelessBinder? : Syntax → Option (Array (TSyntax [`ident, `Lean.Parser.Term.hole]) × BinderInfo)
  | `(bracketedBinderF|($ids*))     => some (ids, .default)
  | `(bracketedBinderF|{$ids*})     => some (ids, .implicit)
  | `(bracketedBinderF|⦃$ids*⦄)     => some (ids, .strictImplicit)
  | `(bracketedBinderF|[$id:ident]) => some (#[id], .instImplicit)
  | _                               => none

/--  If `id` is an identifier, return true if `ids` contains `id`. -/
private def containsId (ids : Array (TSyntax [`ident, ``Parser.Term.hole])) (id : TSyntax [`ident, ``Parser.Term.hole]) : Bool :=
  id.raw.isIdent && ids.any fun id' => id'.raw.getId == id.raw.getId

/--
  Auxiliary method for processing binder annotation update commands:
  `variable (α)`, `variable {α}`, `variable ⦃α⦄`, and `variable [α]`.
  The argument `binder` is the binder of the `variable` command.
  The method returns an array containing the "residue", that is, variables that do not correspond to updates.
  Recall that a `bracketedBinder` can be of the form `(x y)`.
  ```
  variable {α β : Type}
  variable (α γ)
  ```
  The second `variable` command updates the binder annotation for `α`, and returns "residue" `γ`.
-/
private def replaceBinderAnnotation (binder : TSyntax ``Parser.Term.bracketedBinder) : CommandElabM (Array (TSyntax ``Parser.Term.bracketedBinder)) := do
  let some (binderIds, binderInfo) := typelessBinder? binder | return #[binder]
  let varDecls := (← getScope).varDecls
  let mut varDeclsNew := #[]
  let mut binderIds := binderIds
  let mut binderIdsIniSize := binderIds.size
  let mut modifiedVarDecls := false
  -- Go through declarations in reverse to respect shadowing
  for varDecl in varDecls.reverse do
    let (ids, ty?, binderInfo') ← match varDecl with
      | `(bracketedBinderF|($ids* $[: $ty?]? $(annot?)?)) =>
        if annot?.isSome then
          for binderId in binderIds do
            if containsId ids binderId then
              throwErrorAt binderId "cannot update binder annotation of variables with default values/tactics"
        pure (ids, ty?, .default)
      | `(bracketedBinderF|{$ids* $[: $ty?]?}) =>
        pure (ids, ty?, .implicit)
      | `(bracketedBinderF|⦃$ids* $[: $ty?]?⦄) =>
        pure (ids, ty?, .strictImplicit)
      | `(bracketedBinderF|[$id : $ty]) =>
        pure (#[⟨id⟩], some ty, .instImplicit)
      | _ =>
        varDeclsNew := varDeclsNew.push varDecl; continue
    if binderInfo == binderInfo' then
      -- no update, ensure we don't have redundant annotations.
      for binderId in binderIds do
        if containsId ids binderId then
          throwErrorAt binderId "redundant binder annotation update"
      varDeclsNew := varDeclsNew.push varDecl
    else if binderIds.all fun binderId => !containsId ids binderId then
      -- `binderIds` and `ids` are disjoint
      varDeclsNew := varDeclsNew.push varDecl
    else
      let mkBinder (id : TSyntax [`ident, ``Parser.Term.hole]) (binderInfo : BinderInfo) : CommandElabM (TSyntax ``Parser.Term.bracketedBinder) :=
        match binderInfo with
        | .default => `(bracketedBinderF| ($id $[: $ty?]?))
        | .implicit => `(bracketedBinderF| {$id $[: $ty?]?})
        | .strictImplicit => `(bracketedBinderF| {{$id $[: $ty?]?}})
        | .instImplicit => do
          let some ty := ty?
            | throwErrorAt binder "cannot update binder annotation of variable `{id}` to instance implicit:\n\
                variable was originally declared without an explicit type"
          `(bracketedBinderF| [$(⟨id⟩) : $ty])
      for id in ids.reverse do
        if let some idx := binderIds.findFinIdx? fun binderId => binderId.raw.isIdent && binderId.raw.getId == id.raw.getId then
          binderIds := binderIds.eraseIdx idx
          modifiedVarDecls := true
          let newBinder ← mkBinder id binderInfo
          if binderInfo.isInstImplicit then
            -- We elaborate the new binder to make sure it's valid as instance implicit
            try
              runTermElabM fun _ => Term.withSynthesize <| Term.withAutoBoundImplicit <|
                Term.elabBinder newBinder fun _ => pure ()
            catch e =>
              throwErrorAt binder m!"cannot update binder annotation of variable `{id}` to instance implicit:\n\
                {e.toMessageData}"
          varDeclsNew := varDeclsNew.push (← mkBinder id binderInfo)
        else
          varDeclsNew := varDeclsNew.push (← mkBinder id binderInfo')
  if modifiedVarDecls then
    modifyScope fun scope => { scope with varDecls := varDeclsNew.reverse }
  if binderIds.size != binderIdsIniSize then
    binderIds.mapM fun binderId =>
      match binderInfo with
        | .default => `(bracketedBinderF| ($binderId))
        | .implicit => `(bracketedBinderF| {$binderId})
        | .strictImplicit => `(bracketedBinderF| {{$binderId}})
        | .instImplicit => throwUnsupportedSyntax
  else
    return #[binder]

@[builtin_command_elab «variable»] def elabVariable : CommandElab
  | `(variable%$tk $binders*) => do
    let binders ← binders.flatMapM replaceBinderAnnotation
    -- Try to elaborate `binders` for sanity checking
    runTermElabM fun _ => Term.withSynthesize <| Term.withAutoBoundImplicit <|
      Term.elabBinders binders fun xs => do
        -- Determine the set of auto-implicits for this variable command and add an inlay hint
        -- for them. We will only actually add the auto-implicits to a type when the variables
        -- declared here are used in some other declaration, but this is nonetheless the right
        -- place to display the inlay hint.
        let _ ← Term.addAutoBoundImplicits xs (tk.getTailPos? (canonicalOnly := true))
    -- Remark: if we want to produce error messages when variables shadow existing ones, here is the place to do it.
    for binder in binders do
      let varUIds ← (← getBracketedBinderIds binder) |>.mapM (withFreshMacroScope ∘ MonadQuotation.addMacroScope)
      modifyScope fun scope => { scope with varDecls := scope.varDecls.push binder, varUIds := scope.varUIds ++ varUIds }
  | _ => throwUnsupportedSyntax

open Meta

def elabCheckCore (ignoreStuckTC : Bool) : CommandElab
  | `(#check%$tk $term) => withoutModifyingEnv <| runTermElabM fun _ => Term.withDeclName `_check do
    -- show signature for `#check id`/`#check @id`
    if let `($id:ident) := term then
      try
        for c in (← realizeGlobalConstWithInfos term) do
          addCompletionInfo <| .id term id.getId (danglingDot := false) {} none
          logInfoAt tk <| .signature c
          return
      catch _ => pure ()  -- identifier might not be a constant but constant + projection
    let e ← Term.elabTerm term none
    Term.synthesizeSyntheticMVarsNoPostponing (ignoreStuckTC := ignoreStuckTC)
    -- Users might be testing out buggy elaborators. Let's typecheck before proceeding:
    withRef tk <| Meta.check e
    let e ← Term.levelMVarToParam (← instantiateMVars e)
    if e.isSyntheticSorry then
      return
    let type ← inferType e
    logInfoAt tk m!"{e} : {type}"
  | _ => throwUnsupportedSyntax

@[builtin_command_elab Lean.Parser.Command.check] def elabCheck : CommandElab := elabCheckCore (ignoreStuckTC := true)

@[builtin_command_elab Lean.reduceCmd] def elabReduce : CommandElab
  | `(#reduce%$tk $term) => go tk term
  | `(#reduce%$tk (proofs := true) $term) => go tk term (skipProofs := false)
  | `(#reduce%$tk (types := true) $term) => go tk term (skipTypes := false)
  | `(#reduce%$tk (proofs := true) (types := true) $term) => go tk term (skipProofs := false) (skipTypes := false)
  | _ => throwUnsupportedSyntax
where
  go (tk : Syntax) (term : Syntax) (skipProofs := true) (skipTypes := true) : CommandElabM Unit :=
    withoutModifyingEnv <| runTermElabM fun _ => Term.withDeclName `_reduce do
      let e ← Term.elabTerm term none
      Term.synthesizeSyntheticMVarsNoPostponing
      -- Users might be testing out buggy elaborators. Let's typecheck before proceeding:
      withRef tk <| Meta.check e
      let e ← Term.levelMVarToParam (← instantiateMVars e)
      -- TODO: add options or notation for setting the following parameters
      withTheReader Core.Context (fun ctx => { ctx with options := ctx.options.set `smartUnfolding false }) do
        let e ← withTransparency (mode := TransparencyMode.all) <| reduce e (skipProofs := skipProofs) (skipTypes := skipTypes)
        logInfoAt tk e

def hasNoErrorMessages : CommandElabM Bool := do
  return !(← get).messages.hasErrors

def failIfSucceeds (x : CommandElabM Unit) : CommandElabM Unit := do
  let resetMessages : CommandElabM MessageLog := do
    let s ← get
    let messages := s.messages;
    modify fun s => { s with messages := {} };
    pure messages
  let restoreMessages (prevMessages : MessageLog) : CommandElabM Unit := do
    modify fun s => { s with messages := prevMessages ++ s.messages.errorsToInfos }
  let prevMessages ← resetMessages
  let succeeded ← try
    x
    hasNoErrorMessages
  catch
    | ex@(Exception.error _ _) => do logException ex; pure false
    | Exception.internal id _  => do logError (← id.getName); pure false
  finally
    restoreMessages prevMessages
  if succeeded then
    throwError "unexpected success"

@[builtin_command_elab «check_failure»] def elabCheckFailure : CommandElab
  | `(#check_failure $term) => do
    failIfSucceeds <| elabCheckCore (ignoreStuckTC := false) (← `(#check $term))
  | _ => throwUnsupportedSyntax

@[builtin_command_elab «synth»] def elabSynth : CommandElab := fun stx => do
  let term := stx[1]
  withoutModifyingEnv <| runTermElabM fun _ => Term.withDeclName `_synth_cmd do
    let inst ← Term.elabTerm term none
    Term.synthesizeSyntheticMVarsNoPostponing
    let inst ← instantiateMVars inst
    let val  ← synthInstance inst
    logInfo val
    pure ()

@[builtin_command_elab «set_option»] def elabSetOption : CommandElab := fun stx => do
  let options ← Elab.elabSetOption stx[1] stx[3]
  modify fun s => { s with maxRecDepth := maxRecDepth.get options }
  modifyScope fun scope => { scope with opts := options }

open Lean.Parser.Command.InternalSyntax in
@[builtin_macro Lean.Parser.Command.«in»] def expandInCmd : Macro
  | `($cmd₁ in%$tk $cmd₂) =>
    -- Limit ref variability for incrementality; see Note [Incremental Macros]
    withRef tk `(section $cmd₁:command $endLocalScopeSyntax:command $cmd₂ end)
  | _                 => Macro.throwUnsupported

@[builtin_command_elab Parser.Command.addDocString] def elabAddDeclDoc : CommandElab := fun stx => do
  match stx with
  | `($doc:docComment add_decl_doc $id) =>
    let declName ← liftCoreM <| realizeGlobalConstNoOverloadWithInfo id
    unless ((← getEnv).getModuleIdxFor? declName).isNone do
      throwError "invalid 'add_decl_doc', declaration is in an imported module"
    if let .none ← findDeclarationRangesCore? declName then
      -- this is only relevant for declarations added without a declaration range
      -- in particular `Quot.mk` et al which are added by `init_quot`
      addDeclarationRangesFromSyntax declName stx id
    runTermElabM fun _ => addDocString declName (mkNullNode #[]) doc
  | _ => throwUnsupportedSyntax

@[builtin_command_elab Lean.Parser.Command.include] def elabInclude : CommandElab
  | `(Lean.Parser.Command.include| include $ids*) => do
    let sc ← getScope
    let vars ← sc.varDecls.flatMapM getBracketedBinderIds
    let mut uids := #[]
    for id in ids do
      if let some idx := vars.findIdx? (· == id.getId) then
        uids := uids.push sc.varUIds[idx]!
      else
        throwError "invalid 'include', variable `{id}` has not been declared in the current scope"
    modifyScope fun sc => { sc with
      includedVars := sc.includedVars ++ uids.toList
      omittedVars := sc.omittedVars.filter (!uids.contains ·) }
  | _ => throwUnsupportedSyntax

@[builtin_command_elab Lean.Parser.Command.omit] def elabOmit : CommandElab
  | `(Lean.Parser.Command.omit| omit $omits*) => do
    -- TODO: this really shouldn't have to re-elaborate section vars... they should come
    -- pre-elaborated
    let omittedVars ← runTermElabM fun vars => do
      Term.synthesizeSyntheticMVarsNoPostponing
      -- We don't want to store messages produced when elaborating `(getVarDecls s)` because they have already been saved when we elaborated the `variable`(s) command.
      -- So, we use `Core.resetMessageLog`.
      Core.resetMessageLog
      -- resolve each omit to variable user name or type pattern
      let elaboratedOmits : Array (Sum Name Expr) ← omits.mapM fun
        | `(ident| $id:ident) => pure <| Sum.inl id.getId
        | `(Lean.Parser.Term.instBinder| [$id : $_]) => pure <| Sum.inl id.getId
        | `(Lean.Parser.Term.instBinder| [$ty]) =>
          Sum.inr <$> Term.withoutErrToSorry (Term.elabTermAndSynthesize ty none)
        | _ => throwUnsupportedSyntax
      -- check that each omit is actually used in the end
      let mut omitsUsed := omits.map fun _ => false
      let mut omittedVars := #[]
      let mut revSectionFVars : Std.HashMap FVarId Name := {}
      for (uid, var) in (← read).sectionFVars do
        revSectionFVars := revSectionFVars.insert var.fvarId! uid
      for var in vars do
        let ldecl ← var.fvarId!.getDecl
        if let some idx := (← elaboratedOmits.findIdxM? fun
            | .inl id => return ldecl.userName == id
            | .inr ty => do
              let mctx ← getMCtx
              isDefEq ty ldecl.type <* setMCtx mctx) then
          if let some uid := revSectionFVars[var.fvarId!]? then
            omittedVars := omittedVars.push uid
            omitsUsed := omitsUsed.set! idx true
          else
            throwError "invalid 'omit', `{ldecl.userName}` has not been declared in the current scope"
      for o in omits, used in omitsUsed do
        unless used do
          throwError "`{o}` did not match any variables in the current scope"
      return omittedVars
    modifyScope fun sc => { sc with
      omittedVars := sc.omittedVars ++ omittedVars.toList
      includedVars := sc.includedVars.filter (!omittedVars.contains ·) }
  | _ => throwUnsupportedSyntax

@[builtin_command_elab version] def elabVersion : CommandElab := fun _ => do
  let mut target := System.Platform.target
  if target.isEmpty then target := "unknown"
  -- Only one should be set, but good to know if multiple are set in error.
  let platforms :=
    (if System.Platform.isWindows then [" Windows"] else [])
    ++ (if System.Platform.isOSX then [" macOS"] else [])
    ++ (if System.Platform.isEmscripten then [" Emscripten"] else [])
  logInfo m!"Lean {Lean.versionString}\nTarget: {target}{String.join platforms}"

@[builtin_command_elab Parser.Command.exit] def elabExit : CommandElab := fun _ =>
  logWarning "using 'exit' to interrupt Lean"

@[builtin_command_elab Parser.Command.import] def elabImport : CommandElab := fun _ =>
  throwError "invalid 'import' command, it must be used in the beginning of the file"

@[builtin_command_elab Parser.Command.eoi] def elabEoi : CommandElab := fun _ =>
  return

@[builtin_command_elab Parser.Command.where] def elabWhere : CommandElab := fun _ => do
  let scope ← getScope
  let mut msg : Array MessageData := #[]
  -- Noncomputable
  if scope.isNoncomputable then
    msg := msg.push <| ← `(Parser.Command.section| noncomputable section)
  -- Namespace
  if !scope.currNamespace.isAnonymous then
    msg := msg.push <| ← `(command| namespace $(mkIdent scope.currNamespace))
  -- Open namespaces
  if let some openMsg ← describeOpenDecls scope.openDecls.reverse then
    msg := msg.push openMsg
  -- Universe levels
  if !scope.levelNames.isEmpty then
    let levels := scope.levelNames.reverse.map mkIdent
    msg := msg.push <| ← `(command| universe $levels.toArray*)
  -- Variables
  if !scope.varDecls.isEmpty then
    let varDecls : Array (TSyntax `Lean.Parser.Term.bracketedBinder) := scope.varDecls.map (⟨·.raw.unsetTrailing⟩)
    msg := msg.push <| ← `(command| variable $varDecls*)
  -- Included variables
  if !scope.includedVars.isEmpty then
    msg := msg.push <| ← `(command| include $(scope.includedVars.toArray.map (mkIdent ·.eraseMacroScopes))*)
  -- Options
  if let some optionsMsg ← describeOptions scope.opts then
    msg := msg.push optionsMsg
  if msg.isEmpty then
    logInfo m!"-- In root namespace with initial scope"
  else
    logInfo <| MessageData.joinSep msg.toList "\n\n"
where
  /--
  'Delaborate' open declarations.
  Current limitations:
  - does not check whether or not successive namespaces need `_root_`
  - does not combine commands with `renaming` clauses into a single command
  -/
  describeOpenDecls (ds : List OpenDecl) : CommandElabM (Option MessageData) := do
    let mut lines : Array MessageData := #[]
    let mut simple : Array Name := #[]
    let flush (lines : Array MessageData) (simple : Array Name) : CommandElabM (Array MessageData × Array Name) := do
      if simple.isEmpty then
        return (lines, simple)
      else
        return (lines.push <| ← `(command| open $(simple.map mkIdent)*), #[])
    for d in ds do
      match d with
      | .explicit id decl =>
        (lines, simple) ← flush lines simple
        let ns := decl.getPrefix
        let «from» := Name.mkSimple decl.getString!
        lines := lines.push <| ← `(command| open $(mkIdent ns) renaming $(mkIdent «from») → $(mkIdent id))
      | .simple ns ex =>
        if ex == [] then
          simple := simple.push ns
        else
          (lines, simple) ← flush lines simple
          lines := lines.push <| ← `(command| open $(mkIdent ns) hiding $[$(ex.toArray.map mkIdent)]*)
    (lines, _) ← flush lines simple
    return if lines.isEmpty then none else MessageData.joinSep lines.toList "\n"

  describeOptions (opts : Options) : CommandElabM (Option MessageData) := do
    let mut lines : Array MessageData := #[]
    let decls ← getOptionDecls
    for (name, val) in opts do
      -- `#guard_msgs` sets this option internally, we don't want it to end up in its output
      if name == `Elab.async then
        continue
      let (isSet, isUnknown) :=
        match decls.find? name with
        | some decl => (decl.defValue != val, false)
        | none      => (true, true)
      if isSet then
        let cmd : TSyntax `command ←
          match val with
          | .ofBool true  => `(set_option $(mkIdent name) true)
          | .ofBool false => `(set_option $(mkIdent name) false)
          | .ofString str => `(set_option $(mkIdent name) $(Syntax.mkStrLit str))
          | .ofNat n      => `(set_option $(mkIdent name) $(Syntax.mkNatLit n))
          | _             => `(set_option $(mkIdent name) 0 /- unrepresentable value -/)
        if isUnknown then
          lines := lines.push m!"-- {cmd} -- unknown option"
        else
          lines := lines.push cmd
    return if lines.isEmpty then none else MessageData.joinSep lines.toList "\n"

@[builtin_command_elab Parser.Command.withExporting] def elabWithExporting : CommandElab
  | `(Parser.Command.withExporting| #with_exporting $cmd) =>
    withExporting do
      elabCommand cmd
  | _ => throwUnsupportedSyntax

@[builtin_command_elab Parser.Command.dumpAsyncEnvState] def elabDumpAsyncEnvState : CommandElab :=
  fun _ => do
    let env ← getEnv
    IO.eprintln (← env.dbgFormatAsyncState)

end Lean.Elab.Command
"}

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Module.SpanRankOperations
9	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.DiscreteValuationRing.Basic
10	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.KrullsHeightTheorem
11	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.KrullDimension.Field
12	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.KrullDimension.PID
13	
14	/-!
15	# Regular local rings
16	
17	For a Noetherian local ring `R`, we define `IsRegularLocalRing` as
18	`(maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = ringKrullDim R`. This definition is equivalent to
19	the dimension of the cotangent space over the residue field being equal to `ringKrullDim R`,
20	(see `IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace`).
21	
22	# Main Definition and Results
23	
24	* `IsRegularLocalRing` : A Noetherian local ring is regular if
25	  `(maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = ringKrullDim R`,
26	  i.e. its maximal ideal can be generated by `dim R` elements.
27	
28	* `IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace` : the equivalence of `IsRegularLocalRing` and
29	  `Module.finrank (ResidueField R) (CotangentSpace R) = ringKrullDim R`
30	
31	-/
32	
33	@[expose] public section
34	
35	open IsLocalRing
36	
37	/-- A Noetherian local ring is said to be regular if its maximal ideal
38	can be generated by `dim R` elements. -/
39	@[stacks 00KU]
40	class IsRegularLocalRing (R : Type*) [CommRing R] : Prop extends
41	    IsLocalRing R, IsNoetherianRing R where
42	  spanFinrank_maximalIdeal : (maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = ringKrullDim R
43	
44	variable (R : Type*) [CommRing R]
45	
46	lemma isRegularLocalRing_iff [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R] :
47	    IsRegularLocalRing R ↔ (maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank = ringKrullDim R :=
48	  ⟨fun ⟨h⟩ ↦ h, fun h ↦ ⟨h⟩⟩
49	
50	namespace IsRegularLocalRing
51	
52	lemma of_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_le [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
53	    (le : (maximalIdeal R).spanFinrank ≤ ringKrullDim R) : IsRegularLocalRing R :=
54	  (isRegularLocalRing_iff _).mpr (le_antisymm le (ringKrullDim_le_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal R))
55	
56	variable {R} in
57	lemma of_ringEquiv [IsRegularLocalRing R] {R' : Type*} [CommRing R']
58	    (e : R ≃+* R') : IsRegularLocalRing R' := by
59	  have := e.isLocalRing
60	  have := isNoetherianRing_of_ringEquiv R e
61	  rwa [isRegularLocalRing_iff, ← ringKrullDim_eq_of_ringEquiv e, ← map_ringEquiv_maximalIdeal e,
62	    Ideal.spanFinrank_map_eq_of_ringEquiv, ← isRegularLocalRing_iff]
63	
64	lemma iff_finrank_cotangentSpace [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R] :
65	    IsRegularLocalRing R ↔ Module.finrank (ResidueField R) (CotangentSpace R) = ringKrullDim R := by
66	  rw [isRegularLocalRing_iff, spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_eq_finrank_cotangentSpace]
67	
68	instance [IsLocalRing R] [IsDomain R] [IsPrincipalIdealRing R] : IsRegularLocalRing R := by
69	  by_cases isf : IsField R
70	  · let _ := isf.toField
71	    simp [isRegularLocalRing_iff, maximalIdeal_eq_bot]
72	  · apply of_spanFinrank_maximalIdeal_le
73	    rcases IsPrincipalIdealRing.principal (maximalIdeal R) with ⟨x, hx⟩
74	    simpa only [(IsPrincipalIdealRing.ringKrullDim_eq_one R) isf,
75	      Nat.cast_le_one, ← Set.ncard_singleton x, hx] using
76	        Submodule.spanFinrank_span_le_ncard_of_finite (Set.finite_singleton x)
77	
78	end IsRegularLocalRing

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.Algebra.Module.FinitePresentation
9	public import Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.Dual.Lemmas
10	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.AssociatedPrime.Finiteness
11	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Ideal.AssociatedPrime.Localization
12	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.LocalRing.ResidueField.Ideal
13	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Regular.IsSMulRegular
14	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Support
15	
16	/-!
17	
18	# Hom(N,M) is subsingleton iff there exists a smul regular element of M in ann(N)
19	
20	Let `M` and `N` be `R`-modules. In this section we prove that `Hom(N,M)` is subsingleton iff
21	there exist `r : R`, such that `IsSMulRegular M r` and `r ∈ ann(N)`.
22	This is the case if `Depth[I](M) = 0`.
23	
24	## Main statements
25	
26	* `IsSMulRegular.subsingleton_linearMap_iff` : for `R` module `N M`, `Hom(N, M) = 0`
27	  iff there is a `M`-regular in `Module.annihilator R N`.
28	
29	-/
30	
31	public section
32	
33	open IsLocalRing LinearMap Module
34	
35	namespace IsSMulRegular
36	
37	variable {R M N : Type*} [CommRing R] [AddCommGroup M] [AddCommGroup N] [Module R M] [Module R N]
38	
39	lemma linearMap_subsingleton_of_mem_annihilator {r : R} (reg : IsSMulRegular M r)
40	    (mem_ann : r ∈ Module.annihilator R N) : Subsingleton (N →ₗ[R] M) := by
41	  apply subsingleton_of_forall_eq 0 (fun f ↦ ext fun x ↦ ?_)
42	  have : r • (f x) = r • 0 := by
43	    rw [smul_zero, ← map_smul, Module.mem_annihilator.mp mem_ann x, map_zero]
44	  simpa using reg this
45	
46	lemma subsingleton_linearMap_iff [IsNoetherianRing R] [Module.Finite R M] [Module.Finite R N] :
47	    Subsingleton (N →ₗ[R] M) ↔ ∃ r ∈ Module.annihilator R N, IsSMulRegular M r := by
48	  refine ⟨fun hom0 ↦ ?_, fun ⟨r, mem_ann, reg⟩ ↦
49	    linearMap_subsingleton_of_mem_annihilator reg mem_ann⟩
50	  cases subsingleton_or_nontrivial M
51	  · exact ⟨0, ⟨Submodule.zero_mem (Module.annihilator R N), IsSMulRegular.zero⟩⟩
52	  · by_contra! h
53	    have hexist : ∃ p ∈ associatedPrimes R M, Module.annihilator R N ≤ p := by
54	      rcases associatedPrimes.nonempty R M with ⟨Ia, hIa⟩
55	      apply (Ideal.subset_union_prime_finite (associatedPrimes.finite R M) Ia Ia _).mp
56	      · rw [biUnion_associatedPrimes_eq_compl_regular R M]
57	        exact fun r hr ↦ h r hr
58	      · exact fun I hin _ _ ↦ IsAssociatedPrime.isPrime hin
59	    rcases hexist with ⟨p, pass, hp⟩
60	    let _ := pass.isPrime
61	    let p' : PrimeSpectrum R := ⟨p, pass.isPrime⟩
62	    have loc_ne_zero : p' ∈ Module.support R N := Module.mem_support_iff_of_finite.mpr hp
63	    rw [Module.mem_support_iff] at loc_ne_zero
64	    let Rₚ := Localization.AtPrime p
65	    let Nₚ := LocalizedModule.AtPrime p'.asIdeal N
66	    let Mₚ := LocalizedModule.AtPrime p'.asIdeal M
67	    let Nₚ' := Nₚ ⧸ (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (Localization.AtPrime p)) • (⊤ : Submodule Rₚ Nₚ)
68	    have ntr : Nontrivial Nₚ' :=
69	      Submodule.Quotient.nontrivial_iff.mpr <| .symm <|
70	        Submodule.top_ne_ideal_smul_of_le_jacobson_annihilator <|
71	          IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal_le_jacobson _
72	    let Mₚ' := Mₚ ⧸ (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (Localization.AtPrime p)) • (⊤ : Submodule Rₚ Mₚ)
73	    let _ : Module p.ResidueField Nₚ' :=
74	      Module.instQuotientIdealSubmoduleHSMulTop Nₚ (maximalIdeal (Localization.AtPrime p))
75	    have := isAssociatedPrime_iff.mp <| AssociatedPrimes.mem_iff.mp
76	      (associatedPrimes.mem_associatedPrimes_atPrime_of_mem_associatedPrimes pass)
77	    rcases this.2 with ⟨x, hx⟩
78	    have : Nontrivial (Module.Dual p.ResidueField Nₚ') := by simpa using ntr
79	    rcases exists_ne (α := Module.Dual p.ResidueField Nₚ') 0 with ⟨g, hg⟩
80	    let to_res' : Nₚ' →ₗ[Rₚ] p.ResidueField := {
81	      __ := g
82	      map_smul' r x := by
83	        simp only [AddHom.toFun_eq_coe, coe_toAddHom, RingHom.id_apply]
84	        convert g.map_smul (Ideal.Quotient.mk _ r) x }
85	    let to_res : Nₚ →ₗ[Rₚ] p.ResidueField :=
86	      to_res'.comp ((maximalIdeal (Localization.AtPrime p)) • (⊤ : Submodule Rₚ Nₚ)).mkQ
87	    replace hx : maximalIdeal (Localization.AtPrime p) = (toSpanSingleton _ _ x).ker :=
88	      hx.trans (by simp [SetLike.ext_iff])
89	    let i : p.ResidueField →ₗ[Rₚ] Mₚ :=
90	      Submodule.liftQ _ (LinearMap.toSpanSingleton Rₚ Mₚ x) (le_of_eq hx)
91	    have inj1 : Function.Injective i :=
92	      LinearMap.ker_eq_bot.mp (Submodule.ker_liftQ_eq_bot _ _ _ (le_of_eq hx.symm))
93	    let f := i.comp to_res
94	    have f_ne0 : f ≠ 0 := by
95	      intro eq0
96	      absurd hg
97	      apply LinearMap.ext
98	      intro np'
99	      induction np' using Submodule.Quotient.induction_on with | _ np
100	      change to_res np = 0
101	      apply inj1
102	      change f np = _
103	      simp [eq0]
104	    absurd hom0
105	    let _ := Module.finitePresentation_of_finite R N
106	    contrapose f_ne0
107	    exact (Module.FinitePresentation.linearEquivMapExtendScalars
108	      p'.asIdeal.primeCompl).symm.map_eq_zero_iff.mp (Subsingleton.eq_zero _)
109	
110	end IsSMulRegular

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Artinian.Module
9	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.LocalRing.MaximalIdeal.Basic
10	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Nakayama
11	public import Mathlib.RingTheory.Regular.IsSMulRegular

13	/-!
14	# Regular sequences and weakly regular sequences
15	
16	The notion of a regular sequence is fundamental in commutative algebra.
17	Properties of regular sequences encode information about singularities of a
18	ring and regularity of a sequence can be tested homologically.
19	However the notion of a regular sequence is only really sensible for Noetherian local rings.
20	
21	TODO: Koszul regular sequences, `H_1`-regular sequences, quasi-regular sequences, depth.
22	
23	## Tags
24	
25	module, regular element, regular sequence, commutative algebra
26	-/
27	
28	@[expose] public section
29	
30	universe u v
31	
32	open scoped Pointwise
33	
34	variable {R S M M₂ M₃ M₄ : Type*}
35	
36	namespace Ideal
37	
38	variable [Semiring R] [Semiring S]
39	
40	/-- The ideal generated by a list of elements. -/
41	abbrev ofList (rs : List R) := span { r | r ∈ rs }
42	
43	@[simp] lemma ofList_nil : (ofList [] : Ideal R) = ⊥ :=
44	  have : { r | r ∈ [] } = ∅ := Set.eq_empty_of_forall_notMem (fun _ => List.not_mem_nil)
45	  Eq.trans (congrArg span this) span_empty
46	
47	@[simp] lemma ofList_append (rs₁ rs₂ : List R) :
48	    ofList (rs₁ ++ rs₂) = ofList rs₁ ⊔ ofList rs₂ :=
49	  have : { r | r ∈ rs₁ ++ rs₂ } = _ := Set.ext (fun _ => List.mem_append)
50	  Eq.trans (congrArg span this) (span_union _ _)
51	
52	lemma ofList_singleton (r : R) : ofList [r] = span {r} :=
53	  congrArg span (Set.ext fun _ => List.mem_singleton)
54	
55	@[simp] lemma ofList_cons (r : R) (rs : List R) :
56	    ofList (r::rs) = span {r} ⊔ ofList rs :=
57	  Eq.trans (ofList_append [r] rs) (congrArg (· ⊔ _) (ofList_singleton r))
58	
59	@[simp] lemma map_ofList (f : R →+* S) (rs : List R) :
60	    map f (ofList rs) = ofList (rs.map f) :=
61	  Eq.trans (map_span f { r | r ∈ rs }) <| congrArg span <|
62	    Set.ext (fun _ => List.mem_map.symm)
63	
64	lemma ofList_cons_smul {R} [CommSemiring R] (r : R) (rs : List R) {M}
65	    [AddCommMonoid M] [Module R M] (N : Submodule R M) :
66	    ofList (r :: rs) • N = r • N ⊔ ofList rs • N := by
67	  rw [ofList_cons, Submodule.sup_smul, Submodule.ideal_span_singleton_smul]
68	
69	end Ideal
70	
71	namespace Submodule
72	
73	variable (M) [CommRing R] [AddCommGroup M] [AddCommGroup M₂]
74	    [Module R M] [Module R M₂] (r : R) (rs : List R)
75	
76	/-- The equivalence between M ⧸ (r₀, r₁, …, rₙ)M and (M ⧸ r₀M) ⧸ (r₁, …, rₙ) (M ⧸ r₀M). -/
77	def quotOfListConsSMulTopEquivQuotSMulTopInner :
78	    (M ⧸ (Ideal.ofList (r :: rs) • ⊤ : Submodule R M)) ≃ₗ[R]
79	      QuotSMulTop r M ⧸ (Ideal.ofList rs • ⊤ : Submodule R (QuotSMulTop r M)) :=
80	  quotEquivOfEq _ _ (Ideal.ofList_cons_smul r rs ⊤) ≪≫ₗ
81	    (quotientQuotientEquivQuotientSup (r • ⊤) (Ideal.ofList rs • ⊤)).symm ≪≫ₗ
82	      quotEquivOfEq _ _ (by rw [map_smul'', map_top, range_mkQ])
83	
84	/-- The equivalence between M ⧸ (r₀, r₁, …, rₙ)M and (M ⧸ (r₁, …, rₙ)) ⧸ r₀ (M ⧸ (r₁, …, rₙ)). -/
85	def quotOfListConsSMulTopEquivQuotSMulTopOuter :
86	    (M ⧸ (Ideal.ofList (r :: rs) • ⊤ : Submodule R M)) ≃ₗ[R]
87	      QuotSMulTop r (M ⧸ (Ideal.ofList rs • ⊤ : Submodule R M)) :=
88	  quotEquivOfEq _ _ (Eq.trans (Ideal.ofList_cons_smul r rs ⊤) (sup_comm _ _)) ≪≫ₗ
89	    (quotientQuotientEquivQuotientSup (Ideal.ofList rs • ⊤) (r • ⊤)).symm ≪≫ₗ
90	      quotEquivOfEq _ _ (by rw [map_pointwise_smul, map_top, range_mkQ])
91	
92	variable {M}
93	
94	lemma quotOfListConsSMulTopEquivQuotSMulTopInner_naturality (f : M →ₗ[R] M₂) :
95	    (quotOfListConsSMulTopEquivQuotSMulTopInner M₂ r rs).toLinearMap ∘ₗ
96	        mapQ _ _ _ (smul_top_le_comap_smul_top (Ideal.ofList (r :: rs)) f) =
97	      mapQ _ _ _ (smul_top_le_comap_smul_top _ (QuotSMulTop.map r f)) ∘ₗ
98	        (quotOfListConsSMulTopEquivQuotSMulTopInner M r rs).toLinearMap :=
99	  quot_hom_ext _ _ _ fun _ => rfl
100	
101	lemma top_eq_ofList_cons_smul_iff :
102	    (⊤ : Submodule R M) = Ideal.ofList (r :: rs) • ⊤ ↔
103	      (⊤ : Submodule R (QuotSMulTop r M)) = Ideal.ofList rs • ⊤ := by
104	  conv => congr <;> rw [eq_comm, ← Quotient.subsingleton_iff]
105	  exact (quotOfListConsSMulTopEquivQuotSMulTopInner M r rs).toEquiv.subsingleton_congr
106	
107	end Submodule
108	
109	namespace RingTheory.Sequence
110	
111	open scoped TensorProduct List
112	open Function Submodule QuotSMulTop
113	
114	variable (S M)
115	
116	section Definitions
117	
118	/-
119	In theory, regularity of `rs : List α` on `M` makes sense as soon as
120	`[Monoid α]`, `[AddCommGroup M]`, and `[DistribMulAction α M]`.
121	Instead of `Ideal.ofList (rs.take i) • (⊤ : Submodule R M)` we use
122	`⨆ (j : Fin i), rs[j] • (⊤ : AddSubgroup M)`.
123	However it's not clear that this is a useful generalization.
124	If we add the assumption `[SMulCommClass α α M]` this is essentially the same
125	as focusing on the commutative ring case, by passing to the monoid ring
126	`ℤ[abelianization of α]`.
127	-/
128	variable [CommRing R] [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M]
129	
130	open Ideal
131	
132	/-- A sequence `[r₁, …, rₙ]` is weakly regular on `M` iff `rᵢ` is regular on
133	`M⧸(r₁, …, rᵢ₋₁)M` for all `1 ≤ i ≤ n`. -/
134	@[mk_iff]
135	structure IsWeaklyRegular (rs : List R) : Prop where
136	  regular_mod_prev : ∀ i (h : i < rs.length),
137	    IsSMulRegular (M ⧸ (ofList (rs.take i) • ⊤ : Submodule R M)) rs[i]
138	
139	lemma isWeaklyRegular_iff_Fin (rs : List R) :
140	    IsWeaklyRegular M rs ↔ ∀ (i : Fin rs.length),
141	      IsSMulRegular (M ⧸ (ofList (rs.take i) • ⊤ : Submodule R M)) rs[i] :=
142	  Iff.trans (isWeaklyRegular_iff M rs) (Iff.symm Fin.forall_iff)
143	
144	/-- A weakly regular sequence `rs` on `M` is regular if also `M/rsM ≠ 0`. -/
145	@[mk_iff]
146	structure IsRegular (rs : List R) : Prop extends IsWeaklyRegular M rs where
147	  top_ne_smul : (⊤ : Submodule R M) ≠ Ideal.ofList rs • ⊤
148	
149	end Definitions
150	
151	section Congr
152	
153	variable {S M} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] [AddCommGroup M] [AddCommGroup M₂]
154	    [Module R M] [Module S M₂]
155	    {σ : R →+* S} {σ' : S →+* R} [RingHomInvPair σ σ'] [RingHomInvPair σ' σ]
156	
157	open DistribMulAction AddSubgroup in
158	private lemma _root_.AddHom.map_smul_top_toAddSubgroup_of_surjective
159	    {f : M →+ M₂} {as : List R} {bs : List S} (hf : Function.Surjective f)
160	    (h : List.Forall₂ (fun r s => ∀ x, f (r • x) = s • f x) as bs) :
161	    (Ideal.ofList as • ⊤ : Submodule R M).toAddSubgroup.map f =
162	      (Ideal.ofList bs • ⊤ : Submodule S M₂).toAddSubgroup := by
163	  induction h with
164	  | nil =>
165	    convert AddSubgroup.map_bot f using 1 <;>
166	      rw [Ideal.ofList_nil, bot_smul, bot_toAddSubgroup]
167	  | @cons r s _ _ h _ ih =>
168	    conv => congr <;> rw [Ideal.ofList_cons, sup_smul, sup_toAddSubgroup,
169	      ideal_span_singleton_smul, pointwise_smul_toAddSubgroup,
170	      top_toAddSubgroup, AddSubgroup.pointwise_smul_def]
171	    apply DFunLike.ext (f.comp (toAddMonoidEnd R M r))
172	      ((toAddMonoidEnd S M₂ s).comp f) at h
173	    rw [AddSubgroup.map_sup, ih, map_map, h, ← map_map,
174	      map_top_of_surjective f hf]
175	
176	lemma _root_.AddEquiv.isWeaklyRegular_congr {e : M ≃+ M₂} {as bs}
177	    (h : List.Forall₂ (fun (r : R) (s : S) => ∀ x, e (r • x) = s • e x) as bs) :
178	    IsWeaklyRegular M as ↔ IsWeaklyRegular M₂ bs := by
179	  conv => congr <;> rw [isWeaklyRegular_iff_Fin]
180	  let e' i : (M ⧸ (Ideal.ofList (as.take i) • ⊤ : Submodule R M)) ≃+
181	      M₂ ⧸ (Ideal.ofList (bs.take i) • ⊤ : Submodule S M₂) :=
182	    QuotientAddGroup.congr _ _ e <|
183	      AddHom.map_smul_top_toAddSubgroup_of_surjective e.surjective <|
184	        List.forall₂_take i h
185	  refine (finCongr h.length_eq).forall_congr @fun _ => (e' _).isSMulRegular_congr ?_
186	  exact (mkQ_surjective _).forall.mpr fun _ => congrArg (mkQ _) (h.get _ _ _)
187	
188	lemma _root_.LinearEquiv.isWeaklyRegular_congr' (e : M ≃ₛₗ[σ] M₂) (rs : List R) :
189	    IsWeaklyRegular M rs ↔ IsWeaklyRegular M₂ (rs.map σ) :=
190	  e.toAddEquiv.isWeaklyRegular_congr <| List.forall₂_map_right_iff.mpr <|
191	    List.forall₂_same.mpr fun r _ x => e.map_smul' r x
192	
193	lemma _root_.LinearEquiv.isWeaklyRegular_congr [Module R M₂] (e : M ≃ₗ[R] M₂) (rs : List R) :
194	    IsWeaklyRegular M rs ↔ IsWeaklyRegular M₂ rs :=
195	  Iff.trans (e.isWeaklyRegular_congr' rs) <| iff_of_eq <| congrArg _ rs.map_id
196	
197	lemma _root_.AddEquiv.isRegular_congr {e : M ≃+ M₂} {as bs}
198	    (h : List.Forall₂ (fun (r : R) (s : S) => ∀ x, e (r • x) = s • e x) as bs) :
199	    IsRegular M as ↔ IsRegular M₂ bs := by
200	  conv => congr <;> rw [isRegular_iff, ne_comm, ← Quotient.nontrivial_iff]
201	  let e' := QuotientAddGroup.congr _ _ e <|
202	    AddHom.map_smul_top_toAddSubgroup_of_surjective e.surjective h
203	  exact and_congr (e.isWeaklyRegular_congr h) e'.nontrivial_congr
204	
205	lemma _root_.LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr' (e : M ≃ₛₗ[σ] M₂) (rs : List R) :
206	    IsRegular M rs ↔ IsRegular M₂ (rs.map σ) :=
207	  e.toAddEquiv.isRegular_congr <| List.forall₂_map_right_iff.mpr <|
208	    List.forall₂_same.mpr fun r _ x => e.map_smul' r x
209	
210	lemma _root_.LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr [Module R M₂] (e : M ≃ₗ[R] M₂) (rs : List R) :
211	    IsRegular M rs ↔ IsRegular M₂ rs :=
212	  Iff.trans (e.isRegular_congr' rs) <| iff_of_eq <| congrArg _ rs.map_id
213	
214	end Congr
215	
216	lemma isWeaklyRegular_map_algebraMap_iff [CommRing R] [CommRing S]
217	    [Algebra R S] [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [Module S M]
218	    [IsScalarTower R S M] (rs : List R) :
219	    IsWeaklyRegular M (rs.map (algebraMap R S)) ↔ IsWeaklyRegular M rs :=
220	  (AddEquiv.refl M).isWeaklyRegular_congr <| List.forall₂_map_left_iff.mpr <|
221	    List.forall₂_same.mpr fun r _ => algebraMap_smul S r
222	
223	variable [CommRing R] [AddCommGroup M] [AddCommGroup M₂] [AddCommGroup M₃]
224	    [AddCommGroup M₄] [Module R M] [Module R M₂] [Module R M₃] [Module R M₄]
225	
226	@[simp]
227	lemma isWeaklyRegular_cons_iff (r : R) (rs : List R) :
228	    IsWeaklyRegular M (r :: rs) ↔
229	      IsSMulRegular M r ∧ IsWeaklyRegular (QuotSMulTop r M) rs :=
230	  have := Eq.trans (congrArg (· • ⊤) Ideal.ofList_nil) (bot_smul ⊤)
231	  let e i := quotOfListConsSMulTopEquivQuotSMulTopInner M r (rs.take i)
232	  Iff.trans (isWeaklyRegular_iff_Fin _ _) <| Iff.trans Fin.forall_iff_succ <|
233	    and_congr ((quotEquivOfEqBot _ this).isSMulRegular_congr r) <|
234	      Iff.trans (forall_congr' fun i => (e i).isSMulRegular_congr (rs.get i))
235	        (isWeaklyRegular_iff_Fin _ _).symm
236	
237	lemma isWeaklyRegular_cons_iff' (r : R) (rs : List R) :
238	    IsWeaklyRegular M (r :: rs) ↔
239	      IsSMulRegular M r ∧
240	        IsWeaklyRegular (QuotSMulTop r M)
241	          (rs.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {r}))) :=
242	  Iff.trans (isWeaklyRegular_cons_iff M r rs) <| and_congr_right' <|
243	    Iff.symm <| isWeaklyRegular_map_algebraMap_iff (R ⧸ Ideal.span {r}) _ rs
244	
245	@[simp]
246	lemma isRegular_cons_iff (r : R) (rs : List R) :
247	    IsRegular M (r :: rs) ↔
248	      IsSMulRegular M r ∧ IsRegular (QuotSMulTop r M) rs := by
249	  rw [isRegular_iff, isRegular_iff, isWeaklyRegular_cons_iff M r rs,
250	    ne_eq, top_eq_ofList_cons_smul_iff, and_assoc]
251	
252	lemma isRegular_cons_iff' (r : R) (rs : List R) :
253	    IsRegular M (r :: rs) ↔
254	      IsSMulRegular M r ∧ IsRegular (QuotSMulTop r M)
255	          (rs.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {r}))) := by
256	  conv => congr <;> rw [isRegular_iff, ne_eq]
257	  rw [isWeaklyRegular_cons_iff', ← restrictScalars_inj R (R ⧸ _),
258	    ← Ideal.map_ofList, ← Ideal.Quotient.algebraMap_eq, Ideal.smul_restrictScalars,
259	    restrictScalars_top, top_eq_ofList_cons_smul_iff, and_assoc]
260	
261	variable {M}
262	
263	namespace IsWeaklyRegular
264	
265	variable (R M) in
266	@[simp] lemma nil : IsWeaklyRegular M ([] : List R) :=
267	  .mk (False.elim <| Nat.not_lt_zero · ·)
268	
269	lemma cons {r : R} {rs : List R} (h1 : IsSMulRegular M r)
270	    (h2 : IsWeaklyRegular (QuotSMulTop r M) rs) : IsWeaklyRegular M (r :: rs) :=
271	  (isWeaklyRegular_cons_iff M r rs).mpr ⟨h1, h2⟩
272	
273	lemma cons' {r : R} {rs : List R} (h1 : IsSMulRegular M r)
274	    (h2 : IsWeaklyRegular (QuotSMulTop r M)
275	            (rs.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {r})))) :
276	    IsWeaklyRegular M (r :: rs) :=
277	  (isWeaklyRegular_cons_iff' M r rs).mpr ⟨h1, h2⟩
278	
279	/-- Weakly regular sequences can be inductively characterized by:
280	* The empty sequence is weakly regular on any module.
281	* If `r` is regular on `M` and `rs` is a weakly regular sequence on `M⧸rM` then
282	  the sequence obtained from `rs` by prepending `r` is weakly regular on `M`.
283	
284	This is the induction principle produced by the inductive definition above.
285	The motive will usually be valued in `Prop`, but `Sort*` works too. -/
286	@[induction_eliminator]
287	def recIterModByRegular
288	    {motive : (M : Type v) → [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → (rs : List R) →
289	      IsWeaklyRegular M rs → Sort*}
290	    (nil : (M : Type v) → [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → motive M [] (nil R M))
291	    (cons : {M : Type v} → [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → (r : R) →
292	      (rs : List R) → (h1 : IsSMulRegular M r) →
293	      (h2 : IsWeaklyRegular (QuotSMulTop r M) rs) →
294	      (ih : motive (QuotSMulTop r M) rs h2) → motive M (r :: rs) (cons h1 h2)) :
295	    {M : Type v} → [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → {rs : List R} →
296	    (h : IsWeaklyRegular M rs) → motive M rs h
297	  | M, _, _, [], _ => nil M
298	  | M, _, _, r :: rs, h =>
299	    let ⟨h1, h2⟩ := (isWeaklyRegular_cons_iff M r rs).mp h
300	    cons r rs h1 h2 (recIterModByRegular nil cons h2)
301	
302	/-- A simplified version of `IsWeaklyRegular.recIterModByRegular` where the
303	motive is not allowed to depend on the proof of `IsWeaklyRegular`. -/
304	def ndrecIterModByRegular
305	    {motive : (M : Type v) → [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → (rs : List R) → Sort*}
306	    (nil : (M : Type v) → [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → motive M [])
307	    (cons : {M : Type v} → [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → (r : R) →
308	      (rs : List R) → IsSMulRegular M r → IsWeaklyRegular (QuotSMulTop r M) rs →
309	      motive (QuotSMulTop r M) rs → motive M (r :: rs))
310	    {M} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] {rs} :
311	    IsWeaklyRegular M rs → motive M rs :=
312	  recIterModByRegular (motive := fun M _ _ rs _ => motive M rs) nil cons
313	
314	/-- An alternate induction principle from `IsWeaklyRegular.recIterModByRegular`
315	where we mod out by successive elements in both the module and the base ring.
316	This is useful for propagating certain properties of the initial `M`, e.g.
317	faithfulness or freeness, throughout the induction. -/
318	def recIterModByRegularWithRing
319	    {motive : (R : Type u) → [CommRing R] → (M : Type v) → [AddCommGroup M] →
320	      [Module R M] → (rs : List R) → IsWeaklyRegular M rs → Sort*}
321	    (nil : (R : Type u) → [CommRing R] → (M : Type v) → [AddCommGroup M] →
322	      [Module R M] → motive R M [] (nil R M))
323	    (cons : {R : Type u} → [CommRing R] → {M : Type v} → [AddCommGroup M] →
324	      [Module R M] → (r : R) → (rs : List R) → (h1 : IsSMulRegular M r) →
325	      (h2 : IsWeaklyRegular (QuotSMulTop r M)
326	              (rs.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {r})))) →
327	      (ih : motive (R ⧸ Ideal.span {r}) (QuotSMulTop r M)
328	              (rs.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {r}))) h2) →
329	            motive R M (r :: rs) (cons' h1 h2)) :
330	    {R : Type u} → [CommRing R] → {M : Type v} → [AddCommGroup M] →
331	    [Module R M] → {rs : List R} → (h : IsWeaklyRegular M rs) → motive R M rs h
332	  | R, _, M, _, _, [], _ => nil R M
333	  | _, _, M, _, _, r :: rs, h =>
334	    let ⟨h1, h2⟩ := (isWeaklyRegular_cons_iff' M r rs).mp h
335	    cons r rs h1 h2 (recIterModByRegularWithRing nil cons h2)
336	  termination_by _ _ _ _ _ rs => List.length rs
337	
338	/-- A simplified version of `IsWeaklyRegular.recIterModByRegularWithRing` where
339	the motive is not allowed to depend on the proof of `IsWeaklyRegular`. -/
340	def ndrecWithRing
341	    {motive : (R : Type u) → [CommRing R] → (M : Type v) →
342	      [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → (rs : List R) → Sort*}
343	    (nil : (R : Type u) → [CommRing R] → (M : Type v) →
344	      [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → motive R M [])
345	    (cons : {R : Type u} → [CommRing R] → {M : Type v} → [AddCommGroup M] →
346	      [Module R M] → (r : R) → (rs : List R) → IsSMulRegular M r →
347	      IsWeaklyRegular (QuotSMulTop r M)
348	        (rs.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {r}))) →
349	      motive (R ⧸ Ideal.span {r}) (QuotSMulTop r M)
350	        (rs.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {r}))) → motive R M (r :: rs))
351	    {R} [CommRing R] {M} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] {rs} :
352	    IsWeaklyRegular M rs → motive R M rs :=
353	  recIterModByRegularWithRing (motive := fun R _ M _ _ rs _ => motive R M rs)
354	    nil cons
355	
356	end IsWeaklyRegular
357

359	
360	variable (M)
361	
362	lemma isWeaklyRegular_singleton_iff (r : R) :
363	    IsWeaklyRegular M [r] ↔ IsSMulRegular M r :=
364	  Iff.trans (isWeaklyRegular_cons_iff M r []) (and_iff_left (.nil R _))
365	
366	lemma isWeaklyRegular_append_iff (rs₁ rs₂ : List R) :
367	    IsWeaklyRegular M (rs₁ ++ rs₂) ↔
368	      IsWeaklyRegular M rs₁ ∧
369	        IsWeaklyRegular (M ⧸ (Ideal.ofList rs₁ • ⊤ : Submodule R M)) rs₂ := by
370	  induction rs₁ generalizing M with
371	  | nil =>
372	    refine Iff.symm <| Iff.trans (and_iff_right (.nil R M)) ?_
373	    refine (quotEquivOfEqBot _ ?_).isWeaklyRegular_congr rs₂
374	    rw [Ideal.ofList_nil, bot_smul]
375	  | cons r rs₁ ih =>
376	    let e := quotOfListConsSMulTopEquivQuotSMulTopInner M r rs₁
377	    rw [List.cons_append, isWeaklyRegular_cons_iff, isWeaklyRegular_cons_iff,
378	      ih, ← and_assoc, ← e.isWeaklyRegular_congr rs₂]
379	
380	lemma isWeaklyRegular_append_iff' (rs₁ rs₂ : List R) :
381	    IsWeaklyRegular M (rs₁ ++ rs₂) ↔
382	      IsWeaklyRegular M rs₁ ∧
383	        IsWeaklyRegular (M ⧸ (Ideal.ofList rs₁ • ⊤ : Submodule R M))
384	          (rs₂.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.ofList rs₁))) :=
385	  Iff.trans (isWeaklyRegular_append_iff M rs₁ rs₂) <| and_congr_right' <|
386	    Iff.symm <| isWeaklyRegular_map_algebraMap_iff (R ⧸ Ideal.ofList rs₁) _ rs₂
387	
388	end

392	variable (R M) in
393	lemma nil [Nontrivial M] : IsRegular M ([] : List R) where
394	  toIsWeaklyRegular := IsWeaklyRegular.nil R M
395	  top_ne_smul h := by
396	    rw [Ideal.ofList_nil, bot_smul, eq_comm, subsingleton_iff_bot_eq_top] at h
397	    exact not_subsingleton M ((Submodule.subsingleton_iff _).mp h)
398	
399	lemma cons {r : R} {rs : List R} (h1 : IsSMulRegular M r)
400	    (h2 : IsRegular (QuotSMulTop r M) rs) : IsRegular M (r :: rs) :=
401	  (isRegular_cons_iff M r rs).mpr ⟨h1, h2⟩
402	
403	lemma cons' {r : R} {rs : List R} (h1 : IsSMulRegular M r)
404	    (h2 : IsRegular (QuotSMulTop r M) (rs.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {r})))) :
405	    IsRegular M (r :: rs) :=
406	  (isRegular_cons_iff' M r rs).mpr ⟨h1, h2⟩
407	
408	/-- Regular sequences can be inductively characterized by:
409	* The empty sequence is regular on any nonzero module.
410	* If `r` is regular on `M` and `rs` is a regular sequence on `M⧸rM` then the
411	  sequence obtained from `rs` by prepending `r` is regular on `M`.
412	
413	This is the induction principle produced by the inductive definition above.
414	The motive will usually be valued in `Prop`, but `Sort*` works too. -/
415	@[induction_eliminator]
416	def recIterModByRegular
417	    {motive : (M : Type v) → [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → (rs : List R) →
418	      IsRegular M rs → Sort*}
419	    (nil : (M : Type v) → [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → [Nontrivial M] →
420	      motive M [] (nil R M))
421	    (cons : {M : Type v} → [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → (r : R) →
422	      (rs : List R) → (h1 : IsSMulRegular M r) → (h2 : IsRegular (QuotSMulTop r M) rs) →
423	      (ih : motive (QuotSMulTop r M) rs h2) → motive M (r :: rs) (cons h1 h2))
424	    {M} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] {rs} (h : IsRegular M rs) : motive M rs h :=
425	  h.toIsWeaklyRegular.recIterModByRegular
426	    (motive := fun N _ _ rs' h' => ∀ h'', motive N rs' ⟨h', h''⟩)
427	    (fun N _ _ h' =>
428	      haveI := (nontrivial_iff R).mp (nontrivial_of_ne _ _ h'); nil N)
429	    (fun r rs' h1 h2 h3 h4 =>
430	      have ⟨h5, h6⟩ := (isRegular_cons_iff _ _ _).mp ⟨h2.cons h1, h4⟩
431	      cons r rs' h5 h6 (h3 h6.top_ne_smul))
432	    h.top_ne_smul
433	
434	/-- A simplified version of `IsRegular.recIterModByRegular` where the motive is
435	not allowed to depend on the proof of `IsRegular`. -/
436	def ndrecIterModByRegular
437	    {motive : (M : Type v) → [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → (rs : List R) → Sort*}
438	    (nil : (M : Type v) → [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → [Nontrivial M] → motive M [])
439	    (cons : {M : Type v} → [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → (r : R) →
440	      (rs : List R) → IsSMulRegular M r → IsRegular (QuotSMulTop r M) rs →
441	      motive (QuotSMulTop r M) rs → motive M (r :: rs))
442	    {M} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] {rs} : IsRegular M rs → motive M rs :=
443	  recIterModByRegular (motive := fun M _ _ rs _ => motive M rs) nil cons
444	
445	/-- An alternate induction principle from `IsRegular.recIterModByRegular` where
446	we mod out by successive elements in both the module and the base ring. This is
447	useful for propagating certain properties of the initial `M`, e.g. faithfulness
448	or freeness, throughout the induction. -/
449	def recIterModByRegularWithRing
450	    {motive : (R : Type u) → [CommRing R] → (M : Type v) → [AddCommGroup M] →
451	      [Module R M] → (rs : List R) → IsRegular M rs → Sort*}
452	    (nil : (R : Type u) → [CommRing R] → (M : Type v) → [AddCommGroup M] →
453	      [Module R M] → [Nontrivial M] → motive R M [] (nil R M))
454	    (cons : {R : Type u} → [CommRing R] → {M : Type v} → [AddCommGroup M] →
455	      [Module R M] → (r : R) → (rs : List R) → (h1 : IsSMulRegular M r) →
456	      (h2 : IsRegular (QuotSMulTop r M)
457	              (rs.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {r})))) →
458	      (ih : motive (R ⧸ Ideal.span {r}) (QuotSMulTop r M)
459	              (rs.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {r}))) h2) →
460	            motive R M (r :: rs) (cons' h1 h2))
461	    {R} [CommRing R] {M} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] {rs}
462	    (h : IsRegular M rs) : motive R M rs h :=
463	  h.toIsWeaklyRegular.recIterModByRegularWithRing
464	    (motive := fun R _ N _ _ rs' h' => ∀ h'', motive R N rs' ⟨h', h''⟩)
465	    (fun R _ N _ _ h' =>
466	      haveI := (nontrivial_iff R).mp (nontrivial_of_ne _ _ h'); nil R N)
467	    (fun r rs' h1 h2 h3 h4 =>
468	      have ⟨h5, h6⟩ := (isRegular_cons_iff' _ _ _).mp ⟨h2.cons' h1, h4⟩
469	      cons r rs' h5 h6 <| h3 h6.top_ne_smul)
470	    h.top_ne_smul
471	
472	/-- A simplified version of `IsRegular.recIterModByRegularWithRing` where the
473	motive is not allowed to depend on the proof of `IsRegular`. -/
474	def ndrecIterModByRegularWithRing
475	    {motive : (R : Type u) → [CommRing R] → (M : Type v) →
476	      [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → (rs : List R) → Sort*}
477	    (nil : (R : Type u) → [CommRing R] → (M : Type v) →
478	      [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → [Nontrivial M] → motive R M [])
479	    (cons : {R : Type u} → [CommRing R] → {M : Type v} →
480	      [AddCommGroup M] → [Module R M] → (r : R) → (rs : List R) →
481	      IsSMulRegular M r →
482	      IsRegular (QuotSMulTop r M)
483	        (rs.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {r}))) →
484	      motive (R ⧸ Ideal.span {r}) (QuotSMulTop r M)
485	        (rs.map (Ideal.Quotient.mk (Ideal.span {r}))) →
486	      motive R M (r :: rs))
487	    {R} [CommRing R] {M} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] {rs} :
488	    IsRegular M rs → motive R M rs :=
489	  recIterModByRegularWithRing (motive := fun R _ M _ _ rs _ => motive R M rs)
490	    nil cons
491	
492	lemma quot_ofList_smul_nontrivial {rs : List R} (h : IsRegular M rs)
493	    (N : Submodule R M) : Nontrivial (M ⧸ Ideal.ofList rs • N) :=
494	  Submodule.Quotient.nontrivial_iff.mpr <|
495	    ne_top_of_le_ne_top h.top_ne_smul.symm (smul_mono_right _ le_top)
496	
497	lemma nontrivial {rs : List R} (h : IsRegular M rs) : Nontrivial M :=
498	  haveI := quot_ofList_smul_nontrivial h ⊤
499	  (mkQ_surjective (Ideal.ofList rs • ⊤ : Submodule R M)).nontrivial
500	
501	end IsRegular
502	
503	lemma isRegular_iff_isWeaklyRegular_of_subset_jacobson_annihilator
504	    [Nontrivial M] [Module.Finite R M] {rs : List R}
505	    (h : ∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ Ideal.jacobson (Module.annihilator R M)) :
506	    IsRegular M rs ↔ IsWeaklyRegular M rs :=
507	  Iff.trans (isRegular_iff M rs) <| and_iff_left <|
508	    top_ne_ideal_smul_of_le_jacobson_annihilator <| Ideal.span_le.mpr h
509	
510	lemma _root_.IsLocalRing.isRegular_iff_isWeaklyRegular_of_subset_maximalIdeal
511	    [IsLocalRing R] [Nontrivial M] [Module.Finite R M] {rs : List R}
512	    (h : ∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) :
513	    IsRegular M rs ↔ IsWeaklyRegular M rs :=
514	  have H h' := bot_ne_top.symm <| annihilator_eq_top_iff.mp <|
515	    Eq.trans annihilator_top h'
516	  isRegular_iff_isWeaklyRegular_of_subset_jacobson_annihilator fun r hr =>
517	    IsLocalRing.jacobson_eq_maximalIdeal (Module.annihilator R M) H ▸ h r hr
518	
519	open IsWeaklyRegular IsArtinian in
520	lemma eq_nil_of_isRegular_on_artinian [IsArtinian R M] :
521	    {rs : List R} → IsRegular M rs → rs = []
522	  | [], _ => rfl
523	  | r :: rs, h => by
524	    rw [isRegular_iff, ne_comm, ← lt_top_iff_ne_top, Ideal.ofList_cons,
525	      sup_smul, ideal_span_singleton_smul, isWeaklyRegular_cons_iff] at h
526	    refine absurd ?_ (ne_of_lt (lt_of_le_of_lt le_sup_left h.right))
527	    exact Eq.trans (Submodule.map_top _) <| LinearMap.range_eq_top.mpr <|
528	      surjective_of_injective_endomorphism (LinearMap.lsmul R M r) h.left.left
529	
530	lemma IsWeaklyRegular.isWeaklyRegular_lTensor [Module.Flat R M₂]
531	    {rs : List R} (h : IsWeaklyRegular M rs) :
532	    IsWeaklyRegular (M₂ ⊗[R] M) rs := by
533	  induction h with
534	  | nil N => exact nil R (M₂ ⊗[R] N)
535	  | @cons N _ _ r rs' h1 _ ih =>
536	    let e := tensorQuotSMulTopEquivQuotSMulTop r M₂ N
537	    exact ((e.isWeaklyRegular_congr rs').mp ih).cons (h1.lTensor M₂)
538	
539	lemma IsWeaklyRegular.isWeaklyRegular_rTensor [Module.Flat R M₂]
540	    {rs : List R} (h : IsWeaklyRegular M rs) :
541	    IsWeaklyRegular (M ⊗[R] M₂) rs := by
542	  induction h with
543	  | nil N => exact nil R (N ⊗[R] M₂)
544	  | @cons N _ _ r rs' h1 _ ih =>
545	    let e := quotSMulTopTensorEquivQuotSMulTop r M₂ N
546	    exact ((e.isWeaklyRegular_congr rs').mp ih).cons (h1.rTensor M₂)
547	-- TODO: apply the above to localization and completion (Corollary 1.1.3 in B&H)
548	
549	lemma map_first_exact_on_four_term_right_exact_of_isSMulRegular_last
550	    {rs : List R} {f₁ : M →ₗ[R] M₂} {f₂ : M₂ →ₗ[R] M₃} {f₃ : M₃ →ₗ[R] M₄}
551	    (h₁₂ : Exact f₁ f₂) (h₂₃ : Exact f₂ f₃) (h₃ : Surjective f₃)
552	    (h₄ : IsWeaklyRegular M₄ rs) :
553	    Exact (mapQ _ _ _ (smul_top_le_comap_smul_top (Ideal.ofList rs) f₁))
554	          (mapQ _ _ _ (smul_top_le_comap_smul_top (Ideal.ofList rs) f₂)) := by
555	  induction h₄ generalizing M M₂ M₃ with
556	  | nil =>
557	    apply (Exact.iff_of_ladder_linearEquiv ?_ ?_).mp h₁₂
558	    any_goals exact quotEquivOfEqBot _ <|
559	      Eq.trans (congrArg (· • ⊤) Ideal.ofList_nil) (bot_smul ⊤)
560	    all_goals exact quot_hom_ext _ _ _ fun _ => rfl
561	  | cons r rs h₄ _ ih =>
562	    specialize ih
563	      (map_first_exact_on_four_term_exact_of_isSMulRegular_last h₁₂ h₂₃ h₄)
564	      (map_exact r h₂₃ h₃) (map_surjective r h₃)
565	    have H₁ := quotOfListConsSMulTopEquivQuotSMulTopInner_naturality r rs f₁
566	    have H₂ := quotOfListConsSMulTopEquivQuotSMulTopInner_naturality r rs f₂
567	    exact (Exact.iff_of_ladder_linearEquiv H₁.symm H₂.symm).mp ih

571	section Perm

573	open _root_.LinearMap in
574	private lemma IsWeaklyRegular.swap {a b : R} (h1 : IsWeaklyRegular M [a, b])
575	    (h2 : torsionBy R M b = a • torsionBy R M b → torsionBy R M b = ⊥) :
576	    IsWeaklyRegular M [b, a] := by
577	  rw [isWeaklyRegular_cons_iff, isWeaklyRegular_singleton_iff] at h1 ⊢
578	  obtain ⟨ha, hb⟩ := h1
579	  rw [← isSMulRegular_iff_torsionBy_eq_bot] at h2
580	  specialize h2 (le_antisymm ?_ (smul_le_self_of_tower a (torsionBy R M b)))
581	  · refine le_of_eq_of_le ?_ <| smul_top_inf_eq_smul_of_isSMulRegular_on_quot <|
582	      ha.of_injective _ <| ker_eq_bot.mp <| ker_liftQ_eq_bot' _ (lsmul R M b) rfl
583	    rw [← (isSMulRegular_on_quot_iff_lsmul_comap_eq _ _).mp hb]
584	    exact (inf_eq_right.mpr (ker_le_comap _)).symm
585	  · rwa [ha.isSMulRegular_on_quot_iff_smul_top_inf_eq_smul, inf_comm, smul_comm,
586	      ← h2.isSMulRegular_on_quot_iff_smul_top_inf_eq_smul, and_iff_left hb]
587	
588	-- TODO: Equivalence of permutability of regular sequences to regularity of
589	-- subsequences and regularity on poly ring. See [07DW] in stacks project
590	-- We need a theory of multivariate polynomial modules first
591	
592	lemma IsWeaklyRegular.prototype_perm {rs : List R} (h : IsWeaklyRegular M rs)
593	    {rs'} (h'' : rs ~ rs') (h' : ∀ a b rs', (a :: b :: rs') <+~ rs →
594	      let K := torsionBy R (M ⧸ (Ideal.ofList rs' • ⊤ : Submodule R M)) b
595	      K = a • K → K = ⊥) : IsWeaklyRegular M rs' :=
596	  have H := LinearEquiv.isWeaklyRegular_congr <| quotEquivOfEqBot _ <|
597	    Eq.trans (congrArg (· • ⊤) Ideal.ofList_nil) (bot_smul ⊤)
598	  (H rs').mp <| (aux [] h'' (.refl rs) (h''.symm.subperm)) <| (H rs).mpr h
599	  where aux {rs₁ rs₂} (rs₀ : List R)
600	    (h₁₂ : rs₁ ~ rs₂) (H₁ : rs₀ ++ rs₁ <+~ rs) (H₃ : rs₀ ++ rs₂ <+~ rs)
601	    (h : IsWeaklyRegular (M ⧸ (Ideal.ofList rs₀ • ⊤ : Submodule R M)) rs₁) :
602	    IsWeaklyRegular (M ⧸ (Ideal.ofList rs₀ • ⊤ : Submodule R M)) rs₂ := by {
603	  induction h₁₂ generalizing rs₀ with
604	  | nil => exact .nil R _
605	  | cons r _ ih =>
606	    let e := quotOfListConsSMulTopEquivQuotSMulTopOuter M r rs₀
607	    rw [isWeaklyRegular_cons_iff, ← e.isWeaklyRegular_congr] at h ⊢
608	    refine h.imp_right (ih (r :: rs₀) ?_ ?_) <;>
609	      exact List.perm_middle.subperm_right.mp ‹_›
610	  | swap a b t =>
611	    rw [show ∀ x y z, x :: y :: z = [x, y] ++ z from fun _ _ _ => rfl,
612	      isWeaklyRegular_append_iff] at h ⊢
613	    have : Ideal.ofList [b, a] = Ideal.ofList [a, b] :=
614	      congrArg Ideal.span <| Set.ext fun _ => (List.Perm.swap a b []).mem_iff
615	    rw [(quotEquivOfEq _ _ (congrArg₂ _ this rfl)).isWeaklyRegular_congr] at h
616	    rw [List.append_cons, List.append_cons, List.append_assoc _ [b] [a]] at H₁
617	    apply (List.sublist_append_left (rs₀ ++ [b, a]) _).subperm.trans at H₁
618	    apply List.perm_append_comm.subperm.trans at H₁
619	    exact h.imp_left (swap · (h' b a rs₀ H₁))
620	  | trans h₁₂ _ ih₁₂ ih₂₃ =>
621	    have H₂ := (h₁₂.append_left rs₀).subperm_right.mp H₁
622	    exact ih₂₃ rs₀ H₂ H₃ (ih₁₂ rs₀ H₁ H₂ h) }
623	
624	-- putting `{rs' : List R}` and `h2` after `h3` would be better for partial
625	-- application, but this argument order seems nicer overall
626	lemma IsWeaklyRegular.of_perm_of_subset_jacobson_annihilator [IsNoetherian R M]
627	    {rs rs' : List R} (h1 : IsWeaklyRegular M rs) (h2 : List.Perm rs rs')
628	    (h3 : ∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ (Module.annihilator R M).jacobson) :
629	    IsWeaklyRegular M rs' :=
630	  h1.prototype_perm h2 fun r _ _ h h' =>
631	    eq_bot_of_eq_pointwise_smul_of_mem_jacobson_annihilator
632	      (IsNoetherian.noetherian _) h'
633	      (Ideal.jacobson_mono
634	        (le_trans
635	          -- The named argument `(R := R)` below isn't necessary, but
636	          -- typechecking is much slower without it
637	          (LinearMap.annihilator_le_of_surjective (R := R) _ (mkQ_surjective _))
638	          (LinearMap.annihilator_le_of_injective _ (injective_subtype _)))
639	        (h3 r (h.subset List.mem_cons_self)))
640	
641	end Perm
642	
643	lemma IsRegular.of_perm_of_subset_jacobson_annihilator [IsNoetherian R M]
644	    {rs rs' : List R} (h1 : IsRegular M rs) (h2 : List.Perm rs rs')
645	    (h3 : ∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ (Module.annihilator R M).jacobson) : IsRegular M rs' :=
646	  ⟨h1.toIsWeaklyRegular.of_perm_of_subset_jacobson_annihilator h2 h3,
647	    letI := h1.nontrivial
648	    top_ne_ideal_smul_of_le_jacobson_annihilator <|
649	      Ideal.span_le.mpr (h3 · <| h2.mem_iff.mpr ·)⟩
650	
651	lemma _root_.IsLocalRing.isWeaklyRegular_of_perm_of_subset_maximalIdeal
652	    [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherian R M] {rs rs' : List R}
653	    (h1 : IsWeaklyRegular M rs) (h2 : List.Perm rs rs')
654	    (h3 : ∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) : IsWeaklyRegular M rs' :=
655	  IsWeaklyRegular.of_perm_of_subset_jacobson_annihilator h1 h2 fun r hr =>
656	    IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal_le_jacobson _ (h3 r hr)
657	
658	lemma _root_.IsLocalRing.isRegular_of_perm [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherian R M]
659	    {rs rs' : List R} (h1 : IsRegular M rs) (h2 : List.Perm rs rs') :
660	    IsRegular M rs' := by
661	  obtain ⟨h3, h4⟩ := h1
662	  refine ⟨IsLocalRing.isWeaklyRegular_of_perm_of_subset_maximalIdeal h3 h2 ?_, ?_⟩
663	  · intro x (h6 : x ∈ { r | r ∈ rs })
664	    refine IsLocalRing.le_maximalIdeal ?_ (Ideal.subset_span h6)
665	    exact h4 ∘ Eq.trans (top_smul _).symm ∘ Eq.symm ∘ congrArg (· • ⊤)
666	  · refine ne_of_ne_of_eq h4 (congrArg (Ideal.span · • ⊤) ?_)
667	    exact Set.ext fun _ => h2.mem_iff
668	
669	end RingTheory.Sequence

13	/-!
14	
15	# Krull Dimension of quotient regular sequence
16	
17	## Main results
18	
19	- `Module.supportDim_add_length_eq_supportDim_of_isRegular`: If `M` is a finite module over a
20	  Noetherian local ring `R`, `r₁, …, rₙ` is an `M`-sequence, then
21	  `dim M/(r₁, …, rₙ)M + n = dim M`.
22	-/
23	
24	public section
25	
26	namespace Module

28	variable {R : Type*} [CommRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
29	  {M : Type*} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [Module.Finite R M]

33	omit [IsNoetherianRing R] [Module.Finite R M] in
34	lemma exists_ltSeries_support_isMaximal_last_of_ltSeries_support (q : LTSeries (support R M)) :
35	    ∃ p : LTSeries (support R M), q.length ≤ p.length ∧ p.last.1.1.IsMaximal := by
36	  obtain ⟨m, hmm, hm⟩ := exists_le_maximal _ q.last.1.2.1
37	  obtain hlt | rfl := lt_or_eq_of_le hm
38	  · use q.snoc ⟨⟨m, inferInstance⟩, mem_support_mono hm q.last.2⟩ hlt
39	    simpa
40	  · use q
41	
42	theorem supportDim_le_supportDim_quotSMulTop_succ_of_mem_jacobson {x : R}
43	    (h : x ∈ (annihilator R M).jacobson) : supportDim R M ≤ supportDim R (QuotSMulTop x M) + 1 := by
44	  nontriviality M
45	  refine iSup_le_iff.mpr (fun p ↦ ?_)
46	  wlog hxp : x ∈ p.last.1.1 generalizing p
47	  · obtain ⟨p, hle, hm⟩ := exists_ltSeries_support_isMaximal_last_of_ltSeries_support p
48	    have hj : (annihilator R M).jacobson ≤ p.last.1.1 :=
49	      sInf_le ⟨mem_support_iff_of_finite.mp p.last.2, inferInstance⟩
50	    exact (Nat.cast_le.mpr hle).trans <| this _ (hj h)
51	  -- `q` is a chain of primes such that `x ∈ q 1`, `p.length = q.length` and `p.head = q.head`.
52	  obtain ⟨q, hxq, hq, h0, _⟩ : ∃ q : LTSeries (PrimeSpectrum R), _ ∧ _ ∧ p.head = q.head ∧ _ :=
53	    exist_ltSeries_mem_one_of_mem_last (p.map Subtype.val (fun ⦃_ _⦄ lt ↦ lt)) hxp
54	  by_cases hp0 : p.length = 0
55	  · have hb : supportDim R (QuotSMulTop x M) ≠ ⊥ :=
56	      (supportDim_ne_bot_iff_nontrivial R (QuotSMulTop x M)).mpr <|
57	        Submodule.Quotient.nontrivial_iff.mpr <|
58	          (Submodule.top_ne_pointwise_smul_of_mem_jacobson_annihilator h).symm
59	    rw [hp0, ← WithBot.coe_unbot (supportDim R (QuotSMulTop x M)) hb]
60	    exact WithBot.coe_le_coe.mpr (zero_le ((supportDim R (QuotSMulTop x M)).unbot hb + 1))
61	  -- Let `q' i := q (i + 1)`, then `q'` is a chain of prime ideals in `Supp(M/xM)`.
62	  let q' : LTSeries (support R (QuotSMulTop x M)) := {
63	    length := p.length - 1
64	    toFun := by
65	      intro ⟨i, hi⟩
66	      have hi : i + 1 < q.length + 1 :=
67	        Nat.succ_lt_succ (hi.trans_eq ((Nat.sub_add_cancel (Nat.pos_of_ne_zero hp0)).trans hq))
68	      exact ⟨q ⟨i + 1, hi⟩, by simpa using
69	        ⟨mem_support_mono (by simpa [h0] using q.monotone (Fin.zero_le _)) p.head.2, q.monotone
70	          ((Fin.natCast_eq_mk (Nat.lt_of_add_left_lt hi)).trans_le (Nat.le_add_left 1 i)) hxq⟩⟩
71	    step := by exact fun _ ↦ q.strictMono (by simp)
72	  }
73	  grw [le_tsub_add (b := p.length) (a := 1), Nat.cast_add_one, supportDim, Order.krullDim,
74	    ← le_iSup _ q']

77	/-- If `M` is a finite module over a commutative ring `R`, `x ∈ M` is not in any minimal prime of
78	  `M`, then `dim M/xM + 1 ≤ dim M`. -/
79	theorem supportDim_quotSMulTop_succ_le_of_notMem_minimalPrimes {x : R}
80	    (hn : ∀ p ∈ (annihilator R M).minimalPrimes, x ∉ p) :
81	    supportDim R (QuotSMulTop x M) + 1 ≤ supportDim R M := by
82	  nontriviality M
83	  nontriviality (QuotSMulTop x M)
84	  have : Nonempty (Module.support R M) := nonempty_support_of_nontrivial.to_subtype
85	  have : Nonempty (Module.support R (QuotSMulTop x M)) := nonempty_support_of_nontrivial.to_subtype
86	  simp only [supportDim, Order.krullDim_eq_iSup_length]
87	  apply WithBot.coe_le_coe.mpr
88	  simp only [ENat.iSup_add, iSup_le_iff]
89	  intro p
90	  have hp := p.head.2
91	  simp only [support_quotSMulTop, Set.mem_inter_iff, mem_zeroLocus, Set.singleton_subset_iff] at hp
92	  have le : support R (QuotSMulTop x M) ⊆ support R M := by simp
93	  -- Since `Supp(M/xM) ⊆ Supp M`, `p` can be viewed as a chain of prime ideals in `Supp M`,
94	  -- which we denote by `q`.
95	  let q : LTSeries (support R M) :=
96	    p.map (Set.MapsTo.restrict id (support R (QuotSMulTop x M)) (support R M) le) (fun _ _ h ↦ h)
97	  obtain ⟨r, hrm, hr⟩ := exists_minimalPrimes_le (mem_support_iff_of_finite.mp q.head.2)
98	  let r : support R M := ⟨⟨r, minimalPrimes_isPrime hrm⟩, mem_support_iff_of_finite.mpr hrm.1.2⟩
99	  have hr : r < q.head := lt_of_le_of_ne hr (fun h ↦ hn q.head.1.1 (by rwa [← h]) hp.2)
100	  exact le_of_eq_of_le (by simp [q]) (le_iSup _ (q.cons r hr))
101	
102	theorem supportDim_quotSMulTop_succ_eq_of_notMem_minimalPrimes_of_mem_jacobson {x : R}
103	    (hn : ∀ p ∈ (annihilator R M).minimalPrimes, x ∉ p) (hx : x ∈ (annihilator R M).jacobson) :
104	    supportDim R (QuotSMulTop x M) + 1 = supportDim R M :=
105	  le_antisymm (supportDim_quotSMulTop_succ_le_of_notMem_minimalPrimes hn)
106	    (supportDim_le_supportDim_quotSMulTop_succ_of_mem_jacobson hx)
