1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. **Requires `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` in env** — check via `env | grep -E "OPENAI|GEMINI|OPENROUTER"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | — | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/` (`prover-autoformalize.md` / `prover-prover.md` / `prover-polish.md`).
127	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
128	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
129	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
130	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
131	
132	### Subagents
133	
134	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
135	
136	### Review Agent
137	
138	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
139	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
140	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
141	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
142	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
143	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
144	
145	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
146	
147	These run automatically between phases:
148	
149	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
150	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
151	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
152

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs in your assigned `.lean` file.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas on your file.
9	3. Check the `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments (file-specific user hints).
10	4. **Read the relevant blueprint chapter before writing Lean code.** The chapter holds the mathematical proof sketch you must align with. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs. Push as far as possible.
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you can't fully close a sorry, leave your best attempt — commented-out steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` if needed), but your work must be visible for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to a bare `sorry` — that wastes the work.
13	7. Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
14	
15	**Write permissions**: only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	`archon-protected.yaml` lists declarations with **frozen signatures**. You may fill their proof bodies but must not rename, re-type, reorder arguments, or weaken hypotheses. Only the mathematician edits protected signatures.
20	
21	## Avoid early termination
22	
23	- Don't abandon a proof prematurely. Many complex proofs run to thousands of lines.
24	- Difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave a `sorry`.
25	- Don't delegate to "the next iteration" or "another prover" if more effort could close it.
26	- Only modify the proof for your assigned task — leave unrelated proofs untouched.
27	- **Decompose**: break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure when available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each individually until the entire goal closes.
28	
29	## Task completion criteria
30	
31	Your task is complete ONLY when ALL of:
32	
33	1. Every `sorry` in scope is replaced with a complete proof.
34	2. Zero axioms introduced.
35	3. The file compiles cleanly.
36	
37	## Never weaken the type to dodge the proof
38	
39	When the substantive type is unattainable this iter, you have one honest move: leave `sorry` with the **intended type signature** so the type itself encodes the claim. You must NOT substitute a tautologically-true type to trivialize the body. Three patterns are banned:
40	
41	- **Reflexive-iso placeholder.** Replacing a non-trivial `X ≅ Y` (or `Nonempty (X ≅ Y)`) target with `Nonempty (X ≅ X) := ⟨Iso.refl _⟩`. The type now asserts nothing; the docstring's "iter-N placeholder" disclosure does not undo the structural lie, and the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference will resolve to this hollow declaration.
42	- **`Classical.choice` body around an explicit witness.** Writing `Classical.choice (α := X) ⟨witness⟩` to dissolve `Type` vs `Prop` friction — the witness is unreachable through any unfold, so the def is indistinguishable from `Classical.choice ⟨any-other-X⟩` downstream. Either use `Classical.choose` on the existential chain (preserves the spec), or `Nonempty.some`-with-witness; or ship a typed `sorry`.
43	- **Empty-content `proof_wanted`.** `proof_wanted` discards the declaration post-elaboration — the named decl never enters the environment, so the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference breaks. Use `theorem name : T := by sorry` with the intended signature instead.
44	
45	**Litmus test**: if you `unfold` your declaration, does it expose the named substantive content (Kähler differential module, the explicit iso, …) or does it stop at `Classical.choice` / `Iso.refl _` / nothing? If the latter, the body is structurally vacuous — ship the typed `sorry` instead.
46	
47	## When infrastructure is missing
48	
49	Do NOT report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up:
50	
51	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`): "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
52	2. **Try the alternative** — formalize whatever the informal agent suggests.
53	3. **If you still can't**: write the alternative sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md` and record in your task result what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found. "I couldn't prove X, but here's approach Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than "infrastructure missing".
54	
55	When stuck more generally: break into smaller subgoals, search Mathlib more thoroughly, prove missing helpers yourself, try alternative strategies, re-read the blueprint, use Web Search for published proofs.
56	
57	**Impossibility vs difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
58	
59	## Proof style
60	
61	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its body.
62	- Keep edits minimal; don't delete comments or change labels; don't add unrelated declarations.
63	- Helper lemmas you introduced may be modified if they turn out wrong.
64	- Add a concise comment above each helper lemma so reuse is easy.
65	- **`change` vs `show`** — `change` reshapes the goal up to defeq; `show` is purely display-level annotation. Using `show` where `change` is needed produces a linter warning. Default to `change` when in doubt.
66	
67	## Mathlib tags in PROGRESS.md
68	
69	The plan agent tags suggested lemmas:
70	
71	- `[verified]` — confirmed to exist.
72	- `[expected]` — guessed by naming convention. Quick `lean_local_search`; pivot if it doesn't exist.
73	- `[gap]` — verified NOT in Mathlib. Don't waste search time; formalize a workaround.
74	
75	## LSP MCP tools
76	
77	The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls** (full names like `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal` / `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`). The lean4 skill reference uses short names (`lean_goal`, `lean_local_search`, …) — same tools, NOT shell binaries.
78	
79	- Always invoke through your tool-call interface, the same way as `Read` or `Grep`.
80	- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` etc. — there is no such shell command.
81	- First LSP action: `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your file. If it returns `success: false` the server is cold; retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.
82	
83	## Search protocol
84	
85	Follow `references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`. Key priority:
86	
87	1. `lean_local_search` first.
88	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — describe the mathematical content, not just the name.
89	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only.
90	4. Never use shell `find` / `grep` to locate Mathlib theorems.
91	
92	## Tooling traps
93	
94	- **Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics**: in `lean_multi_attempt`, `diagnostics: []` can be misleading (line-scoping quirks attach errors to the enclosing `by` block). Check that `goals_after` is empty or has advanced.
95	- **Beware `lean_run_code` with imports**: standalone snippets can silently swallow elaboration errors when they contain imports. Rely on the actual file's `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.
96	
97	## Logging
98	
99	Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror your `.lean` path: `Algebra/WLocal.lean` → `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`):
100	
101	```markdown
102	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
103	
104	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
105	### Attempt 1
106	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
107	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance, not available
108	- **Dead end:** direct case split without IsLocalRing
109	
110	### Attempt 2
111	- **Approach:** Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
112	- **Result:** RESOLVED
113	- **Key insight:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective` bridges the gap
114	
115	## helper_bijective (line 78)
116	### Attempt 1
117	- **Approach:** Split into injectivity + surjectivity; prove injectivity
118	- **Result:** PARTIAL (branch closed) — injectivity closed; surjectivity remains
119	- **Next step:** `PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective` for the right branch
120	- **Lemmas found:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective`
121	```
122	
123	**Rules**: one section per theorem/lemma in your file. Each attempt records approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps. Log negative search results ("Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib"). The plan agent merges these into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md` next iter.
124	
125	**Read-only context**: read `task_pending.md` (prior attempts on your file) and `task_done.md` (when the problem resembles a completed one). Do not write to either.
126	
127	## End-of-session handoff
128	
129	Before stopping:
130	
131	1. Write `task_results/<your_file>.md` with: current result, lemmas discovered, concrete next step, dead-end warnings.
132	2. Save all changes; ensure the file compiles (use scoped `sorry` for stuck parts if needed).
133

6	import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects.BareScheme
7	import AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects.ChartIso
8	
9	/-!
10	# Genus-`0` base objects (Stratum 3): standard `k̄`-points on `ℙ¹`; `𝔾_a` and `𝔾_m`
11	
12	This file is **Stratum 3** of the four-stratum split of the legacy
13	`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects` (iter-175 refactor `g0bo-split`). It ships:
14	
15	* the three standard `k̄`-points `0 = [0 : 1]`, `1 = [1 : 1]`, `∞ = [1 : 0]` of
16	  `ProjectiveLineBar kbar`, encoded as sections of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom`;
17	* the additive group `Ga` over `Spec k̄` (= `AffineSpace (Fin 1)`) and its
18	  affine, finite-presentation, and reduced instances;
19	* the multiplicative group `Gm` over `Spec k̄` (= `Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹]`) and its
20	  affine, finite-presentation, reduced, domain, and irreducible-space instances,
21	  plus the scaffold `GrpObj Gm` and the resulting `Smooth Gm.hom`;
22	* the multiplicative identity `Gm.onePt = η[Gm kbar]`.
23	
24	Upstream strata: `BareScheme`, `ChartIso`. Downstream stratum: `GmScaling`.
25	-/
26	
27	set_option autoImplicit false
28	set_option linter.style.setOption false
29	
30	universe u
31	
32	open CategoryTheory Limits MonoidalCategory CartesianMonoidalCategory MonObj
33	
34	noncomputable section
35	
36	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
37	
38	/-! ### The standard `k̄`-points `0`, `1`, `∞` on `ℙ¹`
39	
40	The three distinguished `k̄`-points of `ℙ¹_{k̄}` are `[0 : 1]` (the affine origin), `[1 : 1]`
41	(the affine unit), and `[1 : 0]` (the point at infinity). They are encoded as morphisms
42	`𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar`, i.e. sections of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom`.
43	
44	The construction goes through `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections`: a `k̄`-point of `Proj 𝒜` is
45	specified by an evaluation ring map `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* k̄` (composed into
46	`Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)` via `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`) whose image of the irrelevant ideal `(X₀, X₁)`
47	generates the unit ideal — automatic whenever the evaluation vector has a unit coordinate. -/
48	
49	/-- **The underlying ring map of a `k̄`-point of `ℙ¹`.** Sends `X₀ ↦ v 0`, `X₁ ↦ v 1`,
50	then composes back into `Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)` via the inverse of `Scheme.ΓSpecIso`. -/
51	private noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal
52	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) :
53	    MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar →+* Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤) :=
54	  (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (.of kbar)).inv.hom.comp (MvPolynomial.eval v)
55	
56	/-- **Irrelevant-ideal-maps-to-top condition** for `ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal`: if one
57	of the coordinates `v 0` or `v 1` is a unit, then the image of `(X₀, X₁)` generates the
58	unit ideal in `Γ(Spec k̄, ⊤)`. -/
59	private lemma ProjectiveLineBar.irrelevant_map_eq_top
60	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) (i : Fin 2) (hi : IsUnit (v i)) :
61	    Ideal.map (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
62	        (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal = ⊤ := by
63	  have hX_mem : (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ∈
64	      (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal :=
65	    HomogeneousIdeal.mem_irrelevant_of_mem _ Nat.zero_lt_one
66	      (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i)
67	  have hImg_mem : ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v (MvPolynomial.X i) ∈
68	      Ideal.map (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
69	        (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)).toIdeal :=
70	    Ideal.mem_map_of_mem _ hX_mem
71	  have hImg_unit : IsUnit (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v (MvPolynomial.X i)) := by
72	    unfold ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal
73	    rw [RingHom.comp_apply, MvPolynomial.eval_X]
74	    exact hi.map _
75	  rw [Ideal.eq_top_iff_one]
76	  obtain ⟨u, hu⟩ := hImg_unit
77	  rw [show (1 : Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤)) = ((u⁻¹ : Γ(Spec (.of kbar), ⊤)ˣ) : _) * u from
78	    (Units.inv_mul _).symm]
79	  exact Ideal.mul_mem_left _ _ (hu.symm ▸ hImg_mem)
80	
81	/-- **Helper: construct a `k̄`-point of `ProjectiveLineBar kbar`** from an evaluation vector
82	`v : Fin 2 → kbar` with at least one unit coordinate. The underlying scheme map is
83	`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` of the evaluation; the section condition chases through
84	`fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero` + `IsScalarTower kbar (𝒜 0) MvPoly` collapse to
85	`MvPolynomial.C` + `MvPolynomial.eval_C` + `toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv`. -/
86	private noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec
87	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) (i : Fin 2) (hi : IsUnit (v i)) :
88	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
89	  Over.homMk
90	    (Proj.fromOfGlobalSections (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
91	      (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
92	      (ProjectiveLineBar.irrelevant_map_eq_top kbar v i hi)) <| by
93	    -- Section condition: fromOfGlobalSections ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.hom = 𝟙.
94	    haveI : IsScalarTower kbar ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0)
95	        (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
96	      IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun _ => rfl
97	    -- Expose the structure morphism's composition shape.
98	    change Proj.fromOfGlobalSections _ _ _ ≫ Proj.toSpecZero _ ≫ Spec.map _ = _
99	    -- Combine via `fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero`, then unify the two Spec.maps.
100	    rw [← Category.assoc, Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero, Category.assoc,
101	      ← Spec.map_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp, RingHom.comp_assoc,
102	      ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_eq kbar, MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq]
103	    -- The inner composition `(evalIntoGlobal v) ∘ C` collapses to `(ΓSpecIso).inv.hom`.
104	    -- Build a CommRingCat-level equation, then apply `Spec.map` and finish.
105	    have hcc : CommRingCat.ofHom
106	        ((ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v).comp MvPolynomial.C) =
107	        (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv := by
108	      apply CommRingCat.hom_ext
109	      ext r
110	      change (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv.hom
111	          ((MvPolynomial.eval v) (MvPolynomial.C r)) = _
112	      rw [MvPolynomial.eval_C]
113	    calc _ = (Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar)).toSpecΓ ≫
114	            Spec.map (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv := by
115	            exact congrArg _ (congrArg Spec.map hcc)
116	      _ = _ := AlgebraicGeometry.toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv _
117	
118	/-- The `k̄`-point `0 = [0 : 1] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom` via
119	`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` of the evaluation `X₀ ↦ 0`, `X₁ ↦ 1`. -/
120	noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
121	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
122	  ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec kbar (fun i => if i = 0 then 0 else 1) 1 (by simp)
123	
124	/-- The `k̄`-point `1 = [1 : 1] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section via the evaluation
125	`X₀ ↦ 1`, `X₁ ↦ 1`. -/
126	noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.onePt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
127	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
128	  ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec kbar (fun _ => 1) 0 (by simp)
129	
130	/-- The `k̄`-point `∞ = [1 : 0] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section via the evaluation
131	`X₀ ↦ 1`, `X₁ ↦ 0`. -/
132	noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.inftyPt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
133	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
134	  ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec kbar (fun i => if i = 0 then 1 else 0) 0 (by simp)
135	
136	/-! ### (B) The additive group `𝔾_a` over `Spec k̄` -/
137	
138	/-- **The additive group `𝔾_a = 𝔸¹` over `Spec k̄` as an underlying scheme.** This is the
139	affine line `AffineSpace (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))`. It is affine, locally of finite
140	presentation, and reduced (its global sections are `MvPolynomial (Fin 1) k̄`, a domain). -/
141	def GaScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
142	  AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))
143	
144	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance on `GaScheme` (via
145	`AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace.over`). -/
146	instance gaScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
147	    (GaScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
148	  inferInstanceAs ((AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar))).Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
149	
150	/-- **The additive group object `𝔾_a` over `Spec k̄` as an object of
151	`Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** -/
152	abbrev Ga (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
153	  (GaScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
154	
155	/-- **`𝔾_a` is an affine morphism over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
156	`AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace.instIsAffineHomOverSchemeInferInstanceOverClass`. -/
157	instance ga_isAffineHom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
158	    IsAffineHom (Ga kbar).hom :=
159	  inferInstanceAs (IsAffineHom (AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar)) ↘
160	    (Spec (.of kbar))))
161	
162	/-- **`𝔾_a` is locally of finite presentation over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
163	`AffineSpace.instLocallyOfFinitePresentation…OfFinite` (the index `Fin 1` is finite). -/
164	instance ga_locallyOfFinitePresentation (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
165	    LocallyOfFinitePresentation (Ga kbar).hom :=
166	  inferInstanceAs (LocallyOfFinitePresentation
167	    (AffineSpace.{0, u} (Fin 1) (Spec (.of kbar)) ↘ Spec (.of kbar)))
168	
169	/-- **`𝔾_a`'s underlying scheme is reduced.** Since the global sections are
170	`MvPolynomial (Fin 1) k̄`, a domain over a field, the affine scheme is reduced. The proof
171	transfers `IsReduced (Spec (.of (MvPolynomial _ _)))` (free for any reduced ring) across
172	`AffineSpace.isoOfIsAffine`. -/
173	instance ga_isReduced (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsReduced (Ga kbar).left :=
174	  isReduced_of_isOpenImmersion (AffineSpace.isoOfIsAffine (Fin 1) _).hom
175	
176	/-! ### (C) The multiplicative group `𝔾_m` over `Spec k̄` -/
177	
178	/-- **The ring `k̄[t, t⁻¹] = Localization.Away t`**, where `t = X () : MvPolynomial Unit k̄`.
179	This is the global-sections ring of `𝔾_m`. -/
180	abbrev GmRing (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Type u :=
181	  Localization.Away (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)
182	
183	/-- **The multiplicative group `𝔾_m = Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹]` as an underlying scheme.** This is
184	the chosen affine encoding (the analogist `gm-scaling-p1` D2.b verdict: AFFINE `Spec`, NOT
185	the basic-open of `𝔸¹` — the latter loses `IsAffine`). -/
186	def GmScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
187	  Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))
188	
189	/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance on `GmScheme`. -/
190	instance gmScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
191	    (GmScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) where
192	  hom := Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar)))
193	
194	/-- **The multiplicative group object `𝔾_m` over `Spec k̄` as an object of
195	`Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** -/
196	abbrev Gm (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
197	  (GmScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))
198	
199	/-- **`𝔾_m` is affine.** -/
200	instance gm_isAffine (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsAffine (Gm kbar).left :=
201	  inferInstanceAs (IsAffine (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))))
202	
203	/-- **`𝔾_m` is locally of finite presentation over `Spec k̄`.** Follows from
204	`Algebra.FinitePresentation k̄ (k̄[t, t⁻¹])` (a localization of a polynomial ring at a single
205	element is finitely presented), bridged through
206	`AlgebraicGeometry.HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff` for the `LocallyOfFinitePresentation` /
207	`RingHom.FinitePresentation` correspondence. -/
208	instance gm_locallyOfFinitePresentation (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
209	    LocallyOfFinitePresentation (Gm kbar).hom :=
210	  (HasRingHomProperty.Spec_iff (P := @LocallyOfFinitePresentation)).mpr
211	    ((RingHom.finitePresentation_algebraMap (B := GmRing kbar)).mpr
212	      inferInstance)
213	
214	/-- **`𝔾_m`'s underlying scheme is reduced.** `k̄[t, t⁻¹]` is a localization of a domain at
215	a non-zero-divisor, hence a domain, hence reduced. The `IsReduced (Spec _)` instance picks
216	up directly from `IsReduced (Localization.Away _)`. -/
217	instance gm_isReduced (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsReduced (Gm kbar).left :=
218	  inferInstanceAs (IsReduced (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))))
219	
220	/-- **`k̄[t, t⁻¹]` is an integral domain.** Localization of the integral polynomial ring
221	`MvPolynomial Unit k̄` at the powers of a nonzero element `X () ≠ 0` preserves the domain
222	property (`IsLocalization.isDomain_localization` applied to
223	`Submonoid.powers (X ()) ≤ nonZeroDivisors`, the latter from `MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero`).
224	
225	Load-bearing for `gm_irreducibleSpace` and `gm_geomIrred`. -/
226	instance gmRing_isDomain (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsDomain (GmRing kbar) := by
227	  unfold GmRing
228	  exact IsLocalization.isDomain_localization
229	    (powers_le_nonZeroDivisors_of_noZeroDivisors (MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero _))
230	
231	/-- **`𝔾_m`'s underlying scheme is irreducible.** Follows from the fact that the
232	global-sections ring `GmRing = k̄[t, t⁻¹]` is an integral domain (`gmRing_isDomain`)
233	together with Mathlib's `Spec` of a domain being irreducible
234	(`PrimeSpectrum.irreducibleSpace`). -/
235	instance gm_irreducibleSpace (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
236	    IrreducibleSpace (Gm kbar).left := by
237	  change IrreducibleSpace (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar)))
238	  infer_instance
239	
240	/-! ### `gm_grpObj` — `GrpObj` instance on `𝔾_m` via `ofRepresentableBy`
241	
242	The route is per the iter-179 `gm-grpobj-representable` analogy (8-step recipe).
243	
244	Step A: build the units-of-global-sections functor `gmHomFunctor : (Over (Spec k̄))ᵒᵖ ⥤ GrpCat`,
245	which sends `T ↦ Γ(T.left, ⊤)ˣ`. This is the presheaf of groups that `Gm` represents.
246	
247	Step B: build the `RepresentableBy` witness asserting `Hom(-, Gm) ≃ Γ(-, ⊤)ˣ` naturally.
248	The per-`T` bijection is the 3-step chain (over-cat unfold → Γ-Spec adjunction →
249	`IsLocalization.Away.lift` universal property of `k̄[t, t⁻¹]`).
250	
251	Step C: `gm_grpObj := GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy (Gm kbar) gmHomFunctor _`. -/
252	
253	/-- The `(Over (Spec k̄))ᵒᵖ ⥤ GrpCat.{u}` functor of units of global sections.
254	This is the presheaf of groups that `Gm` represents. -/
255	private noncomputable def gmHomFunctor (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
256	    (Over (Spec (.of kbar)))ᵒᵖ ⥤ GrpCat.{u} where
257	  obj T := GrpCat.of (Γ(T.unop.left, ⊤))ˣ
258	  map {_ _} φ := GrpCat.ofHom (Units.map (φ.unop.left.appTop).hom.toMonoidHom)
259	  map_id := by
260	    intro T
261	    apply GrpCat.hom_ext
262	    apply MonoidHom.ext
263	    intro u
264	    apply Units.ext
265	    change ((𝟙 T.unop : T.unop ⟶ T.unop).left.appTop).hom u.val = u.val
266	    rw [Over.id_left]
267	    simp
268	  map_comp := by
269	    intros T T' T'' φ ψ
270	    apply GrpCat.hom_ext
271	    apply MonoidHom.ext
272	    intro u
273	    apply Units.ext
274	    change ((φ ≫ ψ).unop.left.appTop).hom u.val
275	      = ((ψ.unop.left.appTop).hom (((φ.unop.left.appTop).hom) u.val))
276	    rw [show (φ ≫ ψ).unop = ψ.unop ≫ φ.unop from rfl, Over.comp_left]
277	    simp
278	
279	/-- **Forward** of the per-`T` bijection: a morphism `f : T ⟶ Gm kbar` in `Over (Spec k̄)`
280	gives the unit in `Γ(T.left, ⊤)ˣ` corresponding to the image of the standard generator
281	`t ∈ k̄[t, t⁻¹]` under the induced ring map.
282	
283	Concretely, `f.left.appTop : Γ(Spec(GmRing), ⊤) ⟶ Γ(T.left, ⊤)`; composing with
284	`ΓSpecIso.inv` gives a ring map `GmRing → Γ(T.left, ⊤)`. The image of the algebra-map
285	generator `t ∈ GmRing` (a unit via `IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit`) is a unit
286	in `Γ(T.left, ⊤)`. -/
287	private noncomputable def gmHomEquiv_toFun (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
288	    (T : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) (f : T ⟶ Gm kbar) : (Γ(T.left, ⊤))ˣ :=
289	  letI ringMap : (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar)) ⟶ (Γ(T.left, ⊤) : CommRingCat) :=
290	    (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫ f.left.appTop
291	  Units.map ringMap.hom.toMonoidHom
292	    (IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit
293	      (S := GmRing kbar) (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)).unit
294	
295	/-- Helper for `gmHomEquiv_invFun`: the underlying scheme morphism factors over `Spec k̄`.
296	Substantive content: reduces to `IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp` + `MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq`
297	+ `Scheme.toSpecΓ_naturality` (the Γ⊣Spec unit triangle). -/
298	private lemma gmHomEquiv_invFun_isOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
299	    (T : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) (u : (Γ(T.left, ⊤))ˣ) :
300	    letI structureRingMap : CommRingCat.of kbar ⟶ (Γ(T.left, ⊤) : CommRingCat) :=
301	      (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv ≫ T.hom.appTop
302	    letI polyMap : MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* Γ(T.left, ⊤) :=
303	      MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom structureRingMap.hom (fun _ => u.val)
304	    haveI hg : IsUnit (polyMap (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) := by
305	      change IsUnit (MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom _ _ (MvPolynomial.X ()))
306	      rw [MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X']
307	      exact u.isUnit
308	    letI liftedMap : GmRing kbar →+* Γ(T.left, ⊤) :=
309	      IsLocalization.Away.lift (S := GmRing kbar)
310	        (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) hg
311	    (T.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom liftedMap)) ≫ (Gm kbar).hom = T.hom := by
312	  -- Set up local abbreviations matching the letI's of the goal.
313	  set structureRingMap : CommRingCat.of kbar ⟶ (Γ(T.left, ⊤) : CommRingCat) :=
314	    (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv ≫ T.hom.appTop
315	  set polyMap : MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* Γ(T.left, ⊤) :=
316	    MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom structureRingMap.hom (fun _ => u.val) with polyMap_def
317	  have hg : IsUnit (polyMap (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) := by
318	    rw [polyMap_def, MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X']
319	    exact u.isUnit
320	  set liftedMap : GmRing kbar →+* Γ(T.left, ⊤) :=
321	    IsLocalization.Away.lift (S := GmRing kbar)
322	      (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) hg with liftedMap_def
323	  -- Unfold `(Gm kbar).hom`.
324	  change T.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom liftedMap) ≫
325	      Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar))) = T.hom
326	  -- Combine the Spec.maps.
327	  rw [← Spec.map_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp]
328	  -- `liftedMap ∘ algebraMap kbar GmRing = structureRingMap.hom`.
329	  have halg : (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar)) =
330	      (algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar)).comp
331	        (algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) := by
332	    ext r
333	    exact (IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) r).symm
334	  have hcomp : liftedMap.comp (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar)) = structureRingMap.hom := by
335	    change (IsLocalization.Away.lift _ hg).comp _ = _
336	    rw [halg, ← RingHom.comp_assoc, IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp]
337	    ext r
338	    change MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom _ _ ((algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) r) = _
339	    rw [MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq, MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_C]
340	  rw [hcomp]
341	  -- Now: T.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map structureRingMap.hom = T.hom
342	  -- structureRingMap = ΓSpecIso.inv ≫ T.hom.appTop  (as CommRingCat morphism)
343	  change T.left.toSpecΓ ≫
344	      Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv ≫
345	          T.hom.appTop).hom)) = T.hom
346	  -- CommRingCat.ofHom (...).hom = the original morphism (since ofHom and .hom are inverses)
347	  rw [show CommRingCat.ofHom (((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv ≫
348	        T.hom.appTop).hom) = (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv ≫ T.hom.appTop from
349	        CommRingCat.ofHom_hom _]
350	  rw [Spec.map_comp, ← Category.assoc, ← Scheme.toSpecΓ_naturality]
351	  -- Now: (T.hom ≫ (Spec _).toSpecΓ) ≫ Spec.map (ΓSpecIso.inv) = T.hom
352	  rw [Category.assoc, toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv, Category.comp_id]
353	
354	/-- **Backward** of the per-`T` bijection: a unit `u : Γ(T.left, ⊤)ˣ` gives the morphism
355	`T ⟶ Gm kbar` defined by the ring map `k̄[t, t⁻¹] → Γ(T.left, ⊤)` sending `t ↦ u.val`.
356	
357	Concretely: build the polynomial-ring map `MvPolynomial Unit k̄ →+* Γ(T.left, ⊤)` via
358	`MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom` (sending `X() ↦ u.val`); lift through the localization via
359	`IsLocalization.Away.lift` (using that `u.val` is a unit); convert to a scheme morphism
360	via `Spec.map`-then-`toSpecΓ`; bundle with the `Over`-commutativity proof. -/
361	private noncomputable def gmHomEquiv_invFun (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
362	    (T : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) (u : (Γ(T.left, ⊤))ˣ) : T ⟶ Gm kbar :=
363	  -- structure ring map kbar → Γ(T.left, ⊤), via T.hom.appTop and ΓSpecIso
364	  letI structureRingMap : CommRingCat.of kbar ⟶ (Γ(T.left, ⊤) : CommRingCat) :=
365	    (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv ≫ T.hom.appTop
366	  letI polyMap : MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* Γ(T.left, ⊤) :=
367	    MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom structureRingMap.hom (fun _ => u.val)
368	  haveI hg : IsUnit (polyMap (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) := by
369	    change IsUnit (MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom _ _ (MvPolynomial.X ()))
370	    rw [MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X']
371	    exact u.isUnit
372	  letI liftedMap : GmRing kbar →+* Γ(T.left, ⊤) :=
373	    IsLocalization.Away.lift (S := GmRing kbar)
374	      (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) hg
375	  Over.homMk
376	    (T.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom liftedMap))
377	    (gmHomEquiv_invFun_isOver kbar T u)
378	
379	/-- Round-trip identity 1: `invFun ∘ toFun = id`. Substantive content reduces to
380	the Γ⊣Spec adjunction injectivity (`ext_to_Spec`) + `IsLocalization.Away.lift` uniqueness
381	applied to the lifted ring map from the morphism's `appTop`. -/
382	private lemma gmHomEquiv_left_inv (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
383	    (T : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) :
384	    Function.LeftInverse (gmHomEquiv_invFun kbar T) (gmHomEquiv_toFun kbar T) := by
385	  intro f
386	  apply Over.OverMorphism.ext
387	  -- Goal: (gmHomEquiv_invFun (gmHomEquiv_toFun f)).left = f.left
388	  -- Both go T.left ⟶ Gm.left. By Scheme extensionality + IsAffine target,
389	  -- compare via Γ-Spec adjunction: the ring maps GmRing → Γ(T.left, ⊤) agree.
390	  -- On generators of GmRing (image of algebraMap kbar GmRing + image of X())
391	  -- the maps agree by:
392	  --  (a) Over.w forces both ring maps to factor through structureRingMap on kbar
393	  --  (b) toFun was defined precisely as the image of X() under f's induced ring map
394	  -- Sound mathematically; iter-181+ Mathlib-driven proof.

397	/-- Round-trip identity 2: `toFun ∘ invFun = id`. Substantive content reduces to
398	`IsLocalization.Away.lift_eq` (image of the algebra-map generator is the unit)
399	plus `ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality` + `Scheme.toSpecΓ_appTop` (canceling the unit triangle). -/
400	private lemma gmHomEquiv_right_inv (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
401	    (T : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) :
402	    Function.RightInverse (gmHomEquiv_invFun kbar T) (gmHomEquiv_toFun kbar T) := by
403	  intro u

406	  change ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫
407	        (gmHomEquiv_invFun kbar T u).left.appTop).hom
408	      ((algebraMap _ _) (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) = u.val

412	  --       Iso.inv_hom_id + Category.comp_id (this part works).
413	  --   (b) Rewrite using (a).

415	  -- BLOCKER: step (a)'s morphism-level rewriting `rw [← Scheme.ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality_assoc]`

418	  -- iter-181+ work to land via a manual `congr` or `Iso.inv_comp_eq` rewrite path.
419	  sorry

421	/-- Naturality of `homEquiv`: pre-composition by `φ : T' ⟶ T` in `Over (Spec k̄)` corresponds
422	to `Units.map` of `φ.left.appTop` on the units side. Reduces to `Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop`
423	and the unit-element transfer through `IsLocalization.Away.lift` and the functor's `map` action. -/
424	private lemma gmHomEquiv_homEquiv_comp (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
425	    {T T' : Over (Spec (.of kbar))} (f : T ⟶ T') (g : T' ⟶ Gm kbar) :
426	    gmHomEquiv_toFun kbar T (f ≫ g) =
427	      (gmHomFunctor kbar ⋙ forget _).map f.op (gmHomEquiv_toFun kbar T' g) := by
428	  apply Units.ext
429	  simp only [gmHomEquiv_toFun, gmHomFunctor, Functor.comp_map, Units.coe_map,
430	    IsUnit.unit_spec, GrpCat.hom_ofHom, Over.comp_left, Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop]
431	  rfl
432	
433	/-- The `RepresentableBy` witness exhibiting `Gm kbar` as a representing object for the
434	units-of-global-sections functor. Per-`T` bijection is `gmHomEquiv_toFun` / `gmHomEquiv_invFun`;
435	round-trip identities split into `gmHomEquiv_left_inv` / `gmHomEquiv_right_inv` (named
436	substantive sorries) and naturality `homEquiv_comp` (named substantive sorry). The deep
437	Mathlib-gap content per the iter-179 `gm-grpobj-representable` analogy — no
438	`Spec(Localization.Away)` analogue of `AffineSpace.homOverEquiv` in Mathlib. -/
439	private noncomputable def gmHomFunctor_representableBy (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
440	    (gmHomFunctor kbar ⋙ forget _).RepresentableBy (Gm kbar) where
441	  homEquiv {T} :=
442	    { toFun := gmHomEquiv_toFun kbar T
443	      invFun := gmHomEquiv_invFun kbar T
444	      left_inv := gmHomEquiv_left_inv kbar T
445	      right_inv := gmHomEquiv_right_inv kbar T }
446	  homEquiv_comp := gmHomEquiv_homEquiv_comp kbar
447	
448	/-- **`GrpObj`-structure on `𝔾_m` via `ofRepresentableBy`.**
449	
450	Installs the multiplicative-group structure on `Gm` using `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` with
451	the units functor `T ↦ GrpCat.of Γ(T.left, ⊤)ˣ`. The representable-by witness exploits the
452	fact that morphisms into `Spec (Localization.Away t)` correspond exactly to units in the
453	global sections (Mathlib's `IsLocalization.Away`-Spec bijection).
454	
455	This `GrpObj Gm` is the LIVE consumer of the iter-166 `morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const`
456	proof body (the `𝔾_m`-scaling shortcut applies `hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` with
457	`W = Gm`). -/
458	instance gm_grpObj (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : GrpObj (Gm kbar) :=
459	  GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy (Gm kbar) (gmHomFunctor kbar) (gmHomFunctor_representableBy kbar)
460	
461	/-- **`𝔾_m` is smooth over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from `smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed` once
462	`GrpObj`, `LocallyOfFinitePresentation`, and `IsReduced` are installed. -/
463	instance gm_smooth (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar] :
464	    Smooth (Gm kbar).hom :=
465	  have : GrpObj (Over.mk (Gm kbar).hom) := gm_grpObj kbar
466	  smooth_of_grpObj_of_isAlgClosed (Gm kbar).hom

468	/-- The `k̄`-point `1 ∈ 𝔾_m` (the multiplicative identity), encoded as the group-object
469	unit `η[Gm kbar] : 𝟙_ ⟶ Gm kbar`. -/
470	def Gm.onePt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
471	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ Gm kbar :=
472	  η[Gm kbar]

1	# Analogy: installing `GrpObj Gm` via `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy`
2	
3	## Mode
4	api-alignment
5	
6	## Slug
7	gm-grpobj-representable
8

10	179
11	
12	## Question
13	
14	What is the canonical Mathlib idiom for installing the `GrpObj` instance on
15	`𝔾_m = Spec (k̄[t, t⁻¹])` via the representable-by-units functor
16	construction? The project's
17	`AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/Points.lean:251` has had this as
18	bare `sorry` for 11+ iterations (iter-167 escalation watch). It is
19	load-bearing for `gm_smooth` (L255), the Route C base case
20	`morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const_aux` chain, and `iotaGm_isDominant`
21	(`AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean:86`).
22	
23	## Project artifact(s)
24	
25	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/Points.lean:240-251` — declaration
26	  + docstring; body bare `sorry`.
27	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/Points.lean:253-258` — downstream
28	  `gm_smooth` instance consuming `gm_grpObj`.
29	- `analogies/gm-grpobj-and-friends.md` (iter-167) — prior analogy with
30	  the same recipe sketched but not landed.
31	
32	## Mathlib state (verified iter-179)
33	
34	- **`GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy`** lives at
35	  `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Monoidal/Cartesian/Grp_.lean:35`.
36	  Signature (verbatim):
37	
38	  ```lean
39	  def GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy
40	      (F : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ GrpCat.{w}) (α : (F ⋙ forget _).RepresentableBy X) :
41	      GrpObj X where
42	    __ := MonObj.ofRepresentableBy X (F ⋙ forget₂ GrpCat MonCat) α
43	    inv := α.homEquiv'.symm (α.homEquiv (𝟙 _))⁻¹
44	    left_inv := …  -- discharged by Mathlib
45	    right_inv := … -- discharged by Mathlib
46	  ```
47	
48	  All five group-object axioms are handled inside `ofRepresentableBy` (the
49	  monoid axioms come from `MonObj.ofRepresentableBy` at
50	  `Cartesian/Mon_.lean:157`; only `left_inv`/`right_inv` need to be added
51	  in `ofRepresentableBy` itself, and those are also discharged by the
52	  Mathlib body). The caller's only obligation is to supply `F` and `α`.
53	
54	- **`Functor.RepresentableBy`** lives at `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Yoneda.lean:285`:
55	
56	  ```lean
57	  structure RepresentableBy (F : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ Type v) (Y : C) where
58	    homEquiv {X : C} : (X ⟶ Y) ≃ F.obj (op X)
59	    homEquiv_comp {X X' : C} (f : X ⟶ X') (g : X' ⟶ Y) :
60	      homEquiv (f ≫ g) = F.map f.op (homEquiv g) := by cat_disch
61	  ```
62	
63	  So the project must build a per-`T` equiv `(T ⟶ Gm) ≃ Γ(T.left, ⊤)ˣ`
64	  plus naturality, where `T` ranges over `(Over (Spec (.of kbar)))ᵒᵖ`.
65	
66	- **No concrete-scheme uses of `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` in Mathlib.**
67	  A grep across `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/` finds zero callers. The
68	  uses in Mathlib are abstract (the round-trip lemma
69	  `ofRepresentableBy_yonedaGrpObjRepresentableBy` at
70	  `Cartesian/Grp_.lean:90` and three internal hooks). No
71	  `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Group.Gm`, no `GroupScheme.Gm` namespace.
72	
73	- **`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace.homOverEquiv`** (the
74	  affine-space analogue at `AffineSpace.lean:155`):
75	
76	  ```lean
77	  def homOverEquiv : { f : X ⟶ 𝔸(n; S) // f.IsOver S } ≃ (n → Γ(X, ⊤))
78	  ```
79	
80	  This is the closed form of the bijection chain for `Ga`. For `Gm`,
81	  Mathlib does NOT ship a parallel `Spec(Localization.Away t).homOverEquiv`,
82	  so the project must compose the chain by hand from primitives
83	  (`ΓSpec.adjunction.homEquiv` + `IsLocalization.Away.lift`).
84	
85	- **`ΓSpec.adjunction.homEquiv`** at `GammaSpecAdjunction.lean:494/500`:
86	  `(X ⟶ Spec R) ≃ (R ⟶ Γ(X, ⊤))` (the global-sections / Spec adjunction)
87	  with the over-`Spec k̄` condition automatic from the Spec-of-algebra-map
88	  structure.
89	
90	- **`IsLocalization.Away.lift`** at `Mathlib/RingTheory/Localization/Away/Basic.lean`:
91	
92	  ```lean
93	  lemma IsLocalization.Away.lift (x : R) [IsLocalization.Away x S] {g : R →+* P}
94	      (hg : IsUnit (g x)) : S →+* P
95	  ```
96	
97	  with companion `IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit` (algebraMap x is
98	  a unit in S) and `IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp` (lift composed with
99	  algebraMap equals g). These three lemmas together give the bijection
100	  `(k̄[t,t⁻¹] →ₐ[k̄] R) ≃ {u : R // IsUnit u}` (forward: send `φ` to
101	  `φ(t)` which is forced to be a unit by
102	  `IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit`; backward: apply
103	  `IsLocalization.Away.lift` to the polynomial ring map `t ↦ u`).
104	
105	## Decisions identified
106	
107	### Decision 1: Is `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` the right Mathlib idiom?
108	
109	- **Mathlib idiom**: YES. `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` is THE canonical
110	  Yoneda installer for `GrpObj` on a representable presheaf of groups.
111	  Cite: `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Monoidal/Cartesian/Grp_.lean:35`. Why
112	  Mathlib chose it: this is the categorical formulation of "to give a
113	  group structure on an object `X`, exhibit `Hom(-, X)` as a presheaf of
114	  groups". It is strictly more powerful than `GrpObj.mk` (the explicit
115	  μ/η/ι path) because it automates all five axioms via the Equiv's
116	  injectivity (see the inv-axiom proofs at L39-L54). The downstream
117	  Mathlib API (`Hom.group`, `yonedaGrp`, the FullyFaithful embedding)
118	  composes naturally with `ofRepresentableBy`-installed instances.
119	
120	- **Project's current path**: planned to use `ofRepresentableBy`; body is
121	  `sorry`. The docstring at L240-L251 names the idiom explicitly. Aligned.
122	
123	- **Gap**: identical. The project's planned path IS the Mathlib idiom.
124	
125	- **Verdict**: **PROCEED with `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy`**. No parallel
126	  API risk.
127	
128	### Decision 2: Is there a Mathlib analogue for `𝔾_m`'s `GrpObj` instance already?
129	
130	- **Mathlib state**: NO. Searched all of `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/` for
131	  any instance of `GrpObj` on a `Spec`-of-units construction, on
132	  `Spec (Localization.Away _)`, on `Spec (FractionRing _)`, or in any
133	  `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Group/*.lean` file. The Group folder ships
134	  only `Smooth.lean` (smoothness from `GrpObj`) and `Abelian.lean`
135	  (commutativity from properness + group-object). Both CONSUME an
136	  arbitrary `GrpObj`; neither produces one for a concrete scheme.
137	
138	- **Project's current path**: build the instance from scratch using
139	  `ofRepresentableBy` + the 3-step bijection chain. NOT a parallel API —
140	  this is a genuine Mathlib gap.
141	
142	- **Gap**: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL. The project is FIRST in Mathlib
143	  ecosystem to install a concrete-scheme `GrpObj` via `ofRepresentableBy`.
144	  Future upstream candidate: `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Group.Gm` (single
145	  file, ~100 LOC).
146	
147	- **Verdict**: **PROCEED** with the from-scratch build. Reusable as an
148	  upstream contribution post-formalization.
149	
150	### Decision 3: What's the closed body's shape?
151	
152	The 8-step recipe (each line a single Mathlib idiom):
153	
154	1. Open `letI := Spec (.of kbar)`, then build `F : (Over (Spec (.of kbar)))ᵒᵖ ⥤ GrpCat.{u}`
155	   with `obj T := GrpCat.of Γ(T.unop.left, ⊤)ˣ`. Use `Scheme.Hom.appTop` for
156	   the map: a morphism `φ : T → T'` in `Over (Spec k̄)` gives
157	   `(φ.unop.left).appTop : Γ(T'.unop.left, ⊤) ⟶ Γ(T.unop.left, ⊤)`, which
158	   restricts to a group hom on units via `Units.map`.
159	
160	2. Build the per-`T` equiv `homEquiv : (T ⟶ Gm kbar) ≃ Γ(T.unop.left, ⊤)ˣ`
161	   as a 3-step composition of equivs:
162	
163	   - **Step 2a** (over-category unfold): `(T ⟶ Gm)` in `Over (Spec k̄)` ≃
164	     `{f : T.unop.left ⟶ Spec(k̄[t,t⁻¹]) // f ≫ struct = T.unop.hom}`.
165	     Use `Over.homMk` / `Over.Hom.left` pair to assemble/destructure.
166	
167	   - **Step 2b** (Spec adjunction): the underlying scheme morphism
168	     `f : T.unop.left ⟶ Spec(k̄[t,t⁻¹])` corresponds via
169	     `ΓSpec.adjunction.homEquiv` to a ring map `k̄[t,t⁻¹] →+* Γ(T.unop.left, ⊤)`,
170	     and the "over `Spec k̄`" commutativity turns it into a `k̄`-algebra map
171	     (the structure map's image of `algebraMap k̄ (k̄[t,t⁻¹])` is forced).
172	
173	   - **Step 2c** (universal property of the Away localisation): a
174	     `k̄`-algebra map `k̄[t,t⁻¹] →ₐ[k̄] Γ(T.unop.left, ⊤)` is the choice of
175	     a unit. Forward: take the image of `t = X ()` (it is a unit by
176	     `IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit`'s functorial transfer:
177	     algebraMap of t in k̄[t,t⁻¹] is a unit, hence so is its image). Backward:
178	     given a unit `u : Γ(T.unop.left, ⊤)ˣ`, build the polynomial ring map
179	     `MvPolynomial.aeval (Unit.elim u) : MvPolynomial Unit k̄ →ₐ[k̄] Γ(T.unop.left, ⊤)`,
180	     then factor through `Localization.Away (X ()) = k̄[t,t⁻¹]` via
181	     `IsLocalization.Away.lift`. `MvPolynomial.aeval_unique` discharges
182	     well-definedness.
183	
184	3. Build `homEquiv_comp` (naturality): the three component equivs are
185	   each natural (the over-cat unfold is structurally natural; the Spec
186	   adjunction is the unit/counit of an adjunction; the Localization.Away
187	   step is natural by `IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp`). Discharge
188	   via `Equiv.trans` composition; `cat_disch` should close most of it.
189	
190	4. Apply `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy (Gm kbar) F ⟨homEquiv, homEquiv_comp⟩`.
191	
192	### Decision 4: Cross-domain comparison
193	
194	- **Searched in `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/Grp/`**: no `Spec`-based
195	  `GrpObj` constructions.
196	- **Searched in `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Monoidal/`**: only the abstract
197	  `ofRepresentableBy` idioms (Grp_, CommGrp_, Mon_, CommMon_); no concrete
198	  builders.
199	- **Searched in `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/`**: no concrete `MonObj`
200	  or `GrpObj` on `Spec (Localization.Away _)` or
201	  `Spec (FractionRing _)`.
202	
203	Conclusion: the project's path is the FIRST concrete-scheme instance
204	of `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` in the Mathlib ecosystem. No prior art to
205	mimic; the recipe in Decision 3 is the canonical synthesis from
206	Mathlib's primitives.
207	
208	## LOC estimate
209	
210	- Functor `F : (Over (Spec (.of kbar)))ᵒᵖ ⥤ GrpCat.{u}` definition: **20-30 LOC**.
211	- The per-`T` `homEquiv` (3-step composition): **40-60 LOC**.
212	- The `homEquiv_comp` naturality field: **15-25 LOC** (mostly `cat_disch`
213	  + one or two explicit rewrites).
214	- Total: **75-115 LOC** for a clean closed body.
215	
216	## Recommendation
217	
218	Use `GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy` with the 3-step bijection chain in
219	Decision 3. The skeleton:
220	
221	```lean
222	instance gm_grpObj (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : GrpObj (Gm kbar) := by
223	  refine GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy (Gm kbar)
224	    { obj := fun T => GrpCat.of (Γ(T.unop.left, ⊤))ˣ
225	      map := fun {T T'} φ =>
226	        GrpCat.ofHom (Units.map (φ.unop.left.appTop).hom.toMonoidHom)
227	      map_id := by intro T; ext u; simp [Scheme.Hom.id_appTop]
228	      map_comp := by intros T T' T'' φ ψ; ext u; simp [Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop] }
229	    ?_
230	  refine
231	    { homEquiv := fun {T} => ?_
232	      homEquiv_comp := ?_ }
233	  · -- Step 2a-2c composed:
234	    -- (T ⟶ Gm) in Over (Spec k̄)
235	    --   ≃ {f : T.unop.left ⟶ Spec(k̄[t,t⁻¹]) // IsOver Spec k̄}   -- over-cat unfold
236	    --   ≃ {φ : k̄[t,t⁻¹] →ₐ[k̄] Γ(T.unop.left, ⊤)}                 -- ΓSpec.adjunction
237	    --   ≃ {u : Γ(T.unop.left, ⊤) // IsUnit u}                     -- IsLocalization.Away.lift
238	    --   = Γ(T.unop.left, ⊤)ˣ
239	    sorry  -- the 3-step Equiv composition (recipe per Decision 3, 40-60 LOC)
240	  · sorry  -- naturality via cat_disch + Spec.map / Units.map naturality
241	```
242	
243	The dispatching prover should split the `homEquiv` body into three
244	named helper lemmas (one per step in Decision 3), each ~15-20 LOC,
245	so the assembly is just `Equiv.trans ∘ Equiv.trans`. The `homEquiv_comp`
246	field reduces to checking that pre-composing `T ⟶ Gm` with
247	`φ : T' → T` produces the comap of the unit on the `Γ`-side; this is
248	`Scheme.Hom.appTop` naturality + `Units.map` functoriality.
249	
250	**Bonus parallel — `ga_grpObj`:** the same `ofRepresentableBy` idiom with
251	`AffineSpace.homOverEquiv` (a single Mathlib lemma at `AffineSpace.lean:155`)
252	discharging the 3-step chain in one step closes `ga_grpObj` in 2-3 lines.
253	The asymmetry: `Ga = 𝔸(Fin 1, Spec k̄)` is exactly the affine space
254	`AffineSpace.homOverEquiv` is built for; `Gm` is not, because Mathlib
255	does NOT ship a "homOverEquiv for `Spec (Localization.Away t)`" lemma.
256	This is the missing Mathlib gap-fill that would unify the two
257	constructions; for now, the project owes ~100 LOC for `gm_grpObj`
258	versus ~3 LOC for `ga_grpObj`.
259	
260	## Reversal trigger
261	
262	If the 3-step bijection composition encounters:
263	
264	- **Universe issues** in `GrpCat.{w}` vs the project's `.{u}` (the
265	  `homEquiv` value must live in `Type u`; if the unit-group at level `u`
266	  can't be coerced to level `w` of `F`, redefine `F` with explicit
267	  universe annotation `F : (Over (Spec (.of kbar)))ᵒᵖ ⥤ GrpCat.{u}`);
268	- **Definitional-eq issues** with the `IsOver` predicate (the
269	  over-category unfold may need `Over.homMk`-η-expansion via `Over.hom_ext`);
270	- **`IsScalarTower kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (k̄[t,t⁻¹])` synthesis
271	  failures** in step 2c (mitigated by the iter-170
272	  `Algebra.compHom`-style fix from `route-c-option-c-committed-iter170.md`
273	  — instantiate with the same `Algebra.compHom kbar (algebraMap kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar))`
274	  pattern that unblocked `Algebra kbar (HomogeneousLocalization.Away _ _)`);
275	
276	then **fall back to direct `GrpObj.mk`**: hand-define `μ`, `η`, `ι` as
277	`Spec.map` of explicit ring maps —
278	
279	- `μ := Spec.map ofHom (k̄[t,t⁻¹] → k̄[t,t⁻¹] ⊗_{k̄} k̄[t,t⁻¹], t ↦ t ⊗ t)`,
280	- `η := Spec.map ofHom (k̄[t,t⁻¹] → k̄, t ↦ 1)`,
281	- `ι := Spec.map ofHom (k̄[t,t⁻¹] → k̄[t,t⁻¹], t ↦ t⁻¹)`,
282	
283	and prove the five group-object axioms by `Spec.map_comp` +
284	`CommRingCat.hom_ext` + ring-level computation. More wiring
285	(~150-200 LOC) but uses only `Spec.map`, `pullbackSpecIso`, and
286	`IsLocalization.Away.lift` — no over-category Yoneda, no Functor
287	build, no naturality. This fallback is the iter-167 escalation-watch
288	backup and was deliberately deferred in favor of the `ofRepresentableBy`
289	path because the latter is API-aligned. Fall back only if the
290	Yoneda path stalls for ≥2 iters with the same root cause.

142	variable {g : R →+* P}
143	
144	/-- Given `x : R`, a localization map `F : R →+* S` away from `x`, and a map of `CommSemiring`s
145	`g : R →+* P` such that `g x` is invertible, the homomorphism induced from `S` to `P` sending
146	`z : S` to `g y * (g x)⁻ⁿ`, where `y : R, n : ℕ` are such that `z = F y * (F x)⁻ⁿ`. -/
147	noncomputable def lift (hg : IsUnit (g x)) : S →+* P :=
148	  IsLocalization.lift fun y : Submonoid.powers x =>
149	    show IsUnit (g y.1) by
150	      obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := y.2
151	      rw [← hn, g.map_pow]
152	      exact IsUnit.map (powMonoidHom n : P →* P) hg
153

155	theorem lift_eq (hg : IsUnit (g x)) (a : R) : lift x hg (algebraMap R S a) = g a :=
156	  IsLocalization.lift_eq _ _

159	theorem lift_comp (hg : IsUnit (g x)) : (lift x hg).comp (algebraMap R S) = g :=
160	  IsLocalization.lift_comp _

162	/-- Given `x y : R` and localizations `S`, `P` away from `x` and `y * x`
163	respectively, the homomorphism induced from `S` to `P`. -/
164	noncomputable def awayToAwayLeft (y : R) [Algebra R P] [IsLocalization.Away (y * x) P] : S →+* P :=
165	  lift x <| isUnit_of_dvd (y * x) (dvd_mul_left _ _)

168	respectively, the homomorphism induced from `S` to `P`. -/
169	noncomputable def awayToAwayRight (y : R) [Algebra R P] [IsLocalization.Away (x * y) P] : S →+* P :=
170	  lift x <| isUnit_of_dvd (x * y) (dvd_mul_right _ _)

/-- Round-trip identity 2: `toFun ∘ invFun = id`. Substantive content reduces to
`IsLocalization.Away.lift_eq` (image of the algebra-map generator is the unit)
plus `ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality` + `Scheme.toSpecΓ_appTop` (canceling the unit triangle). -/
private lemma gmHomEquiv_right_inv (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
    (T : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) :
    Function.RightInverse (gmHomEquiv_invFun kbar T) (gmHomEquiv_toFun kbar T) := by
  intro u
  apply Units.ext
  -- Stage the proof at the CommRingCat morphism level.
  change ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫
        (gmHomEquiv_invFun kbar T u).left.appTop).hom
      ((algebraMap _ _) (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) = u.val
  -- The proof would proceed by:
  --   (a) Establish morphism equality `(ΓSpecIso _).inv ≫ invFun(u).left.appTop = ofHom liftedMap`
  --       via Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop + toSpecΓ_appTop + ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality_assoc +
  --       Iso.inv_hom_id + Category.comp_id (this part works).
  --   (b) Rewrite using (a).
  --   (c) Conclude via IsLocalization.Away.lift_eq + MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X'.
  -- BLOCKER: step (a)'s morphism-level rewriting `rw [← Scheme.ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality_assoc]`
  -- fails to find the matching pattern even though both look syntactically identical;

input.new_string:
/-- Round-trip identity 2: `toFun ∘ invFun = id`. Substantive content reduces to
`IsLocalization.Away.lift_eq` (image of the algebra-map generator is the unit)
plus `ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality` + `Scheme.toSpecΓ_appTop` (canceling the unit triangle). -/
private lemma gmHomEquiv_right_inv (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
    (T : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) :
    Function.RightInverse (gmHomEquiv_invFun kbar T) (gmHomEquiv_toFun kbar T) := by
  intro u
  apply Units.ext
  -- Stage the proof at the CommRingCat morphism level.
  change ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫
        (gmHomEquiv_invFun kbar T u).left.appTop).hom
      ((algebraMap _ _) (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)) = u.val
  -- Unfold `invFun` to expose `T.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (ofHom liftedMap)` as the
  -- underlying scheme morphism.
  unfold gmHomEquiv_invFun
  change ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫
      (T.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
        (IsLocalization.Away.lift
          (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) _))).appTop).hom
      ((algebraMap _ _) (MvPolynomial.X ())) = u.val
  -- Step (a): morphism equality `(ΓSpecIso _).inv ≫ invFun(u).left.appTop = ofHom liftedMap`
  -- via `Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop`, `← Scheme.ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality_assoc`,
  -- `Scheme.toSpecΓ_appTop`, `Iso.inv_hom_id`, `Category.comp_id`.
  rw [Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop, ← Scheme.ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality_assoc,
    Scheme.toSpecΓ_appTop, Iso.inv_hom_id, Category.comp_id]
  -- Step (b)+(c): conclude via `IsLocalization.Away.lift_eq` + `MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X'`.
  simp [IsLocalization.Away.lift_eq, MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X']

the Γ⊣Spec adjunction injectivity (`ext_to_Spec`) + `IsLocalization.Away.lift` uniqueness
applied to the lifted ring map from the morphism's `appTop`. -/
private lemma gmHomEquiv_left_inv (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
    (T : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) :
    Function.LeftInverse (gmHomEquiv_invFun kbar T) (gmHomEquiv_toFun kbar T) := by
  intro f
  apply Over.OverMorphism.ext
  -- Goal: (gmHomEquiv_invFun (gmHomEquiv_toFun f)).left = f.left
  -- Both go T.left ⟶ Gm.left. By Scheme extensionality + IsAffine target,
  -- compare via Γ-Spec adjunction: the ring maps GmRing → Γ(T.left, ⊤) agree.
  -- On generators of GmRing (image of algebraMap kbar GmRing + image of X())
  -- the maps agree by:
  --  (a) Over.w forces both ring maps to factor through structureRingMap on kbar
  --  (b) toFun was defined precisely as the image of X() under f's induced ring map
  -- Sound mathematically; iter-181+ Mathlib-driven proof.

input.new_string:
/-- Round-trip identity 1: `invFun ∘ toFun = id`. Substantive content reduces to
the Γ⊣Spec adjunction injectivity (`ext_to_Spec`) + `IsLocalization.Away.lift` uniqueness
applied to the lifted ring map from the morphism's `appTop`. -/
private lemma gmHomEquiv_left_inv (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
    (T : Over (Spec (.of kbar))) :
    Function.LeftInverse (gmHomEquiv_invFun kbar T) (gmHomEquiv_toFun kbar T) := by
  intro f
  apply Over.OverMorphism.ext
  -- Goal: (invFun (toFun f)).left = f.left as T.left ⟶ Spec(GmRing).
  -- (a) `Over.w f` transferred through `Spec.appTop` + `ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality`:
  --     `structureRingMap = algebraMap kbar GmRing ≫ g_f` (as CommRingCat morphisms),
  --     where `g_f := (ΓSpecIso (GmRing)).inv ≫ f.left.appTop`.
  have hw : (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv ≫ T.hom.appTop =
      CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar)) ≫
        ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫ f.left.appTop) := by
    have hOver := Over.w f
    -- `(Gm kbar).hom = Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar)))` by defeq.
    change f.left ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar))) = T.hom
      at hOver
    rw [← hOver, Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop]
    set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
      rw [← Scheme.ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality_assoc]
  -- (b) Compare the two morphisms `T.left ⟶ Spec(GmRing)` via `ext_to_Spec`.
  apply AlgebraicGeometry.ext_to_Spec
  show (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫
      (gmHomEquiv_invFun kbar T (gmHomEquiv_toFun kbar T f)).left.appTop
    = (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫ f.left.appTop
  -- Reduce LHS to `CommRingCat.ofHom liftedMap_{toFun f}` (same chain as in `right_inv`).
  unfold gmHomEquiv_invFun
  change (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫
      (T.left.toSpecΓ ≫ Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (IsLocalization.Away.lift
        (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) _))).appTop
    = (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫ f.left.appTop
  rw [Scheme.Hom.comp_appTop, ← Scheme.ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality_assoc,
    Scheme.toSpecΓ_appTop, Iso.inv_hom_id, Category.comp_id]
  -- Goal: `CommRingCat.ofHom liftedMap_{toFun f} = g_f` as CommRingCat morphisms.
  apply CommRingCat.hom_ext
  simp only [CommRingCat.hom_ofHom, IsLocalization.Away.lift]
  -- Apply `IsLocalization.lift_unique`: it suffices to show
  -- `g_f.hom ∘ algebraMap_{MvPoly→GmRing} = polyMap` on the MvPoly side.
  refine IsLocalization.lift_unique _ ?_
  show ∀ (x : MvPolynomial Unit kbar),
    (((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar))).inv ≫ f.left.appTop).hom.comp
        (algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar))) x = (MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom _ _) x
  apply DFunLike.congr_fun
  -- Both sides are RingHoms `MvPoly Unit kbar →+* Γ(T.left, ⊤)`. By `ringHom_ext`,
  -- it suffices to compare on `C r` (constants) and `X i` (variables).
  refine MvPolynomial.ringHom_ext (fun r => ?_) (fun i => ?_)
  · -- `C r`-case: by `Over.w f`, `g_f ∘ algebraMap_{kbar→GmRing} = structureRingMap`.
    simp only [RingHom.comp_apply, ← MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq,
      ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar)]
    exact congrArg (fun (g : CommRingCat.of kbar ⟶ _) => g.hom r) hw
  · -- `X i`-case: `i : Unit`, so `i = ()`; then both sides reduce to `(toFun f).val`.
    obtain ⟨⟩ := i
    simp [gmHomEquiv_toFun]

88	    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
89	  Over.homMk
90	    (Proj.fromOfGlobalSections (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
91	      (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
92	      (ProjectiveLineBar.irrelevant_map_eq_top kbar v i hi)) <| by
93	    -- Section condition: fromOfGlobalSections ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.hom = 𝟙.
94	    haveI : IsScalarTower kbar ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0)
95	        (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
96	      IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun _ => rfl
97	    -- Expose the structure morphism's composition shape.
98	    change Proj.fromOfGlobalSections _ _ _ ≫ Proj.toSpecZero _ ≫ Spec.map _ = _
99	    -- Combine via `fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero`, then unify the two Spec.maps.
100	    rw [← Category.assoc, Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero, Category.assoc,
101	      ← Spec.map_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp, RingHom.comp_assoc,
102	      ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_eq kbar, MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq]
103	    -- The inner composition `(evalIntoGlobal v) ∘ C` collapses to `(ΓSpecIso).inv.hom`.
104	    -- Build a CommRingCat-level equation, then apply `Spec.map` and finish.
105	    have hcc : CommRingCat.ofHom
106	        ((ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v).comp MvPolynomial.C) =
107	        (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv := by
108	      apply CommRingCat.hom_ext
109	      ext r
110	      change (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv.hom
111	          ((MvPolynomial.eval v) (MvPolynomial.C r)) = _
112	      rw [MvPolynomial.eval_C]
113	    calc _ = (Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar)).toSpecΓ ≫
114	            Spec.map (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv := by
115	            exact congrArg _ (congrArg Spec.map hcc)
116	      _ = _ := AlgebraicGeometry.toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv _
117	
118	/-- The `k̄`-point `0 = [0 : 1] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom` via
119	`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` of the evaluation `X₀ ↦ 0`, `X₁ ↦ 1`. -/
