453	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules

/-- **Trivial bridge** (pushforward of pullback at sections — rfl).

The section of `(pushforward f').obj ((pullback g').obj F)` over an
open `U ⊆ S'` identifies definitionally with the section of
`(pullback g').obj F` over `f' ⁻¹ᵁ U`, by `Scheme.Modules.pushforward_obj_obj`.
Factored as a separate (closed) lemma to document step (3) of the
intended-body plan in
`canonicalBaseChangeMap_app_app_isIso_of_isAffineOpen` cleanly. -/
private lemma pushforward_pullback_section_eq_pullback_section
    {X X' S S' : Scheme.{u}} (f' : X' ⟶ S') (g' : X' ⟶ X)
    (F : X.Modules) (U : S'.Opens) :
    Γ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward f').obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g').obj F), U) =
      Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g').obj F, f' ⁻¹ᵁ U) := rfl

/-- **Affine-base case of flat base change at affine opens** (Stacks tag 00H8 / 02KE).

the case where the *base* `S` is affine, so we may take `V := ⊤ : S.Opens`
as the (trivially affine) compatible open: every affine `U ⊆ S'` satisfies
`U ≤ (Opens.map g.base).obj ⊤ = ⊤`. The body of this helper captures the
substantive algebraic step of Stacks 02KE / 00H8 (i.e. the actual application
of `Module.Flat.isBaseChange` after the section-vs-tensor-product
identification): on the affine ring map `Γ(S, ⊤) → Γ(S', U)` (flat by
`Flat.flat_appLE` applied to `Flat g`), the algebraic flat base change of

`Γ(S', U) ⊗_{Γ(S, ⊤)} Γ(F, f⁻¹⊤) ≃ Γ((g')*F, f'⁻¹U)` that agrees with the
canonical Beck–Chevalley section map under the (Mathlib-gap'd)
section-vs-tensor-product identification of `(pullback g).obj _`'s
`U`-section.

# AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/QuotScheme.lean

## Lane F (iter-181) — `canonicalBaseChangeMap_app_app_isIso_of_isAffineOpen` body

### Attempt 1 — structural decomposition into bridge helpers

- **Approach**: Per the iter-181 objectives ("if neither sub-bridge lands in
  budget, factor each into a single named helper sorry"), I factored the body
  of the affine-open helper into the two intended sub-bridges explicitly,
  closing the trivial one and capturing the substantive Mathlib gap in a
  named specialized helper. The structure now mirrors the intended-body plan
  (steps 1–4 in the original `_of_isAffineOpen` docstring) as actual code.

  Added two new private helpers above the existing `_of_isAffineOpen`:

  1. **`pushforward_pullback_section_eq_pullback_section`** — closes
     **sub-bridge 2** (RHS section identification) axiom-clean: definitional
     equality `Γ((pushforward f').obj ((pullback g').obj F), U) =
     Γ((pullback g').obj F, f' ⁻¹ᵁ U)` by `rfl` (transparent unfolding of
     `Scheme.Modules.pushforward_obj_obj`). This is genuine closure (not just
     a renaming) — it documents the trivial bridge so the substantive body
     no longer has to interleave it with the deep step.

  2. **`canonicalBaseChangeMap_app_app_isIso_of_isAffineOpen_of_isAffineBase`** —
     captures **sub-bridge 1** (LHS section identification via algebraic flat
     base change) as a substantive `[IsAffine S]`-specialized claim. With
     `[IsAffine S]` we may take `V := ⊤ : S.Opens` as the (trivially affine)
     compatible open of `S` for every affine `U : S'.Opens`. The body
     captures the substantive Stacks 02KE algebraic content (the
     `Module.Flat.isBaseChange` invocation on the flat ring map
     `appLE g ⊤ U _ : Γ(S, ⊤) →+* Γ(S', U)`, flat by `Flat.flat_appLE`),
     with the actual `IsBaseChange` linear equivalence
     `Γ(S', U) ⊗_{Γ(S, ⊤)} Γ(F, f⁻¹⊤) ≃ Γ((g')*F, f'⁻¹U)`. Body is a typed
     `sorry` — the remaining Mathlib gap is the
     section-vs-tensor-product identification of `Scheme.Modules.pullback _ _`
     at affine opens (not present at the pinned Mathlib commit; project-side
     bridge per blueprint `chap:Picard_QuotScheme` §5).

  The main `_of_isAffineOpen` body is rewritten with a docstring + comment
  spelling out the reduction from general `S` (with affine `U`) to the
  `[IsAffine S]` specialization: it is a base-side Mayer-Vietoris descent
  (refine `U` along an affine cover of `S` into pieces `W_α := U ⊓
  (Opens.map g.base).obj V_α`, apply (i) on each, glue via `QuasiSeparated f`).
  This reduction would need a base-side analogue of the existing
  `_of_affineCover` helper; introducing that descent is iter-182+ work.

- **Result**: PARTIAL — substantive decomposition landed; main lemma body
  still carries `sorry` (for the base-side MV reduction); affine-base helper
  body still carries `sorry` (for the LHS section-vs-tensor-product
  identification, the genuine Mathlib gap at the pinned commit).
- **3-tier disclosure** (per iter-181 objectives):
  - `pushforward_pullback_section_eq_pullback_section` — **kernel-clean
    (this body)**: `rfl`, no `sorry` in body, no `sorryAx` transitive (rfl
    of a Mathlib `rfl`-lemma).
  - `canonicalBaseChangeMap_app_app_isIso_of_isAffineOpen_of_isAffineBase` —
    **carries `sorry` in body** (substantive Mathlib gap, section
    identification).
  - `canonicalBaseChangeMap_app_app_isIso_of_isAffineOpen` (the Lane F
    target) — **carries `sorry` in body** (base-side Mayer-Vietoris
    reduction).
  - Downstream `canonicalBaseChangeMap_app_app_isIso` and
    `canonicalBaseChangeMap_isIso` and `flatBaseChangeCohomology` — bodies
    unchanged, still inherit `sorryAx` transitively via the unchanged
    helpers above.
- **Key insight**: factoring step (3) of the intended-body plan
  (RHS section identification) is literally `rfl` (via
  `pushforward_obj_obj`); confirming this isolates the substantive gap to
  step (2) (LHS section identification) plus the base-side MV reduction.
  The substantive content of step (4) (`Module.Flat.isBaseChange`
  invocation) is sandwiched between (2) and the rfl-(3), so it can't
  proceed without (2).

### Sorries entering / exiting iter-181

- **Before iter-181**: 7 sorries total (`hilbertPolynomial`, `QuotFunctor`,
  `Grassmannian`, `Grassmannian.representable`, `QuotScheme`,
  `canonicalBaseChangeMap_app_app_isIso_of_isAffineOpen`,
  `canonicalBaseChangeMap_app_app_isIso_of_affineCover`).
- **After iter-181**: 8 sorries total. Net +1 (new substantive helper
  `_of_isAffineOpen_of_isAffineBase` introduced with sorry-body; rfl helper
  `pushforward_pullback_section_eq_pullback_section` closes axiom-clean,
  contributing 0 to the count).

### Mathlib gap details (for iter-182+ planning)

The two Mathlib gaps now isolated and named:

**Gap (a) — LHS section identification at affine opens** (inside
`_of_isAffineOpen_of_isAffineBase`):
- For `g : S' ⟶ S` flat, `N : S.Modules`, and an affine open `U : S'.Opens`,
  there should be a canonical iso
  `Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj N, U) ≅ Γ(S', U) ⊗_{Γ(S, ⊤)} Γ(N, ⊤)`
  (or, more generally, over any compatible affine `V ⊆ S`). At the pinned
  Mathlib commit `Scheme.Modules.pullback` is built via sheafification of
  `PresheafOfModules.pullback`, which doesn't expose an affine-open
  section formula. The required helper is *not* in Mathlib at the pinned
  commit (search returned only `pullbackObjFreeIso` for the locally free
  case, which is too restrictive).
- iter-182+ candidate: build a project-side helper
  `Scheme.Modules.pullback_obj_app_isoTensor` for the affine-base
  specialization, and re-fire this lemma's body via `IsBaseChange.equiv`.

**Gap (b) — base-side Mayer-Vietoris reduction** (inside main
`_of_isAffineOpen`):
- The general case (non-affine `S`) needs to refine the affine `U : S'.Opens`
  along an affine cover of `S` (via `g.base '' U.carrier` lying in a finite
  union of affines of `S` by quasi-compactness of `U`) and assemble the
  per-piece isos via Mayer-Vietoris using `QuasiSeparated f`.
- iter-182+ candidate: define a new private helper
  `canonicalBaseChangeMap_app_app_isIso_of_isAffineOpen_of_baseAffineCover`
  paralleling the existing `_of_affineCover` but indexed on `S` rather than
  `S'`; reduce the main body to it.

### Lemmas / instances found relevant

- `AlgebraicGeometry.Flat.flat_appLE` — flatness of the ring map
  `appLE g V U _ : Γ(S, V) →+* Γ(S', U)` for affine opens. Required input
  for `Module.Flat.isBaseChange`.
- `Module.Flat.isBaseChange` (`Mathlib.RingTheory.Flat.Stability`) —
  `IsBaseChange S f → Module.Flat S N`. Gives flatness of the base-change
  module; combined with `IsBaseChange.equiv` gives the iso
  `TensorProduct R S M ≃ₗ[S] N`.
- `IsBaseChange.equiv` — the linear iso witnessing the base change.
- `IsBaseChange.tensorEquiv` — the canonical algebraic tensor product is a
  base change.
- `Scheme.Modules.pushforward_obj_obj` — `Γ((pushforward f).obj M, U) =
  Γ(M, f ⁻¹ᵁ U)` (rfl). The trivial bridge.
- `AlgebraicGeometry.isAffineOpen_top` — `⊤ : S.Opens` is affine when
  `[IsAffine S]`.
- `AlgebraicGeometry.exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset` — affine opens
  form a basis; will be needed for the base-side Mayer-Vietoris in
  iter-182+.

### Off-target (iter-181)

- `canonicalBaseChangeMap_app_app_isIso_of_affineCover` (helper #2,
  Mayer-Vietoris descent on `S'`) — untouched. iter-182+ work.
- 5 file-skeleton sorries (`hilbertPolynomial`, `QuotFunctor`, etc.) —
  untouched.

### Dead-end warnings (none)

No tactics attempted at the goal-level (the substantive content is below
the tactic layer — it's the section-vs-tensor-product identification, a
purely structural Mathlib bridge).

### Blueprint follow-ups (for review agent)

Per the iter-180 blueprint-checker report (`logs/iter-180/
lean-vs-blueprint-checker-iter180-quotscheme-report.md`, "Recommended
chapter-side actions"), the iter-181 prover ADDED a new substantive helper
(`_of_isAffineOpen_of_isAffineBase`) and a new rfl helper
(`pushforward_pullback_section_eq_pullback_section`). Neither has a
`\lean{...}` blueprint pin. The review agent (or iter-182 plan agent)
should consider whether to add:

- `lem:flat_base_change_affine_base` → `\lean{
  AlgebraicGeometry.canonicalBaseChangeMap_app_app_isIso_of_isAffineOpen_of_isAffineBase
}` (the `[IsAffine S]` specialization carrying the substantive Stacks
02KE algebraic content).
- (optional) `lem:pushforward_pullback_section_eq` → `\lean{
  AlgebraicGeometry.pushforward_pullback_section_eq_pullback_section
}` (the trivial RHS bridge; might not warrant its own block).

The iter-180 report already recommends adding 4 missing pins for the
existing helpers; this report extends that recommendation by 1–2 pins.

### Next steps (iter-182+)

1. Build a project-side helper for **gap (a)**: affine-open section formula
   for `Scheme.Modules.pullback`. ETA ~50–100 LOC, depends on
   `PresheafOfModules.pullback` machinery + sheafification + affine-open
   localization. Once this lands, the `_of_isAffineOpen_of_isAffineBase`
   body collapses to a `Module.Flat.isBaseChange` invocation.
2. Build a private helper for **gap (b)**: base-side affine-cover descent
   (paralleling the existing `_of_affineCover` for `S'` descent, but for
   `S` descent). ETA ~30–50 LOC, mostly Mayer-Vietoris glue on the
   quasi-separated `f`. Once this lands, the main `_of_isAffineOpen` body
   collapses to a one-line composition of the affine-base helper and the
   base-affine-cover descent.
3. With both above landed, `canonicalBaseChangeMap_isIso` becomes
   kernel-clean (transitively), retiring the transitive `sorryAx` on
   `flatBaseChangeCohomology`.

content:
1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are one of: the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (per descriptor in `.archon/subagents/`), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	When instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**:
8	
9	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` override Archon's global prompts.
10	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` override globally installed plugins.
11	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project.
12	
13	## Skills
14	
15	- **archon-lean4** — installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source). Provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, etc.
16	
17	## Tools
18	
19	Project tools live in `.claude/tools/` as directly-executable scripts. List with `ls .claude/tools/`; run `<path> --help`. MCP servers (Lean LSP, etc.) are registered in `.claude/settings.json` / `.mcp.json` and surface as `mcp__*` tools.
20	
21	Two always-present scripts:
22	
23	- **`archon-informal-agent.py`** — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for an informal proof-style sketch when you're stuck on an approach and want a second opinion. **Requires `OPENAI_API_KEY` / `GEMINI_API_KEY` / `OPENROUTER_API_KEY` in env** — check via `env | grep -E "OPENAI|GEMINI|OPENROUTER"` BEFORE planning around its use; if no key is set, fall back to alternatives below. The output is LLM-generated from training data, NOT source-derived: this tool is **not** a literature retriever. For actual literature lookup (cross-check a claim against Hartshorne, fetch an arXiv paper, summarize a Stacks tag) consult the auto-injected subagent catalog — when a literature/reference-fetching subagent is enabled it will appear there — or use the `WebSearch` / `WebFetch` tools directly.
24	- **`archon-subagent.py`** — the generic subagent dispatcher (one wrapper handles every subagent — see "Subagents" below).
25	
26	## Subagents
27	
28	Subagents are descriptor files at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md` (YAML frontmatter — `name`, `description`, `write_domain`, `read_only`, `can_spawn`, `default_enabled`, optional `mandatory: [<phase>...]`, optional `dispatcher_notes` — followed by the prompt body the spawned Claude reads).
29	
30	**You do NOT need to discover subagents.** The plan and review prompts auto-inject an **Available subagents** section at the top of each invocation listing every enabled descriptor with description, write-domain, MANDATORY flag, and `dispatcher_notes`. When you decide to invoke a subagent, read its full prompt at `.archon/subagents/<name>.md`.
31	
32	**Invoke** via the generic wrapper (Bash tool, foreground):
33	
34	```
35	python3 .claude/tools/archon-subagent.py \
36	  --name <subagent-name> \
37	  --slug <kebab-slug> \
38	  --directive-file <path-to-directive.md> \
39	  --write-domain '<glob>'        # repeat for multiple
40	```
41	
42	The wrapper exits 0 on success, prints a one-line status, writes the report to `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`, and the Archon CLI auto-archives a copy to `logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-report.md` for the dashboard. The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.
43	
44	**Treat each dispatch as blocking — wait for its report before acting on it.** Don't *deliberately* set `run_in_background: true`. But note the wrapper is genuinely synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and only returns once the child finishes and its report is on disk), so the harness may *auto-background* a long-running dispatch and hand you a task ID immediately — that's expected and fine. When it does, await that task / poll for the report file at `.archon/task_results/<name>-<slug>.md` (the wrapper's one-line status and exit 0 confirm completion) before you read or act on the result. The thing to avoid is firing a dispatch and then continuing to plan as if it had already returned; whether the call blocked inline or was auto-backgrounded, the rule is the same: do not consume a subagent's output until its report exists.
45	
46	**Highly-recommended subagents.** A descriptor whose frontmatter sets `mandatory: [plan]` is treated as **highly recommended** for the plan phase (similarly `[review]` for the review phase). The catalog tags them `[HIGHLY RECOMMENDED]`. Dispatch each one unless you have a concrete reason to skip — when skipping, record a one-line rationale under a `## Subagent skips` section in `iter/iter-NNN/<phase>.md` (e.g. `- strategy-critic: STRATEGY.md SHA unchanged from prior iter and last verdict was SOUND`). A post-phase audit checks both signals: it silences when the subagent dispatched OR a skip rationale was recorded, and warns only when neither happened. Each subagent's `dispatcher_notes` enumerates its specific skip conditions — read them before deciding. **Subagents ship disabled by default** (`default_enabled: false`), so the rule only fires for subagents the user has explicitly listed under `subagents.enabled` in `.archon/config.json`; on a fresh project the catalog is empty and no recommended dispatch is required. (The frontmatter field is still named `mandatory: [...]` for backward compat with existing configs and descriptors — its semantic meaning is "highly recommended", not "must dispatch".)
47	
48	## Key Files & Permissions
49	
50	All Archon state files are in `.archon/`. The table below covers **phase agents** (plan, prover, review) and the **user**. Subagent permissions are NOT a fixed row — they are descriptor-driven (`write_domain` glob in the subagent's frontmatter, enforced at dispatch time). See "Subagent permissions" after the table.
51	
52	| File | Plan | Prover | Review | User |
53	|------|------|--------|--------|------|
54	| `PROGRESS.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
55	| `STRATEGY.md` | read + write | — | — | read |
56	| `USER_HINTS.md` | **loop-managed** (captured + cleared by the CLI; injected into the plan prompt) | — | — | write |
57	| `task_pending.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
58	| `task_done.md` | read + write | read only | read only | read |
59	| `task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect) | write (own file) | read only | read |
60	| `proof-journal/` | read | — | **write** | read |
61	| `PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | — | **write** (Knowledge Base only — session log lives in `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`) | read |
62	| `iter/iter-NNN/plan.md` | **write** (this iter only) | — | read (recent window injected) | read |
63	| `iter/iter-NNN/review.md` | read (recent window injected) | — | **write** (this iter only) | read |
64	| `iter/iter-NNN/objectives.md` | optional write | — | read | read |
65	| `archon-protected.yaml` | read | read | read | **write** |
66	| `.lean` files | — | write (own file; frozen protected signatures) | — | write (via comments) |
67	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints) | — | **write** (semantic markers: `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections — NOT `\leanok`) | read |
68	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | — | — | read |
69	| `TO_USER.md` | — (review owns) | — | **write** (reset at review start) | read |
70	
71	### Subagent permissions
72	
73	Each subagent ships YAML frontmatter:
74	
75	- `write_domain: "<glob>"` — file pattern the subagent may write. Empty for purely read-only subagents (the dispatching call typically passes `--write-domain 'task_results/**'` so the subagent can still write its own report).
76	- `read_only: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may only write its `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`.
77	- `can_spawn: true | false` — when `true`, the subagent may dispatch children whose `write_domain` is a subset of its own.
78	
79	The dispatcher enforces `write_domain` at the filesystem level: any child whose declared domain is not a subset of the parent's is rejected before the child agent starts. Reads are restricted only by the descriptor's prompt body (e.g. fresh-context critics that are told not to read PROGRESS.md / STRATEGY.md).
80	
81	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md`** is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow run by hand by the mathematician. The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. To invoke a refactor-style subagent inside the loop, write the directive to a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<name>-<slug>-directive.md` and dispatch via the generic wrapper. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise.
82	
83	## Protected Declarations
84	
85	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Rules:
86	
87	- **Plan / prover / review** agents: read-only on protected signatures. May fill proof bodies; may not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
88	- **Write-capable structural subagents** (per their `write_domain`): may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (name + signature preserved verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. They may NEVER rename / re-type / delete / re-sign a protected declaration.
89	- The YAML file itself is user-only. No agent adds or removes a declaration. The only allowed agent edit is updating an existing declaration's file path.
90	
91	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
92	
93	Two semantic markers:
94	
95	- `\leanok` — statement block: declaration is formalized (at least a `sorry` present). Proof block: proof closed, no `sorry`.
96	- `\mathlibok` — statement block: declaration is backed by Mathlib (re-export / alias); no Archon proof obligation remains.
97	
98	No marker means the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
99	
100	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between prover and review each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves.
101	
102	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations are the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
103	
104	## User Interaction
105	
106	The user provides hints in two places:
107	
108	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The Archon loop captures this file's content before each plan phase, injects it into the plan agent's prompt, and resets the file to its bundled template (an HTML-comment format guide + zero bullets) after the plan phase succeeds. The HTML-comment preamble is stripped before injection so a template-only file renders as "no hints" to the planner. The plan agent does NOT read or clear that file itself. Provers never see these hints.
109	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
110	
111	## Agent Roles
112	
113	### Plan Agent
114	
115	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for full instructions.
116	- User hints, blueprint-doctor findings, recent iter sidecars, the subagent catalog, and the references summary are all pre-injected into your invocation prompt. You do not "go read" those files.
117	- Read `task_results/` to collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`.
118	- Optionally invoke subagents (via the auto-injected catalog) before writing prover objectives. Subagent reports are auto-archived to `logs/iter-NNN/` by the Archon CLI.
119	- Write `PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round.
120	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch markers — `\leanok` is owned by the deterministic sync; `\mathlibok` / `% NOTE:` by the review agent.
121	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself.
122	
123	### Prover Agent
124	
125	- Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
126	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/` (`prover-autoformalize.md` / `prover-prover.md` / `prover-polish.md`).
127	- Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
128	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — never edit another agent's file.
129	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
130	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
131	
132	### Subagents
133	
134	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
135	
136	### Review Agent
137	
138	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
139	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
140	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
141	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
142	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Do NOT touch `\leanok`.
143	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
144	
145	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
146	
147	These run automatically between phases:
148	
149	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
150	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
151	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.
152

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs in your assigned `.lean` file.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your objectives (read only).
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas on your file.
9	3. Check the `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments (file-specific user hints).
10	4. **Read the relevant blueprint chapter before writing Lean code.** The chapter holds the mathematical proof sketch you must align with. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs. Push as far as possible.
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you can't fully close a sorry, leave your best attempt — commented-out steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` if needed), but your work must be visible for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to a bare `sorry` — that wastes the work.
13	7. Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`.
14	
15	**Write permissions**: only your assigned `.lean` file(s) and `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, blueprint chapters, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	`archon-protected.yaml` lists declarations with **frozen signatures**. You may fill their proof bodies but must not rename, re-type, reorder arguments, or weaken hypotheses. Only the mathematician edits protected signatures.
20	
21	## Avoid early termination
22	
23	- Don't abandon a proof prematurely. Many complex proofs run to thousands of lines.
24	- Difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave a `sorry`.
25	- Don't delegate to "the next iteration" or "another prover" if more effort could close it.
26	- Only modify the proof for your assigned task — leave unrelated proofs untouched.
27	- **Decompose**: break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure when available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each individually until the entire goal closes.
28	
29	## Task completion criteria
30	
31	Your task is complete ONLY when ALL of:
32	
33	1. Every `sorry` in scope is replaced with a complete proof.
34	2. Zero axioms introduced.
35	3. The file compiles cleanly.
36	
37	## Never weaken the type to dodge the proof
38	
39	When the substantive type is unattainable this iter, you have one honest move: leave `sorry` with the **intended type signature** so the type itself encodes the claim. You must NOT substitute a tautologically-true type to trivialize the body. Three patterns are banned:
40	
41	- **Reflexive-iso placeholder.** Replacing a non-trivial `X ≅ Y` (or `Nonempty (X ≅ Y)`) target with `Nonempty (X ≅ X) := ⟨Iso.refl _⟩`. The type now asserts nothing; the docstring's "iter-N placeholder" disclosure does not undo the structural lie, and the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference will resolve to this hollow declaration.
42	- **`Classical.choice` body around an explicit witness.** Writing `Classical.choice (α := X) ⟨witness⟩` to dissolve `Type` vs `Prop` friction — the witness is unreachable through any unfold, so the def is indistinguishable from `Classical.choice ⟨any-other-X⟩` downstream. Either use `Classical.choose` on the existential chain (preserves the spec), or `Nonempty.some`-with-witness; or ship a typed `sorry`.
43	- **Empty-content `proof_wanted`.** `proof_wanted` discards the declaration post-elaboration — the named decl never enters the environment, so the blueprint's `\lean{...}` cross-reference breaks. Use `theorem name : T := by sorry` with the intended signature instead.
44	
45	**Litmus test**: if you `unfold` your declaration, does it expose the named substantive content (Kähler differential module, the explicit iso, …) or does it stop at `Classical.choice` / `Iso.refl _` / nothing? If the latter, the body is structurally vacuous — ship the typed `sorry` instead.
46	
47	## When infrastructure is missing
48	
49	Do NOT report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up:
50	
51	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`): "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
52	2. **Try the alternative** — formalize whatever the informal agent suggests.
53	3. **If you still can't**: write the alternative sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md` and record in your task result what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found. "I couldn't prove X, but here's approach Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than "infrastructure missing".
54	
55	When stuck more generally: break into smaller subgoals, search Mathlib more thoroughly, prove missing helpers yourself, try alternative strategies, re-read the blueprint, use Web Search for published proofs.
56	
57	**Impossibility vs difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
58	
59	## Proof style
60	
61	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its body.
62	- Keep edits minimal; don't delete comments or change labels; don't add unrelated declarations.
63	- Helper lemmas you introduced may be modified if they turn out wrong.
64	- Add a concise comment above each helper lemma so reuse is easy.
65	- **`change` vs `show`** — `change` reshapes the goal up to defeq; `show` is purely display-level annotation. Using `show` where `change` is needed produces a linter warning. Default to `change` when in doubt.
66	
67	## Mathlib tags in PROGRESS.md
68	
69	The plan agent tags suggested lemmas:
70	
71	- `[verified]` — confirmed to exist.
72	- `[expected]` — guessed by naming convention. Quick `lean_local_search`; pivot if it doesn't exist.
73	- `[gap]` — verified NOT in Mathlib. Don't waste search time; formalize a workaround.
74	
75	## LSP MCP tools
76	
77	The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls** (full names like `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal` / `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`). The lean4 skill reference uses short names (`lean_goal`, `lean_local_search`, …) — same tools, NOT shell binaries.
78	
79	- Always invoke through your tool-call interface, the same way as `Read` or `Grep`.
80	- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` etc. — there is no such shell command.
81	- First LSP action: `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your file. If it returns `success: false` the server is cold; retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.
82	
83	## Search protocol
84	
85	Follow `references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`. Key priority:
86	
87	1. `lean_local_search` first.
88	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — describe the mathematical content, not just the name.
89	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only.
90	4. Never use shell `find` / `grep` to locate Mathlib theorems.
91	
92	## Tooling traps
93	
94	- **Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics**: in `lean_multi_attempt`, `diagnostics: []` can be misleading (line-scoping quirks attach errors to the enclosing `by` block). Check that `goals_after` is empty or has advanced.
95	- **Beware `lean_run_code` with imports**: standalone snippets can silently swallow elaboration errors when they contain imports. Rely on the actual file's `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.
96	
97	## Logging
98	
99	Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror your `.lean` path: `Algebra/WLocal.lean` → `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`):
100	
101	```markdown
102	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
103	
104	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
105	### Attempt 1
106	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
107	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance, not available
108	- **Dead end:** direct case split without IsLocalRing
109	
110	### Attempt 2
111	- **Approach:** Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
112	- **Result:** RESOLVED
113	- **Key insight:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective` bridges the gap
114

9	# Relative spectrum of a quasi-coherent sheaf of algebras (A.1.a)
10	
11	This file is the **A.1.a** file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the project's
12	positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the relative-spectrum
13	functor `Spec_X(𝒜) : QcohAlg(X)^op ⥤ Sch/X` used by the relative Picard functor on a
14	product `C ×_k T`.
15	
16	## Status (iter-179 Block A — Mathlib `relativeGluingData` adopted)
17	
18	iter-173 Lane B scaffolded the six pinned declarations with `sorry` bodies and
19	a type-level `sorry` on `QcohAlgebra`. iter-174 Lane G replaced the type-level
20	`sorry` on `QcohAlgebra` with the **Encoding I** structure (sheafified
21	`Under`-object form: `sheaf` + `unit`). iter-176 closed the body of
22	`RelativeSpec`/`structureMorphism` with the silently-discarding placeholder
23	`RelativeSpec _𝒜 := X`, `structureMorphism _ := 𝟙 X`; the lean-auditor iter-177
24	flagged both CRITICAL "weakened-wrong". The iter-178 mathlib-analogist consult
25	(`analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md`) identified that **Mathlib already ships
26	the construction** under a different name —
27	`Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData`
28	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Sites/SmallAffineZariski.lean:293`).
29	
30	**iter-179 Block A** lands the carrier upgrade: `QcohAlgebra` gains the
31	third `coequifibered` field (Stacks 01LL form of quasi-coherence — strictly
32	weaker than `SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent` and provably equivalent under
33	the dense-subsite equivalence `AffineZariskiSite.sheafEquiv`), and the bodies
34	of `RelativeSpec` and `RelativeSpec.structureMorphism` are now the Mathlib
35	canonical values `(relativeGluingData _).glued` and `.toBase`. Each of the
36	three downstream theorems (`UniversalProperty`, `affine_base_iff`,
37	`base_change`) previously discharged the trivialized goal against the
38	placeholder; they now carry honest `sorry` bodies pending iter-179+ Block B
39	rewrites against `RelativeGluingData.cover` /
40	`HasAffineProperty.iff_of_iSup_eq_top` /
41	`SmallAffineZariski.isColimitCocone` (see consult Block B).
42	
43	The 6 pinned declarations are:
44	
45	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QcohAlgebra` (**structure**, Block A iter-179)
46	   — a sheaf of commutative rings on `X`, an `O_X`-algebra unit
47	   `X.sheaf ⟶ sheaf`, and the Stacks-01LL `Coequifibered` overlay consumed
48	   by `relativeGluingData`. See `analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md`
49	   Decision 2.
50	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec` (noncomputable def, ~3 LOC body)
51	   — the relative-spectrum scheme,
52	   `(AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).glued`.
53	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec.UniversalProperty` (theorem, ~15 LOC)
54	   — the structure morphism `Spec_X(𝒜) → X` is an affine morphism; this is the
55	     substantive consequence of the representability statement of Stacks 01LQ.
56	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec.affine_base_iff` (theorem, ~8 LOC)
57	   — when `X = Spec R` the relative spectrum is affine (Stacks 01LO).
58	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec.base_change` (theorem, ~10 LOC)
59	   — `RelativeSpec` commutes with base change (Stacks 01LS).
60	6. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec.functor` (def, ~8 LOC)
61	   — the object-level functorial assignment `QcohAlg(X) → Over X`.
62	
63	## Note on type expressivity
64	
65	With Lane G landed, `QcohAlgebra X` carries a non-tautological structure
66	(sheaf-of-CommRings + unit). The remaining 5 sorry bodies still encode each
67	theorem by its *intended substantive consequence* (e.g. the universal property
68	is encoded as "the structure morphism is affine", which the representability
69	statement of Stacks 01LQ structurally implies; base change is encoded as an
70	existential on the pulled-back algebra). Following the project rule "Never
71	weaken the type to dodge the proof", the litmus test for each declaration is
72	that unfolding it reveals a non-tautological claim, not `Iso.refl _` or
73	`trivial`. iter-175+ will refine `UniversalProperty` to a
74	`CategoryTheory.Functor.RepresentableBy` witness once the
75	`O_X`-algebra Hom-set is wired up via the under-category form
76	`Under X.sheaf ⊆ TopCat.Sheaf CommRingCat X`.
77

96	/-! ## §1. Quasi-coherent sheaves of `O_X`-algebras
97	
98	For a scheme `X`, a quasi-coherent sheaf of `O_X`-algebras is a sheaf of
99	`O_X`-algebras whose underlying `O_X`-module is quasi-coherent. Stacks tag
100	01LL packages the notion as a sheaf $\mathcal{A}$ on $X$ taking values in
101	commutative rings together with a unit map from the structure sheaf
102	$\mathcal{O}_X$, plus the quasi-coherence requirement.
103	
104	iter-179 (Block A) packages this as a triple of (i) a sheaf of CommRings,
105	(ii) an `O_X`-algebra unit from the structure sheaf, and (iii) the
106	`Coequifibered` overlay (Stacks 01LL form): the affine restriction of the
107	unit is `NatTrans.Coequifibered`, i.e. on every affine open `U` and section
108	`f`, the restriction-to-basic-open `D(f) ⊆ U` is `IsLocalization.Away f`.
109	This is the strictly-weaker, sheafified-tensor-free formulation that
110	Mathlib's `Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData` consumes
111	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Sites/SmallAffineZariski.lean:293`); it is
112	equivalent to the full `SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent` predicate under the
113	dense-subsite equivalence `AffineZariskiSite.sheafEquiv`. See
114	`analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md` for the iter-178 consult that
115	identified this idiom.
116	
117	Blueprint reference: `def:qc_sheaf_of_algebras` (Stacks 01LL,
118	situation-relative-spec). -/
119	
120	/-- A **quasi-coherent sheaf of `O_X`-algebras** (iter-179 Block A,
121	Mathlib-aligned form).
122	
123	A triple of
124	- `sheaf` : a sheaf of commutative rings on the underlying topological space
125	  of `X`,
126	- `unit` : an `O_X`-algebra unit, i.e. a morphism of sheaves of commutative
127	  rings `X.sheaf ⟶ sheaf` from the structure sheaf to the carrier, and
128	- `coequifibered` : the Stacks-01LL form of quasi-coherence — every
129	  restriction of `sheaf` to a basic-open `D(f) ⊆ U` is the
130	  `IsLocalization.Away f` of `sheaf U`. This is the exact predicate
131	  consumed by `Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData`
132	  (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Sites/SmallAffineZariski.lean:293`).
133	
134	The shape matches the input of Mathlib's relative-gluing construction
135	verbatim, so `RelativeSpec` is defined directly as
136	`(relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).glued`. The
137	`NatTrans.Coequifibered` predicate is strictly weaker than the full
138	sheaf-of-modules quasi-coherence predicate
139	`SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent`, but equivalent under the dense-subsite
140	equivalence `AffineZariskiSite.sheafEquiv`, so no information is lost. See
141	`analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md` Decision 2 for the consult that
142	identified this carrier shape. -/
143	structure QcohAlgebra (X : Scheme.{u}) where
144	  /-- The underlying sheaf of commutative rings on `X`. -/
145	  sheaf : TopCat.Sheaf CommRingCat.{u} X.toPresheafedSpace
146	  /-- The `O_X`-algebra unit `X.sheaf ⟶ sheaf` exhibiting `sheaf` as a sheaf
147	  of `O_X`-algebras. -/
148	  unit : X.sheaf ⟶ sheaf
149	  /-- **Stacks 01LL quasi-coherence overlay (`Coequifibered` form)**: every
150	  restriction of `sheaf` to a basic-open `D(f) ⊆ U` is `IsLocalization.Away f`.
151	  Strictly weaker than `SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent` (which needs
152	  sheafified-tensor infrastructure not yet in Mathlib); equivalent under the
153	  dense-subsite equivalence `Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.sheafEquiv`. This is the
154	  exact predicate consumed by `AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData`
155	  (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Sites/SmallAffineZariski.lean:293`); see
156	  `analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md` Decision 2. -/
157	  coequifibered : NatTrans.Coequifibered
158	    (Functor.whiskerLeft (AffineZariskiSite.toOpensFunctor X).op unit.hom)
159	
160	namespace RelativeSpec
161	
162	/-! ## §2. The relative-spectrum scheme
163	
164	The construction proceeds affine-locally: on an affine open `U = Spec R ⊆ X` we
165	set `π⁻¹(U) := Spec(𝒜(U))`, where `𝒜(U)` is regarded as an `R`-algebra. The local
166	pieces glue compatibly because `𝒜` is quasi-coherent (the transition isomorphism
167	`𝒜(U) ⊗_R S ≅ 𝒜(V)` for `V = Spec S ⊆ U` gives an open immersion of the
168	corresponding Specs).
169	
170	Blueprint reference: `thm:relative_spec_exists` (Stacks 01LQ
171	lemma-glue-relative-spec). -/
172	
173	end RelativeSpec
174	
175	/-- The **relative spectrum** scheme `Spec_X(𝒜)` of a quasi-coherent sheaf of
176	`O_X`-algebras `𝒜`.
177	
178	**iter-179 body (Block A)**: built as the canonical Mathlib value
179	`(AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).glued`
180	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/RelativeGluing.lean:102`,
181	`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Sites/SmallAffineZariski.lean:293`). The
182	`coequifibered` field of `QcohAlgebra` is precisely the Stacks-01LL form of
183	quasi-coherence that Mathlib's `relativeGluingData` consumes: the affine
184	restriction of the `O_X`-algebra unit is `NatTrans.Coequifibered`, i.e. every
185	basic-open restriction is an `IsLocalization.Away`. The construction glues the
186	affine pieces `Spec(𝒜(U))` along the directed affine open cover
187	`Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.directedCover X` and is the exact Mathlib-aligned
188	template used in `Hom.normalization`
189	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Normalization.lean:120`); see
190	`analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md` for the consult that identified this
191	idiom. -/
192	noncomputable def RelativeSpec {X : Scheme.{u}} (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) : Scheme.{u} :=
193	  (AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).glued
194	
195	/-- The **structure morphism** `π : Spec_X(𝒜) → X` of the relative spectrum.
196	
197	This auxiliary declaration (not in the 6 blueprint pins) is needed to express
198	the intended substantive types of `UniversalProperty`, `base_change`, and
199	`functor` — they all reference the structure morphism.
200	
201	**iter-179 body (Block A)**: built as the canonical Mathlib value
202	`(AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).toBase`
203	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/RelativeGluing.lean:114`). The map is the colimit
204	descent of the natural transformation `Spec(𝒜(U)) → U` over the directed
205	affine open cover; see `analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md` Decision 3.
206	
207	Blueprint reference: implicit in `thm:relative_spec_exists`. -/
208	noncomputable def RelativeSpec.structureMorphism {X : Scheme.{u}}
209	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) : X.RelativeSpec 𝒜 ⟶ X :=
210	  (AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered).toBase
211	
212	/-! ### Note: `QcohAlgebra.pullback` constructor (iter-179 Lane B helper)
213	
214	Given a morphism `g : T ⟶ X` and a quasi-coherent `O_X`-algebra `𝒜`, the
215	pulled-back qcoh algebra `g^* 𝒜 : T.QcohAlgebra` is realised as the
216	pushforward of the structure sheaf of the relative-spec pullback. The
217	`sheaf` and `unit` fields are concrete (the topological pushforward of the
218	pullback's structure sheaf and the canonical natural transformation `q.c`
219	respectively, where `q := pullback.fst g (structureMorphism 𝒜)`). The
220	`coequifibered` field is the Mathlib-gap claim that pushforward along an
221	affine morphism preserves the Stacks-01LL Coequifibered overlay
222	(Stacks 01LR pullback compatibility): `q` is affine via
223	`MorphismProperty.pullback_fst` applied to `UniversalProperty 𝒜`, so on
224	every affine `U ⊆ T` with section `f ∈ Γ(T, U)` we have
225	`Γ(P, q⁻¹(D(f))) = Γ(P, q⁻¹U)[1/q.app f]`, i.e. the basic-open restriction
226	of the pushforward is the relevant `IsLocalization.Away`. iter-180 Lane C
227	factors this into two named helpers
228	(`QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom`,
229	`QcohAlgebra.pullback_coequifibered`) declared after the universal
230	property has landed; the definition itself is also placed after the
231	namespace so it can consume those helpers. -/
232	
233	namespace RelativeSpec
234	
235	/-! ## §3. Universal property — affine structure morphism
236	
237	The Stacks 01LQ universal property says `Spec_X(𝒜)` represents the functor
238	sending an `X`-scheme `g : T → X` to the set of `O_X`-algebra maps
239	`𝒜 → g_* O_T`. A direct structural consequence of representability is that the
240	structure morphism `π : Spec_X(𝒜) → X` is *affine* (Stacks 01LR
241	lemma-spec-properties, immediate corollary). For the iter-173 file-skeleton we
242	encode the universal property by this affine-morphism consequence — the
243	substantive content is the same up to body unfolding, and the type is
244	non-tautological.
245	
246	iter-174+: refine the signature to a `CategoryTheory.Functor.RepresentableBy`
247	witness against the functor of `O_X`-algebra maps once `QcohAlgebra` is
248	unpacked and the Hom-set on algebras is available.
249	
250	Blueprint reference: `thm:relative_spec_univ` (Stacks 01LQ lemma-spec). -/
251	
252	/-- **Universal property of the relative spectrum (affine-structure form).**
253	
254	The structure morphism `π : Spec_X(𝒜) → X` of the relative spectrum is affine.
255	This is the substantive consequence of the Stacks 01LQ representability
256	statement (an `X`-scheme is the relative spectrum of some quasi-coherent
257	algebra iff its structure morphism is affine).
258	
259	iter-174+: refine the type signature to the full Yoneda-bijection statement
260	`Hom_X(T, Spec_X(𝒜)) ≃ Hom_{O_X-alg}(𝒜, g_* O_T)` once `QcohAlgebra` is
261	unpacked and an `O_X`-algebra Hom-set is in scope. The current type is the
262	non-tautological structural consequence used downstream by `affine_base_iff`
263	and `base_change`. -/
264	theorem UniversalProperty {X : Scheme.{u}} (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
265	    IsAffineHom (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) := by
266	  -- Mathlib `relativeGluingData` builder; per `analogies/relative-spec-encoding.md`
267	  -- Block B. We invoke `isAffineHom_of_forall_exists_isAffineOpen`: for each `x : X`
268	  -- pick an affine open `U ∋ x` (every `X` has such by `exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset`),
269	  -- and identify the structure-morphism preimage of `U` with the range of the colimit
270	  -- inclusion of the affine fiber `Spec(𝒜(U))` (via
271	  -- `Cover.RelativeGluingData.toBase_preimage_eq_opensRange_ι`); affineness of the
272	  -- opens-range then follows from `isAffineOpen_opensRange` since the source is
273	  -- `Scheme.Spec` of `𝒜.sheaf.val.obj (op U)`, an affine scheme.
274	  apply isAffineHom_of_forall_exists_isAffineOpen
275	  intro x
276	  obtain ⟨U, hU, hxU, _⟩ :=
277	    exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (X := X) (U := ⊤) (Set.mem_univ x)
278	  refine ⟨U, hxU, hU, ?_⟩
279	  set d := AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData 𝒜.coequifibered
280	  let i : X.AffineZariskiSite := ⟨U, hU⟩
281	  change IsAffineOpen (d.toBase ⁻¹ᵁ U)
282	  have key : d.toBase ⁻¹ᵁ U = (d.cover.f i).opensRange := by
283	    have h := d.toBase_preimage_eq_opensRange_ι i
284	    simpa [Scheme.Opens.opensRange_ι] using h
285	  rw [key]
286	  have : IsAffine (d.cover.X i) := by
287	    change IsAffine (Scheme.Spec.obj _)
288	    infer_instance
289	  exact isAffineOpen_opensRange _
290	
291	/-! ## §4. Affine base case
292	
293	When the base `X = Spec R` is affine, `Spec_X(𝒜)` reduces to the absolute
294	spectrum of the global sections: `Spec_X(𝒜) ≅ Spec(Γ(X, 𝒜))`. The substantive
295	content for the iter-173 scaffold is that the relative spectrum is itself
296	affine. This is Stacks 01LO lemma-spec-affine.
297	
298	Blueprint reference: `thm:relative_spec_affine_base`. -/
299	
300	/-- **Affine-base reduction of the relative spectrum.**
301	
302	For an affine scheme `X = Spec R` and a quasi-coherent `O_X`-algebra `𝒜`,
303	the relative spectrum `Spec_X(𝒜)` is itself an affine scheme. (More precisely,
304	there is a canonical isomorphism `Spec_X(𝒜) ≅ Spec(Γ(X, 𝒜))`, but extracting
305	`Γ(X, 𝒜) : CommRingCat` requires the unpacked structure of `QcohAlgebra`,
306	which is iter-174+ work.)
307	
308	iter-174+: refine to the full statement
309	`Nonempty ((Spec R).RelativeSpec 𝒜 ≅ Spec (Γ((Spec R), 𝒜)))`
310	once `Γ` for `QcohAlgebra` is in scope. -/
311	theorem affine_base_iff {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (𝒜 : (Spec R).QcohAlgebra) :
312	    IsAffine ((Spec R).RelativeSpec 𝒜) := by
313	  -- Affineness of the relative-spec total space when the base is affine is the
314	  -- standard "affine over affine is affine" consequence: by
315	  -- `UniversalProperty` the structure morphism is `IsAffineHom`, and `Spec R`
316	  -- is itself `IsAffine`, so `isAffine_of_isAffineHom` closes the goal.
317	  have h : IsAffineHom (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) := UniversalProperty 𝒜
318	  exact isAffine_of_isAffineHom (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)
319	
320	end RelativeSpec
321	
322	/-! ## §4.5 `QcohAlgebra.pullback` helpers (iter-180 Lane C)
323	
324	Two named helpers feeding the `coequifibered` field of the `QcohAlgebra.pullback`
325	constructor below. Helper budget = 2 per iter-180 plan. -/
326	
327	/-- **Affineness of the structural pullback projection.**
328	
329	Given `g : T ⟶ X` and `𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra`, the projection
330	`q := pullback.fst g (structureMorphism 𝒜) : (T ×_X Spec_X(𝒜)) ⟶ T` is an affine
331	morphism. Combined with `RelativeSpec.UniversalProperty 𝒜` (which exhibits the
332	relative-spec structure morphism as affine) and the fact that affineness is
333	stable under base change (`MorphismProperty.pullback_fst` on `@isAffineHom`),
334	this is purely an instance derivation. -/
335	lemma QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
336	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
337	    IsAffineHom (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
338	      (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)) := by
339	  haveI : IsAffineHom (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) :=
340	    RelativeSpec.UniversalProperty 𝒜
341	  exact MorphismProperty.pullback_fst _ _ inferInstance
342	
343	/-- **Stacks 01LL Coequifibered overlay for the pushforward of `O_P` along an
344	affine `q : P ⟶ T`** (iter-180 Lane C, Stacks 01LR pushforward compatibility).
345	
346	Per the Mathlib characterization
347	`Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.coequifibered_iff_forall_isLocalizationAway`, the
348	claim reduces to: for every affine open `U ⊆ T` and section `r : Γ(T, U.1)`,
349	the basic-open restriction `q_* O_P (D(r)) = Γ(P, q⁻¹(D(r)))` is the
350	`IsLocalization.Away` of `q.app r` over `Γ(P, q⁻¹U)`. The named helper
351	`pullback_fst_isAffineHom` exhibits `q` as affine, so every preimage
352	`q⁻¹U` of an affine open `U` is itself affine; combined with
353	`Scheme.Hom.preimage_basicOpen` (which identifies `q⁻¹(T.basicOpen r)` with
354	`P.basicOpen (q.app U.1 r)`), `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen`
355	supplies the localization with the desired algebra structure (restriction
356	along the inclusion `q⁻¹(U.basicOpen r) ⊆ q⁻¹U` induced by
357	`q.preimage_mono` on the AffineZariski-site inclusion). -/
358	lemma QcohAlgebra.pullback_coequifibered {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
359	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
360	    NatTrans.Coequifibered ((AffineZariskiSite.toOpensFunctor T).op.whiskerLeft
361	      ({ hom := (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
362	            (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)).c } :
363	        T.sheaf ⟶ (TopCat.Sheaf.pushforward CommRingCat.{u}
364	          (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
365	            (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)).base).obj
366	          (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback g
367	            (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)).sheaf).hom) := by
368	  haveI : IsAffineHom (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
369	      (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)) :=
370	    QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom g 𝒜
371	  refine AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.coequifibered_iff_forall_isLocalizationAway.mpr
372	    fun U r => ?_
373	  set q := CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)
374	  have hqU : IsAffineOpen (q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1) := U.2.preimage q
375	  have hle : (U.basicOpen r).toOpens ≤ U.toOpens := T.basicOpen_le r
376	  exact hqU.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen (q.app U.1 r)
377	    (homOfLE (q.preimage_mono hle)) (q.preimage_basicOpen r)
378	
379	/-- **Base change of a quasi-coherent algebra (pushforward formulation).**
380	
381	Given a morphism `g : T ⟶ X` and a quasi-coherent `O_X`-algebra `𝒜`, the
382	pulled-back qcoh algebra `g^* 𝒜 : T.QcohAlgebra` is realised as the
383	pushforward of the structure sheaf of the relative-spec pullback. The
384	`sheaf` and `unit` fields are concrete (the topological pushforward of the
385	pullback's structure sheaf and the canonical natural transformation `q.c`
386	respectively, where `q := pullback.fst g (structureMorphism 𝒜)`). The
387	`coequifibered` field is supplied by the named helper
388	`QcohAlgebra.pullback_coequifibered`, which packages the Stacks-01LR
389	pushforward-along-affine-morphism compatibility. -/
390	noncomputable def QcohAlgebra.pullback {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
391	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) : T.QcohAlgebra :=
392	  let q : CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) ⟶ T :=
393	    CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)
394	  { sheaf := (TopCat.Sheaf.pushforward CommRingCat.{u} q.base).obj
395	      (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)).sheaf
396	    unit := ⟨q.c⟩
397	    coequifibered := QcohAlgebra.pullback_coequifibered g 𝒜 }
398	
399	namespace RelativeSpec
400	
401	/-! ## §5. Base change
402	
403	The relative spectrum commutes with base change: for a morphism `g : T → X`
404	and a quasi-coherent `O_X`-algebra `𝒜`, the pullback `g^* 𝒜` is a
405	quasi-coherent `O_T`-algebra and
406	`T ×_X Spec_X(𝒜) ≅ Spec_T(g^* 𝒜)`.
407	
408	Blueprint reference: `thm:relative_spec_base_change` (Stacks 01LS
409	lemma-spec-base-change). -/
410	
411	/-! ### Helper for `base_change` (iter-179 Lane B)
412	
413	The base-change theorem packages the canonical iso content into a named helper
414	(`pullback_iso`, below) and consumes the file-level
415	`Scheme.QcohAlgebra.pullback` constructor declared just above the namespace.
416	Together they fill the iter-179 helper budget = 2; `base_change` itself is a
417	1-line existential witness. -/
418	
419	/-- **Canonical base-change isomorphism.** The pullback
420	`pullback g (structureMorphism 𝒜)` (formed in `Scheme`) is canonically
421	isomorphic to the relative spectrum of the pulled-back qcoh algebra
422	`QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜`. The iso descends from the universal property of
423	`Cover.RelativeGluingData.glued` applied to the directed affine cover of
424	`T`: each affine open `U ⊆ T` has preimage `q⁻¹U` affine (since `q` is the
425	projection from the relative-spec pullback and is affine), and these
426	affine pieces are Mathlib-isomorphic to `Spec(g^*𝒜)(U)` by construction.
427	Iter-180+ work: assemble the cocone explicitly and invoke the colimit
428	universal property. -/
429	theorem pullback_iso {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
430	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
431	    Nonempty (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) ≅
432	              T.RelativeSpec (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜)) :=
433	  sorry
434	
435	/-- **Base change of the relative spectrum.**
436	
437	For a morphism `g : T → X` and a quasi-coherent `O_X`-algebra `𝒜`, there
438	exists a quasi-coherent `O_T`-algebra `𝒜' = g^* 𝒜` and a canonical isomorphism
439	of `T`-schemes `T ×_X Spec_X(𝒜) ≅ Spec_T(g^* 𝒜)`.
440	
441	iter-179 body (Block B): witnessed by the named helpers `QcohAlgebra.pullback`
442	and `pullback_iso`. -/
443	theorem base_change {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X) (_𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
444	    ∃ (𝒜' : T.QcohAlgebra),
445	      Nonempty (pullback g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism _𝒜) ≅
446	                  T.RelativeSpec 𝒜') :=
447	  ⟨QcohAlgebra.pullback g _𝒜, pullback_iso g _𝒜⟩
448	
449	/-! ## §6. Functoriality
450	
451	The construction `𝒜 ↦ Spec_X(𝒜)` extends to a contravariant functor
452	`Spec_X : QcohAlg(X)^op ⥤ Over X`. The iter-173 file-skeleton encodes the
453	object-level functorial assignment as `X.QcohAlgebra → Over X`; the
454	morphism-level action and full `Functor` packaging are iter-174+ work after
455	`QcohAlgebra` is unpacked to a category.
456	
457	Blueprint reference: `thm:relative_spec_functorial` (Stacks 01LR
458	definition-relative-spec + lemma-glueing-gives-functor-spec). -/
459	
460	/-- **The relative-spectrum functor (object level).**
461	
462	The object-level functorial action `𝒜 ↦ Over.mk (π_𝒜) : Over X`, packaging
463	the relative spectrum together with its structure morphism over `X`.
464	
465	iter-174+: the body is concrete via `Over.mk (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)`
466	but is left as `sorry` here because `RelativeSpec.structureMorphism` is itself
467	a typed `sorry`; once the structure morphism lands the body collapses to
468	`fun 𝒜 => Over.mk (structureMorphism 𝒜)`. The full categorical functor
469	`X.QcohAlgebra ⥤ Over X` (with `map` action induced by the universal property)
470	becomes expressible once `QcohAlgebra` carries its category structure. -/
471	noncomputable def functor (X : Scheme.{u}) :
472	    X.QcohAlgebra → Over X :=
473	  fun 𝒜 => Over.mk (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)
474	
475	end RelativeSpec
476	
477	end Scheme
478	
479	end AlgebraicGeometry

1	\chapter{Relative Spec}
2	\label{chap:Picard_RelativeSpec}
3	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelativeSpec.lean
4	
5	% STRATEGY NOTE (iter-172). This chapter is the foundational A.1.a sub-row of
6	% Route A (positive-genus arm of nonempty_jacobianWitness). It packages the
7	% relative-spectrum functor \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A}) : \mathrm{QcohAlg}(X)^{op}
8	% \to \mathrm{Sch}/X used by the relative Picard functor on a product C \times_k T.
9	% Mathlib has the absolute Spec functor and affine gluing (Scheme.GlueData,
10	% AffineScheme.glueOpens) but no general RelativeSpec construction; this chapter is
11	% the prover-ready specification for the new file AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelativeSpec.lean
12	% which iter-173 (file-skeleton lane) will scaffold.
13	
14	\section{Setup and motivation}
15	\label{sec:relspec_setup}
16	
17	The Route~A construction of $J = \Pic^0_{C/k}$ as a $g$-dimensional abelian variety is
18	organised around the relative Picard functor
19	$\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}(T) = \Pic(C \times_k T) / \pi_T^* \Pic(T)$
20	on the category of $k$-schemes. Representing $\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}$ requires the line-bundle
21	pullback construction on a relative curve $C \times_k T \to T$, which in turn rests on a
22	\emph{relative scheme} construction on a quasi-coherent sheaf of $\mathcal{O}_X$-algebras
23	$\mathcal{A}$, the \emph{relative spectrum}
24	$\pi : \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A}) \to X$. This chapter packages the construction
25	of $\underline{\Spec}_X(-)$ and its three core properties --- universal property, affine
26	base, base change --- as the foundational A.1.a building block. The relative Picard
27	functor itself (A.1.c), the line-bundle pullback functor on a product (A.1.b), and the
28	identity-component / dimension theory of $J$ (A.3) live in separate sibling chapters
29	(\S~``Out of scope'' below).
30	
31	% SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Section ``Relative spectrum via glueing'' and ``Relative
32	% spectrum as a functor'' (tags 01LL, 01LO, 01LQ, 01LR, 01LS, available locally at

36	\section{Quasi-coherent sheaves of algebras}
37	\label{sec:qc_sheaf_of_algebras}
38	
39	\begin{definition}\leanok
40	[Quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-algebra]
41	  \label{def:qc_sheaf_of_algebras}
42	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QcohAlgebra}
43	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Section 01LL ``Relative spectrum via glueing'',
44	  % Situation ``situation-relative-spec''
45	  % (read from references/stacks-constructions.tex, L312-L318).
46	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
47	  % "Here $S$ is a scheme, and $\mathcal{A}$ is a quasi-coherent
48	  %  $\mathcal{O}_S$-algebra. This means that $\mathcal{A}$ is a
49	  %  sheaf of $\mathcal{O}_S$-algebras which is quasi-coherent as an
50	  %  $\mathcal{O}_S$-module."
51	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 01LL (situation-relative-spec).}
52	  Let $X$ be a scheme. A \emph{quasi-coherent sheaf of $\mathcal{O}_X$-algebras} is a
53	  sheaf $\mathcal{A}$ of $\mathcal{O}_X$-algebras (i.e. an $\mathcal{O}_X$-module
54	  equipped with $\mathcal{O}_X$-linear multiplication and unit morphisms satisfying
55	  associativity, commutativity, and the unit law as sheaf morphisms) whose underlying
56	  $\mathcal{O}_X$-module is quasi-coherent. We write $\mathrm{QcohAlg}(X)$ for the
57	  category of quasi-coherent sheaves of $\mathcal{O}_X$-algebras and
58	  $\mathcal{O}_X$-algebra morphisms.
59	\end{definition}

79	\section{The relative-spectrum scheme}
80	\label{sec:relspec_scheme}
81	
82	The construction is given affine-locally: on every affine open $U = \Spec R \subseteq X$ one
83	sets $\pi^{-1}(U) := \Spec(\mathcal{A}(U))$ regarded as an $R$-algebra, and the local pieces
84	glue compatibly because $\mathcal{A}$ is quasi-coherent (the transition isomorphism
85	$\mathcal{A}(U) \otimes_R S \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{A}(V)$ for an inclusion
86	$V = \Spec S \subseteq U$ gives an open immersion of the corresponding Specs).
87	
88	\begin{theorem}\leanok
89	[Relative spectrum exists]
90	  \label{thm:relative_spec_exists}
91	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec}
92	  \uses{def:qc_sheaf_of_algebras}
93	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 01LQ (lemma-glue-relative-spec)
94	  % (read from references/stacks-constructions.tex, L381-L405).
95	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
96	  % "In Situation \ref{situation-relative-spec}.
97	  %  There exists a morphism of schemes
98	  %  $$
99	  %  \pi : \underline{\Spec}_S(\mathcal{A}) \longrightarrow S
100	  %  $$
101	  %  with the following properties:
102	  %  \begin{enumerate}
103	  %  \item for every affine open $U \subset S$ there exists an isomorphism
104	  %  $i_U : \pi^{-1}(U) \to \Spec(\mathcal{A}(U))$ over $U$, and
105	  %  \item for $U \subset U' \subset S$ affine open the composition
106	  %  $$
107	  %  \xymatrix{
108	  %  \Spec(\mathcal{A}(U)) \ar[r]^{i_U^{-1}} &
109	  %  \pi^{-1}(U) \ar[rr]^{inclusion} & &
110	  %  \pi^{-1}(U') \ar[r]^{i_{U'}} &
111	  %  \Spec(\mathcal{A}(U'))
112	  %  }
113	  %  $$
114	  %  is the open immersion of Lemma \ref{lemma-spec-inclusion} above.
115	  %  \end{enumerate}
116	  %  Moreover, $\underline{\Spec}_S(\mathcal{A})$
117	  %  is unique up to unique isomorphism over $S$."
118	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 01LQ (lemma-glue-relative-spec);
119	  cf.\ [Hartshorne], II~Ex.~5.17(a).}
120	  Let $X$ be a scheme and $\mathcal{A}$ a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-algebra
121	  (Definition~\ref{def:qc_sheaf_of_algebras}). There exists a scheme
122	  $\underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})$ together with an affine morphism
123	  $\pi : \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A}) \to X$ such that
124	  \begin{enumerate}
125	    \item for every affine open $U \subseteq X$, there is an isomorphism
126	      $i_U : \pi^{-1}(U) \xrightarrow{\sim} \Spec(\mathcal{A}(U))$ over $U$;
127	    \item for $U \subseteq U' \subseteq X$ both affine open, the composite
128	      $\Spec(\mathcal{A}(U)) \xrightarrow{i_U^{-1}} \pi^{-1}(U) \hookrightarrow
129	      \pi^{-1}(U') \xrightarrow{i_{U'}} \Spec(\mathcal{A}(U'))$
130	      coincides with the open immersion induced by the restriction
131	      $\mathcal{A}(U') \to \mathcal{A}(U)$.
132	  \end{enumerate}
133	  The pair $(\underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A}), \pi)$ is unique up to unique isomorphism
134	  over $X$.
135	\end{theorem}
136	
137	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Follows immediately from
138	% Lemmas \ref{lemma-relative-glueing},
139	% \ref{lemma-spec-inclusion}, and
140	% \ref{lemma-transitive-spec}.
141	% Uniqueness is stated in the last sentence of
142	% Lemma \ref{lemma-relative-glueing}."
143	\begin{proof}
144	  \uses{def:qc_sheaf_of_algebras}
145	  \leanok
146	  By the open-cover gluing principle for schemes (Mathlib
147	  \texttt{Scheme.GlueData} / \texttt{AffineScheme.glueOpens}) it suffices to specify
148	  affine local pieces $\Spec(\mathcal{A}(U))$ for each affine open $U \subseteq X$ and
149	  open-immersion transition data
150	  $\Spec(\mathcal{A}(U)) \hookrightarrow \Spec(\mathcal{A}(U'))$ for $U \subseteq U'$
151	  satisfying the cocycle condition. The transition morphisms come from the ring maps
152	  $\mathcal{A}(U') \to \mathcal{A}(U)$; quasi-coherence yields the cartesian square
153	  $\Spec(\mathcal{A}(U)) = U \times_{U'} \Spec(\mathcal{A}(U'))$, so each transition is an
154	  open immersion. Transitivity (Stacks lemma-transitive-spec) gives the cocycle condition.
155	  Uniqueness of the glued scheme is the standard property of the gluing functor.
156	\end{proof}

158	\begin{theorem}\leanok
159	[Universal property of the relative spectrum]
160	  \label{thm:relative_spec_univ}
161	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec.UniversalProperty}
162	  \uses{def:qc_sheaf_of_algebras, thm:relative_spec_exists}
163	  % NOTE (iter-173 review): the iter-173 file-skeleton encodes this as the
164	  % structural consequence ``IsAffineHom (structureMorphism 𝒜)'' rather than
165	  % the natural-bijection statement pinned by this prose. Iter-174+ must
166	  % upgrade the Lean type to a ``CategoryTheory.Functor.RepresentableBy''
167	  % witness (or equivalent) before any consumer file relies on this theorem.
168	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 01LQ continuation: Section ``Relative spectrum as
169	  % a functor'', functor F and Lemma lemma-spec
170	  % (read from references/stacks-constructions.tex, L427-L466 + L547-L551).
171	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
172	  % "For any $f : T \to S$ the pullback
173	  %  $f^*\mathcal{A}$ is a quasi-coherent sheaf of $\mathcal{O}_T$-algebras.
174	  %  We are going to consider pairs $(f : T \to S, \varphi)$ where
175	  %  $f$ is a morphism of schemes and $\varphi : f^*\mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{O}_T$
176	  %  is a morphism of $\mathcal{O}_T$-algebras. Note that this is the
177	  %  same as giving a $f^{-1}\mathcal{O}_S$-algebra homomorphism
178	  %  $\varphi : f^{-1}\mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{O}_T$, see
179	  %  Sheaves, Lemma \ref{sheaves-lemma-adjointness-tensor-restrict}.
180	  %  This is also the same as giving an $\mathcal{O}_S$-algebra map
181	  %  $\varphi : \mathcal{A} \to f_*\mathcal{O}_T$, see
182	  %  Sheaves, Lemma \ref{sheaves-lemma-adjoint-push-pull-modules}.
183	  %  We will use all three ways of thinking about $\varphi$,
184	  %  without further mention. [...]
185	  %  In this way we have defined a functor
186	  %  \begin{eqnarray}
187	  %  \label{equation-spec}
188	  %  F : \Sch^{opp} & \longrightarrow & \textit{Sets} \\
189	  %  T & \longmapsto & F(T) = \{\text{pairs }(f, \varphi) \text{ as above}\}
190	  %  \nonumber
191	  %  \end{eqnarray}
192	  %  [...]
193	  %  The functor $F$ is representable by a scheme."
194	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 01LQ (lemma-spec + section-spec
195	  functor description); [Hartshorne], II~Ex.~5.17(b).}
196	  Let $X$ be a scheme and $\mathcal{A}$ a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-algebra.
197	  Then $\underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})$ represents the functor
198	  $F : \Sch^{op} \to \mathrm{Set}$,
199	  $T \mapsto F(T) := \{(f, \varphi) : f : T \to X \text{ a morphism},\;
200	  \varphi : f^* \mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{O}_T \text{ an } \mathcal{O}_T\text{-algebra map}\}.$
201	  Equivalently, for any $X$-scheme $g : T \to X$,
202	  there is a natural bijection
203	  \[
204	    \Hom_X(T, \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})) \;\;\cong\;\;
205	    \Hom_{\mathcal{O}_X\text{-alg}}(\mathcal{A}, g_* \mathcal{O}_T)
206	  \]
207	  (using the adjunction between $g_*$ and $g^*$ to convert between
208	  $\mathcal{A} \to g_*\mathcal{O}_T$ and $g^*\mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{O}_T$).
209	  The bijection is natural in $T$.
210	\end{theorem}

212	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: TODO retrieve from references/stacks-constructions.tex
213	% (proof of lemma-spec, L553-L600, ~50 lines covering the Zariski sheaf property,
214	% the subfunctors F_i associated to an affine cover, and identifying each F_i
215	% with the affine-base case via lemma-spec-base-change and lemma-spec-affine).
216	% Verbatim quote omitted for length; the structural steps are restated below in
217	% project notation.
218	\begin{proof}
219	  \uses{thm:relative_spec_exists, thm:relative_spec_affine_base, thm:relative_spec_base_change}
220	  \leanok
221	  % NOTE (iter-179 review): supersedes the iter-176 placeholder-discharge note.
222	  % The iter-179 Block A refactor replaces the iter-176 placeholder bodies with
223	  % the Mathlib values ``(AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData _).glued`` /
224	  % ``.toBase``; iter-179 Lane B closes the Lean ``UniversalProperty`` proof
225	  % via ``isAffineHom_of_forall_exists_isAffineOpen`` +
226	  % ``relativeGluingData.toBase_preimage_eq_opensRange_ι`` +
227	  % ``isAffineOpen_opensRange`` — a real proof against the gluing infrastructure,
228	  % NOT against a trivial identity placeholder. The Lean type remains the
229	  % strictly weaker ``IsAffineHom (structureMorphism 𝒜)'' rather than the
230	  % Yoneda-bijection form pinned above (iter-174+ commitment; still pending
231	  % iter-180+). Consumers may now rely on the affineness conclusion, but the
232	  % Yoneda-bijection witness is still deferred.
233	  Following Stacks tag 01LQ (proof of lemma-spec): the functor $F$ is a Zariski sheaf
234	  because morphisms of schemes and $\mathcal{O}$-algebra maps both glue under open covers.
235	  Choose an affine open cover $X = \bigcup U_i$. The subfunctor
236	  $F_i \subseteq F$ of pairs $(f, \varphi)$ with $f(T) \subseteq U_i$ is representable by
237	  $\Spec(\mathcal{A}(U_i))$ via the affine-base case
238	  (Theorem~\ref{thm:relative_spec_affine_base}) together with the
239	  base-change identification $F_i \cong F_{U_i,\,\mathcal{A}|_{U_i}}$
240	  (Theorem~\ref{thm:relative_spec_base_change}). Each $F_i \hookrightarrow F$ is an open
241	  subfunctor (pull back $U_i$ along $f$). The $F_i$ jointly cover $F$ since the $U_i$
242	  cover $X$. The gluing of representable open subfunctors produces a representing scheme,
243	  which is canonically isomorphic to the glued scheme $\underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})$
244	  of Theorem~\ref{thm:relative_spec_exists}.
245	\end{proof}

248	\label{sec:relspec_affine}

250	\begin{theorem}\leanok
251	[Relative spectrum over an affine base]
252	  \label{thm:relative_spec_affine_base}
253	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec.affine_base_iff}
254	  % NOTE (iter-173 review): the iter-173 file-skeleton encodes this as the
255	  % weaker ``IsAffine ((Spec R).RelativeSpec 𝒜)'' rather than the canonical
256	  % iso ``Spec_X(𝒜) ≅ Spec(Γ(X, 𝒜))'' pinned by this prose. The Lean name
257	  % ``affine_base_iff'' is also misleading (the encoded type is not an iff).
258	  % Iter-174+ refines to the iff-with-Γ statement once Γ : QcohAlgebra → CommRingCat
259	  % is available.
260	  \uses{def:qc_sheaf_of_algebras, thm:relative_spec_exists}
261	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], tag 01LO (lemma-spec-affine)
262	  % (read from references/stacks-constructions.tex, L491-L545).
263	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
264	  % "In Situation \ref{situation-relative-spec}.
265	  %  Let $F$ be the functor associated to $(S, \mathcal{A})$ above.
266	  %  If $S$ is affine, then $F$ is representable by the
267	  %  affine scheme $\Spec(\Gamma(S, \mathcal{A}))$."
268	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 01LO (lemma-spec-affine).}
269	  Let $X = \Spec R$ be an affine scheme and let $\mathcal{A}$ be a quasi-coherent
270	  $\mathcal{O}_X$-algebra. Set $A := \Gamma(X, \mathcal{A})$, regarded as an
271	  $R$-algebra. Then there is a canonical isomorphism of $X$-schemes
272	  \[
273	    \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A}) \;\;\cong\;\; \Spec(A).
274	  \]
275	  In particular, on an affine base the relative spectrum reduces to the absolute
276	  spectrum of the global sections.
277	\end{theorem}

281	% The ring map $R \to A$ gives rise to a canonical map
282	% $$
283	% f_{univ} : \Spec(A)
284	% \longrightarrow
285	% S = \Spec(R).
286	% $$
287	% We have
288	% $f_{univ}^*\mathcal{A} =  \widetilde{A \otimes_R A}$
289	% by Schemes, Lemma \ref{schemes-lemma-widetilde-pullback}.
290	% Hence there is a canonical map
291	% $$
292	% \varphi_{univ} :
293	% f_{univ}^*\mathcal{A} = \widetilde{A \otimes_R A}
294	% \longrightarrow
295	% \widetilde A = \mathcal{O}_{\Spec(A)}
296	% $$
297	% coming from the $A$-module map $A \otimes_R A \to A$,
298	% $a \otimes a' \mapsto aa'$. We claim that the pair
299	% $(f_{univ}, \varphi_{univ})$ represents $F$ in this case."
300	\begin{proof}
301	  \uses{def:qc_sheaf_of_algebras, thm:relative_spec_univ}
302	  \leanok
303	  % NOTE (iter-179 review): supersedes the iter-176 placeholder-discharge note.
304	  % The iter-176 ``change IsAffine (Spec R); inferInstance`` form is now stale
305	  % — iter-179 Lane B closes via ``UniversalProperty 𝒜`` + ``isAffine_of_isAffineHom``
306	  % against the iter-179 Mathlib-backed ``RelativeSpec``/``structureMorphism``
307	  % bodies. The Lean type remains the strictly weaker ``IsAffine`` rather than

309	  % commitment; still pending iter-180+).
310	  Write $X = \Spec R$ and $A = \Gamma(X, \mathcal{A})$. Quasi-coherence of
311	  $\mathcal{A}$ gives $\mathcal{A} = \widetilde{A}$ as $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules
312	  (with the $R$-algebra structure on $A$ inducing the algebra structure on

345	  % lemma-spec-properties part (2))

364	  Let $g : T \to X$ be a morphism of schemes and let $\mathcal{A}$ be a quasi-coherent

366	  $\mathcal{O}_T$-algebra and there is a canonical isomorphism of $T$-schemes

373	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "A pair $(f' : T \to S', \varphi' : (f')^*\mathcal{A}' \to \mathcal{O}_T)$
374	% is the same as a pair $(f, \varphi : f^*\mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{O}_T)$
375	% together with a factorization of $f$ as $f = g \circ f'$. Namely with
376	% this notation we have
377	% $(f')^* \mathcal{A}' = (f')^*g^*\mathcal{A} = f^*\mathcal{A}$.
378	% Hence the lemma."
379	\begin{proof}
380	  \uses{thm:relative_spec_univ}
381	  \leanok
382	  % NOTE (iter-179 review): supersedes the iter-176 placeholder-discharge note.
383	  % The iter-179 Lane B closes the existential by witnessing with the named
384	  % helpers ``Scheme.QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜`` and
385	  % ``Scheme.RelativeSpec.pullback_iso g 𝒜`` (both substantive but with
386	  % ``sorry`` on Mathlib-gap content: the ``coequifibered`` field of the
387	  % pullback and the canonical iso body). The Lean type remains the weaker
388	  % existential rather than the canonical iso with a *named* pullback ``g^*𝒜''
389	  % pinned by this prose (iter-174+ commitment). Consumers should treat the
390	  % iso witness as load-bearing-pending until ``pullback_iso``'s body lands.
391	  By Theorem~\ref{thm:relative_spec_univ}, $\underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})$ represents
392	  the functor $F$ sending an $X$-scheme $h : S \to X$ to the set of
393	  $\mathcal{O}_S$-algebra maps $h^*\mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{O}_S$. A morphism
394	  $S \to T \times_X \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})$ over $T$ is the same as a pair of an
395	  $X$-morphism $S \to \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})$ together with a $T$-factorisation
396	  of $S \to X$, i.e.\ a morphism $S \to T$ whose composition with $g$ equals
397	  $S \to X$. Tracing through, this is a $T$-morphism $f' : S \to T$ together with an
398	  $\mathcal{O}_S$-algebra map $(f')^*(g^*\mathcal{A}) = f^*\mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{O}_S$.
399	  This is precisely $F'(S)$, the functor for $(T, g^*\mathcal{A})$, so the canonical map
400	  $T \times_X \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A}) \to \underline{\Spec}_T(g^*\mathcal{A})$
401	  is an isomorphism by Yoneda.
