421	  % SOURCE QUOTE:
422	  % "Let $S$ be a scheme. Let $\mathcal{A}$ be a quasi-coherent sheaf of
423	  %  $\mathcal{O}_S$-algebras. The {\it relative spectrum of $\mathcal{A}$ over
424	  %  $S$}, or simply the {\it spectrum of $\mathcal{A}$ over $S$} is the scheme
425	  %  constructed in Lemma \ref{lemma-glue-relative-spec} which represents the
426	  %  functor $F$ (\ref{equation-spec}), see
427	  %  Lemma \ref{lemma-glueing-gives-functor-spec}.
428	  %  We denote it $\pi : \underline{\Spec}_S(\mathcal{A}) \to S$.
429	  %  The ``universal family'' is a morphism of $\mathcal{O}_S$-algebras
430	  %  $$
431	  %  \mathcal{A}
432	  %  \longrightarrow
433	  %  \pi_*\mathcal{O}_{\underline{\Spec}_S(\mathcal{A})}
434	  %  $$
435	  %  [...]
436	  %  The universal map
437	  %  $$
438	  %  \mathcal{A}
439	  %  \longrightarrow
440	  %  \pi_*\mathcal{O}_{\underline{\Spec}_S(\mathcal{A})}
441	  %  $$
442	  %  is an isomorphism of $\mathcal{O}_S$-algebras."
443	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], tag 01LR (definition-relative-spec +
444	  lemma-glueing-gives-functor-spec + lemma-spec-properties);
445	  [Hartshorne], II~Ex.~5.17(b).}
446	  The construction $\mathcal{A} \mapsto \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})$ assembles into
447	  a contravariant functor
448	  \[
449	    \underline{\Spec}_X : \mathrm{QcohAlg}(X)^{op} \longrightarrow \mathrm{AffSch}/X
450	  \]
451	  from quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-algebras to affine $X$-schemes: a morphism of
452	  $\mathcal{O}_X$-algebras $\psi : \mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{B}$ induces, by the universal
453	  property (Theorem~\ref{thm:relative_spec_univ}) applied to the pair
454	  $(\pi_\mathcal{B}, \psi \circ \pi_\mathcal{B}^*) :
455	  \pi_\mathcal{B}^*\mathcal{A} \to \pi_\mathcal{B}^*\mathcal{B} \to \mathcal{O}_{\Spec\mathcal{B}}$,
456	  an $X$-morphism
457	  $\underline{\Spec}_X(\psi) : \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{B}) \to \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})$.
458	  Functoriality (identity, composition) is direct from the universal property. The
459	  pushforward $\pi_* \mathcal{O}_{\underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})} \cong \mathcal{A}$
460	  exhibits $\underline{\Spec}_X$ as an equivalence between $\mathrm{QcohAlg}(X)^{op}$ and
461	  the full subcategory of affine $X$-schemes, with inverse $f \mapsto f_* \mathcal{O}_Y$
462	  (Stacks lemma-spec-properties part~(3)).
463	\end{theorem}
464	
465	\begin{proof}
466	  \uses{thm:relative_spec_exists, thm:relative_spec_univ, thm:relative_spec_affine_base}
467	  \leanok
468	  Functoriality follows directly from Theorem~\ref{thm:relative_spec_univ}: for
469	  $\psi : \mathcal{A} \to \mathcal{B}$, the pair
470	  $(\pi_\mathcal{B} : \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{B}) \to X, \;
471	  \pi_\mathcal{B}^*(\psi))$ defines an $X$-morphism
472	  $\underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{B}) \to \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})$ via the
473	  representing universal pair. Identity and composition follow from naturality of the
474	  representing bijection in $\mathcal{A}$. For the equivalence claim, $\underline{\Spec}_X$
475	  is fully faithful because the isomorphism
476	  $\pi_* \mathcal{O}_{\underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})} \cong \mathcal{A}$
477	  (locally identifying with $\Gamma(\Spec(\mathcal{A}(U)),\mathcal{O}) = \mathcal{A}(U)$
478	  via Theorem~\ref{thm:relative_spec_affine_base}) reconstructs $\mathcal{A}$ from
479	  $\underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})$. Essential surjectivity onto affine $X$-schemes uses
480	  the canonical morphism $X \to \underline{\Spec}_X(\pi_*\mathcal{O}_X)$ of Stacks
481	  lemma-canonical-morphism; on an affine $X$-scheme $\pi : Y \to X$, this canonical
482	  morphism is an isomorphism (affine-locally it is the identity
483	  $\Spec\Gamma(\pi^{-1}(U), \mathcal{O}_Y) \to \Spec\Gamma(\pi^{-1}(U), \mathcal{O}_Y)$).
484	\end{proof}
485	
486	\section{Lean encoding}
487	\label{sec:relspec_lean_encoding}
488	
489	The Lean target file is the new \texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelativeSpec.lean}.
490	The construction proceeds by gluing absolute affine pieces: for each affine open
491	$U \subseteq X$ with $U = \Spec R$ and $A := \mathcal{A}(U)$ regarded as an
492	$R$-algebra, the local piece is $\Spec A$ with structure morphism $\Spec A \to \Spec R = U$
493	induced by the algebra unit $R \to A$. Mathlib provides \texttt{IsAffineOpen.toScheme} for
494	the affine-open inclusion and the absolute \texttt{Scheme.Spec} functor for the local
495	pieces; the transition isomorphisms come from quasi-coherence of $\mathcal{A}$ via
496	\texttt{QuasiCoherent.tensorIso} (or the explicit
497	$\mathcal{A}(U) \otimes_R S \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{A}(V)$ for $V = \Spec S \subseteq U$).
498	The gluing backbone is \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.GlueData} (general construction) or
499	the convenience wrapper \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.AffineScheme.glueOpens}; the cocycle
500	condition reduces to Stacks \texttt{lemma-transitive-spec}. The universal property
501	(Theorem~\ref{thm:relative_spec_univ}) is expressed as a
502	\texttt{CategoryTheory.Functor.RepresentableBy} witness, providing the natural bijection
503	$\Hom_X(T, \underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})) \cong
504	\Hom_{\mathcal{O}_X\text{-alg}}(\mathcal{A}, g_*\mathcal{O}_T)$ as a
505	\texttt{Yoneda}-style structure. The base-change isomorphism
506	(Theorem~\ref{thm:relative_spec_base_change}) and functoriality
507	(Theorem~\ref{thm:relative_spec_functorial}) are derived from the universal property by
508	Yoneda. No new core axiom is required; the entire chapter is a definitional/structural
509	construction on top of existing Mathlib affine-scheme infrastructure.
510	
511	\section{Out of scope}
512	\label{sec:relspec_out_of_scope}
513	
514	This chapter packages \emph{only} the relative-spectrum construction and its three core
515	properties. The following downstream constructions live in separate sibling chapters and
516	are explicitly out of scope here:
517	\begin{itemize}
518	  \item \textbf{Relative Picard functor $\Pic^\sharp_{C/k}$} (A.1.c) --- consumed-by, not
519	    defined-here; its blueprint home is the planned chapter
520	    \texttt{Picard\_RelPicFunctor.tex}.
521	  \item \textbf{Line-bundle pullback on a product $C \times_k T$} (A.1.b) ---
522	    \texttt{Picard\_LineBundlePullback.tex} (planned).
523	  \item \textbf{Connectedness, dimensionality, smoothness of $\underline{\Spec}_X(\mathcal{A})$}
524	    --- downstream properties that depend on assumptions on $\mathcal{A}$
525	    (locally finite type, flat, etc.), not unconditional consequences of the construction.
526	  \item \textbf{Identity-component $\Pic^0$ and abelian-variety structure} (A.3) --- a
527	    separate Route~A sub-row, fed by but not constructed in this chapter.
528	\end{itemize}

module

prelude
public import Lean.Meta.Reduce
public import Lean.Elab.Eval
public import Lean.Elab.Command
public import Lean.Elab.Open
import Init.Data.Nat.Order
import Init.Data.Order.Lemmas
import Init.System.Platform

public section

namespace Lean.Elab.Command

@[builtin_command_elab moduleDoc] def elabModuleDoc : CommandElab := fun stx => do
  let some range ← Elab.getDeclarationRange? stx
    | return  -- must be from partial syntax, ignore

  match stx[1] with
  | Syntax.atom _ val =>
    if getMainVersoModuleDocs (← getEnv) |>.isEmpty then
      let doc := String.Pos.Raw.extract val 0 (val.rawEndPos.unoffsetBy ⟨2⟩)
      modifyEnv fun env => addMainModuleDoc env ⟨doc, range⟩
    else
      throwError m!"Can't add Markdown-format module docs because there is already Verso-format content present."
  | Syntax.node _ ``Lean.Parser.Command.versoCommentBody args =>
    let docSyntax := args.getD 0 .missing
    if docSyntax.getKind == `Lean.Doc.Syntax.parseFailure then
      -- Report parser errors without attempting elaboration
      runTermElabM fun _ => reportVersoParseFailure docSyntax
    else
      runTermElabM fun _ => do
        addVersoModDocString range ⟨docSyntax⟩
  | _ => throwErrorAt stx "unexpected module doc string{indentD <| stx}"

private def addScope (isNewNamespace : Bool) (header : String) (newNamespace : Name)
    (isNoncomputable isPublic isMeta : Bool := false) (attrs : List (TSyntax ``Parser.Term.attrInstance) := []) :
    CommandElabM Unit := do
  modify fun s => { s with
    env    := s.env.registerNamespace newNamespace,
    scopes := { s.scopes.head! with
      header := header, currNamespace := newNamespace
      isNoncomputable := s.scopes.head!.isNoncomputable || isNoncomputable
      isPublic := s.scopes.head!.isPublic || isPublic
      isMeta := s.scopes.head!.isMeta || isMeta
      attrs := s.scopes.head!.attrs ++ attrs
    } :: s.scopes
  }
  pushScope
  if isNewNamespace then
    activateScoped newNamespace

private def addScopes (header : Name) (isNewNamespace : Bool) (isNoncomputable isPublic isMeta : Bool := false)
    (attrs : List (TSyntax ``Parser.Term.attrInstance) := []) : CommandElabM Unit :=
  go header
where go
  | .anonymous => pure ()
  | .str p header => do
    go p
    let currNamespace ← getCurrNamespace
    addScope isNewNamespace header (if isNewNamespace then Name.mkStr currNamespace header else currNamespace) isNoncomputable isPublic isMeta attrs
  | _ => throwError "invalid scope"

private def addNamespace (header : Name) : CommandElabM Unit :=
  addScopes (isNewNamespace := true) (isNoncomputable := false) (attrs := []) header

private def popScopes (numScopes : Nat) : CommandElabM Unit :=
  for _ in *...numScopes do
    popScope

def withNamespace {α} (ns : Name) (elabFn : CommandElabM α) : CommandElabM α := do
  addNamespace ns
  let a ← elabFn
  modify fun s => { s with scopes := s.scopes.drop ns.getNumParts }
  popScopes ns.getNumParts
  pure a

private def innermostScopeName? : List Scope → Option Name
  | { header := "", .. } :: _ => none
  | { header := h, .. }  :: _ => some <| .mkSimple h
  | _                         => some .anonymous -- should not happen

private def checkEndHeader : Name → List Scope → Option Name
  | .anonymous, _ => none
  | .str p s, { header := h, .. } :: scopes =>
    if h == s then
      (.str · s) <$> checkEndHeader p scopes
    else
      some <| .mkSimple h
  | _, _ => some .anonymous -- should not happen

@[builtin_command_elab «namespace»] def elabNamespace : CommandElab := fun stx =>
  match stx with
  | `(namespace $n) => addNamespace n.getId
  | _               => throwUnsupportedSyntax

@[builtin_command_elab «section»] def elabSection : CommandElab := fun stx => do
  match stx with
  | `(Parser.Command.section| $[@[expose%$expTk]]? $[public%$publicTk]? $[noncomputable%$ncTk]? $[meta%$metaTk]? section $(header?)?) =>
    -- TODO: allow more attributes?
    let attrs ← if expTk.isSome then
      pure [← `(Parser.Term.attrInstance| expose)]
    else
      pure []
    if let some header := header? then
      addScopes (isNewNamespace := false) (isNoncomputable := ncTk.isSome) (isPublic := publicTk.isSome) (isMeta := metaTk.isSome) (attrs := attrs) header.getId
    else
      addScope (isNewNamespace := false) (isNoncomputable := ncTk.isSome) (isPublic := publicTk.isSome) (isMeta := metaTk.isSome) (attrs := attrs) "" (← getCurrNamespace)
  | _                        => throwUnsupportedSyntax

@[builtin_command_elab InternalSyntax.end_local_scope] def elabEndLocalScope : CommandElab := fun _ => do
  setDelimitsLocal

/--
Produces a `Name` composed of the names of at most the innermost `n` scopes in `ss`, truncating if an
empty scope is reached (so that we do not suggest names like `Foo.«».Bar`).

If `n` is not specified, will use all scopes in `ss`.
-/
private def nameOfScopes : (ss : List Scope) → (n : Nat := ss.length) → Name
  | _, 0 => .anonymous
  | [], _ => .anonymous
  | s :: ss, n + 1 =>
    if s.header == "" then
      .anonymous
    else
    .str (nameOfScopes ss n) s.header

/--
Returns the first suffix of `base` that begins with `seg`, if one exists.

Note: this uses a naive, O(m*n) implementation for simplicity; we assume repeated partial overlap of
name components to be relatively uncommon, so that practical performance is closer to linear.
-/
private def findSuffixWithPrefix (base : Name) (seg : Name) : Option Name :=
  findSuffixMatch base seg true
where
  /--
  Helper for `findSuffixWithPrefix`. If `allowOffset` is `false`, then `seg` must be a suffix of
  `base`, not just a prefix of a suffix.
  -/
  findSuffixMatch : (base : Name) → (seg : Name) → (allowOffset : Bool) → Option Name
  | _, .anonymous, _ => some .anonymous
  | .anonymous, _, _ => none
  | .num p n, seg@(.num p' n'), allowOffset => do
    if n == n' then
      if let some nm := findSuffixMatch p p' (allowOffset := false) then
        return .num nm n
    if allowOffset then
      return .num (← findSuffixMatch p seg allowOffset) n
    else
      none
  | .str p s, seg@(.str p' s'), allowOffset => do
    if s == s' then
      if let some nm := findSuffixMatch p p' (allowOffset := false) then
        return .str nm s
    if allowOffset then
      return .str (← findSuffixMatch p seg allowOffset) s
    else
      none
  | .str p s, seg, allowOffset =>
    if allowOffset then
      return .str (← findSuffixMatch p seg allowOffset) s
    else
      none
  | .num p n, seg, allowOffset =>
    if allowOffset then
      return .num (← findSuffixMatch p seg allowOffset) n
    else
      none

private def throwNoScope : CommandElabM Unit :=
  throwError m!"Invalid `end`: There is no current scope to end"
    ++ .note m!"Scopes are introduced using `namespace` and `section`"

private def throwMissingName (name : Name) : CommandElabM Unit := do
  let hint ← liftCoreM <| MessageData.hint m!"To end the current scope `{name}`, specify its name:"
    #[← `(end $(mkIdent name))] (codeActionPrefix? := "Add scope name: ")
  throwError "Missing name after `end`: Expected the current scope name `{name}`{hint}"

/--
Produces a hint message with a suggestion to replace the name following `end` at the current ref
with the name given by `scopes` if there is only one active scope; otherwise, returns `none`.

Rationale: When there is only one active scope, only one valid `end` command is possible, so we
suggest it; if there are multiple, then it is harder to determine with confidence which the user
intended to end.
-/
private def mkSingleScopeReplacementHint? (scopes : List Scope) := do
  -- Recall that there is always an anonymous topmost scope, so `scopes.length = 2` when there is
  -- only one active scope:
  if scopes.length == 2 then
    let name := nameOfScopes scopes
    some <$> MessageData.hint m!"Use current scope name `{name}`:" #[(← `(end $(mkIdent name)))]
      (codeActionPrefix? := "Replace scope name: ")
  else
    return none

private def throwTooManyScopeComponents (header : Name) (scopes : List Scope) : CommandElabM Unit := do
  let hint ← liftCoreM do
    if let some hint ← mkSingleScopeReplacementHint? scopes then
      pure hint
    else
      let scopesName := nameOfScopes scopes
      pure <| MessageData.hint' m!"The name after `end` must be `{scopesName}` or some suffix thereof"
  throwError m!"Invalid name after `end`: `{header}` contains too many components" ++ hint

private def throwScopeNameMismatch (header : Name) (scopes : List Scope) (endSize : Nat)
    : CommandElabM Unit := do
  let correspondingScopes := nameOfScopes scopes endSize
  let allScopes := nameOfScopes scopes
  let help ← liftCoreM do
    if let some hint ← mkSingleScopeReplacementHint? scopes then
      pure hint
    else if let some suffix := findSuffixWithPrefix allScopes header then
      let hintMsg := m!"If you meant to end the outer scope(s) `{header}`, you must end all the \
        intervening scopes `{suffix}`:"
      MessageData.hint hintMsg #[← `(end $(mkIdent suffix))]
        (codeActionPrefix? := "Add intervening scopes: ")
    else if correspondingScopes != allScopes then
      pure <| .note m!"The current scopes are `{allScopes}`"
    else pure .nil
  throwError m!"Invalid name after `end`: Expected `{correspondingScopes}`, but found `{header}`" ++ help

private def throwUnnecessaryScopeName (header : Name) : CommandElabM Unit := do
  let hintMsg := m!"Delete the name `{header}` to end the current unnamed scope; outer named scopes \
    can then be closed using additional `end` command(s):"
  let hint ← liftCoreM <| MessageData.hint hintMsg #[← `(end)] (codeActionPrefix? := "Delete name: ")
  throwError m!"Unexpected name `{header}` after `end`: The current section is unnamed" ++ hint

@[builtin_command_elab «end»] def elabEnd : CommandElab := fun stx => do
  let `(end $[$header? $[.%$trailingDotTk?$_]?]?) := stx
    | throwUnsupportedSyntax
  let header? := header?.map (·.getId)
  let danglingDot := trailingDotTk?.join.isSome
  let endSize : Nat := match header? with
    | none   => 1
    | some n => n.getNumParts
  let scopes ← getScopes
  let numScopes := scopes.length
  if numScopes == 1 then
    throwNoScope
  match header? with
  | none        =>
    addCompletionInfo <| .endSection stx none false <| scopes.map (·.header)
    if let some name := innermostScopeName? scopes then
      throwMissingName name
  | some header =>
    if endSize >= numScopes then
      throwTooManyScopeComponents header scopes
    else
      addCompletionInfo <| .endSection stx header danglingDot <| scopes.map (·.header)
      let scopesName := nameOfScopes scopes endSize
      if scopesName != header then
        if scopesName == .anonymous then
          throwUnnecessaryScopeName header
        else
          throwScopeNameMismatch header scopes endSize
  modify fun s => {s with scopes := s.scopes.drop endSize }
  popScopes endSize

private partial def elabChoiceAux (cmds : Array Syntax) (i : Nat) : CommandElabM Unit :=
  if h : i < cmds.size then
    catchInternalId unsupportedSyntaxExceptionId
      (elabCommand cmds[i])
      (fun _ => elabChoiceAux cmds (i+1))
  else
    throwUnsupportedSyntax

@[builtin_command_elab choice] def elabChoice : CommandElab := fun stx =>
  elabChoiceAux stx.getArgs 0

@[builtin_command_elab «universe»] def elabUniverse : CommandElab := fun n => do
  n[1].forArgsM addUnivLevel

@[builtin_command_elab «init_quot»] def elabInitQuot : CommandElab := fun _ => do
  liftCoreM <| addDecl Declaration.quotDecl

@[builtin_command_elab «docs_to_verso»] def elabDocsToVerso : CommandElab := fun stx => do
  let xs := stx[1].getArgs
  for x in xs do
    if x.getKind == identKind then -- skip commas
      let declName ← liftCoreM <| realizeGlobalConstNoOverload x
      runTermElabM <| fun _ => withRef x <| makeDocStringVerso declName
      -- Add the info afterwards so the hover shows the updated docstring
      addConstInfo x declName

@[builtin_command_elab «export»] def elabExport : CommandElab := fun stx => do
  let `(export $ns ($ids*)) := stx | throwUnsupportedSyntax
  let nss ← resolveNamespace ns
  let currNamespace ← getCurrNamespace
  if nss == [currNamespace] then throwError "invalid 'export', self export"
  let mut aliases := #[]
  for idStx in ids do
    let id := idStx.getId
    let declName ← resolveNameUsingNamespaces nss idStx
    if (← getInfoState).enabled then
      addConstInfo idStx declName
    aliases := aliases.push (currNamespace ++ id, declName)
  modify fun s => { s with env := aliases.foldl (init := s.env) fun env p => addAlias env p.1 p.2 }

@[builtin_command_elab «open»] def elabOpen : CommandElab
  | `(open $decl:openDecl) => do
    let openDecls ← elabOpenDecl decl
    modifyScope fun scope => { scope with openDecls := openDecls }
  | _ => throwUnsupportedSyntax

open Lean.Parser.Term

private def typelessBinder? : Syntax → Option (Array (TSyntax [`ident, `Lean.Parser.Term.hole]) × BinderInfo)
  | `(bracketedBinderF|($ids*))     => some (ids, .default)
  | `(bracketedBinderF|{$ids*})     => some (ids, .implicit)
  | `(bracketedBinderF|⦃$ids*⦄)     => some (ids, .strictImplicit)
  | `(bracketedBinderF|[$id:ident]) => some (#[id], .instImplicit)
  | _                               => none

/--  If `id` is an identifier, return true if `ids` contains `id`. -/
private def containsId (ids : Array (TSyntax [`ident, ``Parser.Term.hole])) (id : TSyntax [`ident, ``Parser.Term.hole]) : Bool :=
  id.raw.isIdent && ids.any fun id' => id'.raw.getId == id.raw.getId

/--
  Auxiliary method for processing binder annotation update commands:
  `variable (α)`, `variable {α}`, `variable ⦃α⦄`, and `variable [α]`.
  The argument `binder` is the binder of the `variable` command.
  The method returns an array containing the "residue", that is, variables that do not correspond to updates.
  Recall that a `bracketedBinder` can be of the form `(x y)`.
  ```
  variable {α β : Type}
  variable (α γ)
  ```
  The second `variable` command updates the binder annotation for `α`, and returns "residue" `γ`.
-/
private def replaceBinderAnnotation (binder : TSyntax ``Parser.Term.bracketedBinder) : CommandElabM (Array (TSyntax ``Parser.Term.bracketedBinder)) := do
  let some (binderIds, binderInfo) := typelessBinder? binder | return #[binder]
  let varDecls := (← getScope).varDecls
  let mut varDeclsNew := #[]
  let mut binderIds := binderIds
  let mut binderIdsIniSize := binderIds.size
  let mut modifiedVarDecls := false
  -- Go through declarations in reverse to respect shadowing
  for varDecl in varDecls.reverse do
    let (ids, ty?, binderInfo') ← match varDecl with
      | `(bracketedBinderF|($ids* $[: $ty?]? $(annot?)?)) =>
        if annot?.isSome then
          for binderId in binderIds do
            if containsId ids binderId then
              throwErrorAt binderId "cannot update binder annotation of variables with default values/tactics"
        pure (ids, ty?, .default)
      | `(bracketedBinderF|{$ids* $[: $ty?]?}) =>
        pure (ids, ty?, .implicit)
      | `(bracketedBinderF|⦃$ids* $[: $ty?]?⦄) =>
        pure (ids, ty?, .strictImplicit)
      | `(bracketedBinderF|[$id : $ty]) =>
        pure (#[⟨id⟩], some ty, .instImplicit)
      | _ =>
        varDeclsNew := varDeclsNew.push varDecl; continue
    if binderInfo == binderInfo' then
      -- no update, ensure we don't have redundant annotations.
      for binderId in binderIds do
        if containsId ids binderId then
          throwErrorAt binderId "redundant binder annotation update"
      varDeclsNew := varDeclsNew.push varDecl
    else if binderIds.all fun binderId => !containsId ids binderId then
      -- `binderIds` and `ids` are disjoint
      varDeclsNew := varDeclsNew.push varDecl
    else
      let mkBinder (id : TSyntax [`ident, ``Parser.Term.hole]) (binderInfo : BinderInfo) : CommandElabM (TSyntax ``Parser.Term.bracketedBinder) :=
        match binderInfo with
        | .default => `(bracketedBinderF| ($id $[: $ty?]?))
        | .implicit => `(bracketedBinderF| {$id $[: $ty?]?})
        | .strictImplicit => `(bracketedBinderF| {{$id $[: $ty?]?}})
        | .instImplicit => do
          let some ty := ty?
            | throwErrorAt binder "cannot update binder annotation of variable `{id}` to instance implicit:\n\
                variable was originally declared without an explicit type"
          `(bracketedBinderF| [$(⟨id⟩) : $ty])
      for id in ids.reverse do
        if let some idx := binderIds.findFinIdx? fun binderId => binderId.raw.isIdent && binderId.raw.getId == id.raw.getId then
          binderIds := binderIds.eraseIdx idx
          modifiedVarDecls := true
          let newBinder ← mkBinder id binderInfo
          if binderInfo.isInstImplicit then
            -- We elaborate the new binder to make sure it's valid as instance implicit
            try
              runTermElabM fun _ => Term.withSynthesize <| Term.withAutoBoundImplicit <|
                Term.elabBinder newBinder fun _ => pure ()
            catch e =>
              throwErrorAt binder m!"cannot update binder annotation of variable `{id}` to instance implicit:\n\
                {e.toMessageData}"
          varDeclsNew := varDeclsNew.push (← mkBinder id binderInfo)
        else
          varDeclsNew := varDeclsNew.push (← mkBinder id binderInfo')
  if modifiedVarDecls then
    modifyScope fun scope => { scope with varDecls := varDeclsNew.reverse }
  if binderIds.size != binderIdsIniSize then
    binderIds.mapM fun binderId =>
      match binderInfo with
        | .default => `(bracketedBinderF| ($binderId))
        | .implicit => `(bracketedBinderF| {$binderId})
        | .strictImplicit => `(bracketedBinderF| {{$binderId}})
        | .instImplicit => throwUnsupportedSyntax
  else
    return #[binder]

@[builtin_command_elab «variable»] def elabVariable : CommandElab
  | `(variable%$tk $binders*) => do
    let binders ← binders.flatMapM replaceBinderAnnotation
    -- Try to elaborate `binders` for sanity checking
    runTermElabM fun _ => Term.withSynthesize <| Term.withAutoBoundImplicit <|
      Term.elabBinders binders fun xs => do
        -- Determine the set of auto-implicits for this variable command and add an inlay hint
        -- for them. We will only actually add the auto-implicits to a type when the variables
        -- declared here are used in some other declaration, but this is nonetheless the right
        -- place to display the inlay hint.
        let _ ← Term.addAutoBoundImplicits xs (tk.getTailPos? (canonicalOnly := true))
    -- Remark: if we want to produce error messages when variables shadow existing ones, here is the place to do it.
    for binder in binders do
      let varUIds ← (← getBracketedBinderIds binder) |>.mapM (withFreshMacroScope ∘ MonadQuotation.addMacroScope)
      modifyScope fun scope => { scope with varDecls := scope.varDecls.push binder, varUIds := scope.varUIds ++ varUIds }
  | _ => throwUnsupportedSyntax

open Meta

def elabCheckCore (ignoreStuckTC : Bool) : CommandElab
  | `(#check%$tk $term) => withoutModifyingEnv <| runTermElabM fun _ => Term.withDeclName `_check do
    -- show signature for `#check id`/`#check @id`
    if let `($id:ident) := term then
      try
        for c in (← realizeGlobalConstWithInfos term) do
          addCompletionInfo <| .id term id.getId (danglingDot := false) {} none
          logInfoAt tk <| .signature c
          return
      catch _ => pure ()  -- identifier might not be a constant but constant + projection
    let e ← Term.elabTerm term none
    Term.synthesizeSyntheticMVarsNoPostponing (ignoreStuckTC := ignoreStuckTC)
    -- Users might be testing out buggy elaborators. Let's typecheck before proceeding:
    withRef tk <| Meta.check e
    let e ← Term.levelMVarToParam (← instantiateMVars e)
    if e.isSyntheticSorry then
      return
    let type ← inferType e
    logInfoAt tk m!"{e} : {type}"
  | _ => throwUnsupportedSyntax

@[builtin_command_elab Lean.Parser.Command.check] def elabCheck : CommandElab := elabCheckCore (ignoreStuckTC := true)

@[builtin_command_elab Lean.reduceCmd] def elabReduce : CommandElab
  | `(#reduce%$tk $term) => go tk term
  | `(#reduce%$tk (proofs := true) $term) => go tk term (skipProofs := false)
  | `(#reduce%$tk (types := true) $term) => go tk term (skipTypes := false)
  | `(#reduce%$tk (proofs := true) (types := true) $term) => go tk term (skipProofs := false) (skipTypes := false)
  | _ => throwUnsupportedSyntax
where
  go (tk : Syntax) (term : Syntax) (skipProofs := true) (skipTypes := true) : CommandElabM Unit :=
    withoutModifyingEnv <| runTermElabM fun _ => Term.withDeclName `_reduce do
      let e ← Term.elabTerm term none
      Term.synthesizeSyntheticMVarsNoPostponing
      -- Users might be testing out buggy elaborators. Let's typecheck before proceeding:
      withRef tk <| Meta.check e
      let e ← Term.levelMVarToParam (← instantiateMVars e)
      -- TODO: add options or notation for setting the following parameters
      withTheReader Core.Context (fun ctx => { ctx with options := ctx.options.set `smartUnfolding false }) do
        let e ← withTransparency (mode := TransparencyMode.all) <| reduce e (skipProofs := skipProofs) (skipTypes := skipTypes)
        logInfoAt tk e

def hasNoErrorMessages : CommandElabM Bool := do
  return !(← get).messages.hasErrors

def failIfSucceeds (x : CommandElabM Unit) : CommandElabM Unit := do
  let resetMessages : CommandElabM MessageLog := do
    let s ← get
    let messages := s.messages;
    modify fun s => { s with messages := {} };
    pure messages
  let restoreMessages (prevMessages : MessageLog) : CommandElabM Unit := do
    modify fun s => { s with messages := prevMessages ++ s.messages.errorsToInfos }
  let prevMessages ← resetMessages
  let succeeded ← try
    x
    hasNoErrorMessages
  catch
    | ex@(Exception.error _ _) => do logException ex; pure false
    | Exception.internal id _  => do logError (← id.getName); pure false
  finally
    restoreMessages prevMessages
  if succeeded then
    throwError "unexpected success"

@[builtin_command_elab «check_failure»] def elabCheckFailure : CommandElab
  | `(#check_failure $term) => do
    failIfSucceeds <| elabCheckCore (ignoreStuckTC := false) (← `(#check $term))
  | _ => throwUnsupportedSyntax

@[builtin_command_elab «synth»] def elabSynth : CommandElab := fun stx => do
  let term := stx[1]
  withoutModifyingEnv <| runTermElabM fun _ => Term.withDeclName `_synth_cmd do
    let inst ← Term.elabTerm term none
    Term.synthesizeSyntheticMVarsNoPostponing
    let inst ← instantiateMVars inst
    let val  ← synthInstance inst
    logInfo val
    pure ()

@[builtin_command_elab «set_option»] def elabSetOption : CommandElab := fun stx => do
  let options ← Elab.elabSetOption stx[1] stx[3]
  modify fun s => { s with maxRecDepth := maxRecDepth.get options }
  modifyScope fun scope => { scope with opts := options }

open Lean.Parser.Command.InternalSyntax in
@[builtin_macro Lean.Parser.Command.«in»] def expandInCmd : Macro
  | `($cmd₁ in%$tk $cmd₂) =>
    -- Limit ref variability for incrementality; see Note [Incremental Macros]
    withRef tk `(section $cmd₁:command $endLocalScopeSyntax:command $cmd₂ end)
  | _                 => Macro.throwUnsupported

@[builtin_command_elab Parser.Command.addDocString] def elabAddDeclDoc : CommandElab := fun stx => do
  match stx with
  | `($doc:docComment add_decl_doc $id) =>
    let declName ← liftCoreM <| realizeGlobalConstNoOverloadWithInfo id
    unless ((← getEnv).getModuleIdxFor? declName).isNone do
      throwError "invalid 'add_decl_doc', declaration is in an imported module"
    if let .none ← findDeclarationRangesCore? declName then
      -- this is only relevant for declarations added without a declaration range
      -- in particular `Quot.mk` et al which are added by `init_quot`
      addDeclarationRangesFromSyntax declName stx id
    runTermElabM fun _ => addDocString declName (mkNullNode #[]) doc
  | _ => throwUnsupportedSyntax

@[builtin_command_elab Lean.Parser.Command.include] def elabInclude : CommandElab
  | `(Lean.Parser.Command.include| include $ids*) => do
    let sc ← getScope
    let vars ← sc.varDecls.flatMapM getBracketedBinderIds
    let mut uids := #[]
    for id in ids do
      if let some idx := vars.findIdx? (· == id.getId) then
        uids := uids.push sc.varUIds[idx]!
      else
        throwError "invalid 'include', variable `{id}` has not been declared in the current scope"
    modifyScope fun sc => { sc with
      includedVars := sc.includedVars ++ uids.toList
      omittedVars := sc.omittedVars.filter (!uids.contains ·) }
  | _ => throwUnsupportedSyntax

@[builtin_command_elab Lean.Parser.Command.omit] def elabOmit : CommandElab
  | `(Lean.Parser.Command.omit| omit $omits*) => do
    -- TODO: this really shouldn't have to re-elaborate section vars... they should come
    -- pre-elaborated
    let omittedVars ← runTermElabM fun vars => do
      Term.synthesizeSyntheticMVarsNoPostponing
      -- We don't want to store messages produced when elaborating `(getVarDecls s)` because they have already been saved when we elaborated the `variable`(s) command.
      -- So, we use `Core.resetMessageLog`.
      Core.resetMessageLog
      -- resolve each omit to variable user name or type pattern
      let elaboratedOmits : Array (Sum Name Expr) ← omits.mapM fun
        | `(ident| $id:ident) => pure <| Sum.inl id.getId
        | `(Lean.Parser.Term.instBinder| [$id : $_]) => pure <| Sum.inl id.getId
        | `(Lean.Parser.Term.instBinder| [$ty]) =>
          Sum.inr <$> Term.withoutErrToSorry (Term.elabTermAndSynthesize ty none)
        | _ => throwUnsupportedSyntax
      -- check that each omit is actually used in the end
      let mut omitsUsed := omits.map fun _ => false
      let mut omittedVars := #[]
      let mut revSectionFVars : Std.HashMap FVarId Name := {}
      for (uid, var) in (← read).sectionFVars do
        revSectionFVars := revSectionFVars.insert var.fvarId! uid
      for var in vars do
        let ldecl ← var.fvarId!.getDecl
        if let some idx := (← elaboratedOmits.findIdxM? fun
            | .inl id => return ldecl.userName == id
            | .inr ty => do
              let mctx ← getMCtx
              isDefEq ty ldecl.type <* setMCtx mctx) then
          if let some uid := revSectionFVars[var.fvarId!]? then
            omittedVars := omittedVars.push uid
            omitsUsed := omitsUsed.set! idx true
          else
            throwError "invalid 'omit', `{ldecl.userName}` has not been declared in the current scope"
      for o in omits, used in omitsUsed do
        unless used do
          throwError "`{o}` did not match any variables in the current scope"
      return omittedVars
    modifyScope fun sc => { sc with
      omittedVars := sc.omittedVars ++ omittedVars.toList
      includedVars := sc.includedVars.filter (!omittedVars.contains ·) }
  | _ => throwUnsupportedSyntax

@[builtin_command_elab version] def elabVersion : CommandElab := fun _ => do
  let mut target := System.Platform.target
  if target.isEmpty then target := "unknown"
  -- Only one should be set, but good to know if multiple are set in error.
  let platforms :=
    (if System.Platform.isWindows then [" Windows"] else [])
    ++ (if System.Platform.isOSX then [" macOS"] else [])
    ++ (if System.Platform.isEmscripten then [" Emscripten"] else [])
  logInfo m!"Lean {Lean.versionString}\nTarget: {target}{String.join platforms}"

@[builtin_command_elab Parser.Command.exit] def elabExit : CommandElab := fun _ =>
  logWarning "using 'exit' to interrupt Lean"

@[builtin_command_elab Parser.Command.import] def elabImport : CommandElab := fun _ =>
  throwError "invalid 'import' command, it must be used in the beginning of the file"

@[builtin_command_elab Parser.Command.eoi] def elabEoi : CommandElab := fun _ =>
  return

@[builtin_command_elab Parser.Command.where] def elabWhere : CommandElab := fun _ => do
  let scope ← getScope
  let mut msg : Array MessageData := #[]
  -- Noncomputable
  if scope.isNoncomputable then
    msg := msg.push <| ← `(Parser.Command.section| noncomputable section)
  -- Namespace
  if !scope.currNamespace.isAnonymous then
    msg := msg.push <| ← `(command| namespace $(mkIdent scope.currNamespace))
  -- Open namespaces
  if let some openMsg ← describeOpenDecls scope.openDecls.reverse then
    msg := msg.push openMsg
  -- Universe levels
  if !scope.levelNames.isEmpty then
    let levels := scope.levelNames.reverse.map mkIdent
    msg := msg.push <| ← `(command| universe $levels.toArray*)
  -- Variables
  if !scope.varDecls.isEmpty then
    let varDecls : Array (TSyntax `Lean.Parser.Term.bracketedBinder) := scope.varDecls.map (⟨·.raw.unsetTrailing⟩)
    msg := msg.push <| ← `(command| variable $varDecls*)
  -- Included variables
  if !scope.includedVars.isEmpty then
    msg := msg.push <| ← `(command| include $(scope.includedVars.toArray.map (mkIdent ·.eraseMacroScopes))*)
  -- Options
  if let some optionsMsg ← describeOptions scope.opts then
    msg := msg.push optionsMsg
  if msg.isEmpty then
    logInfo m!"-- In root namespace with initial scope"
  else
    logInfo <| MessageData.joinSep msg.toList "\n\n"
where
  /--
  'Delaborate' open declarations.
  Current limitations:
  - does not check whether or not successive namespaces need `_root_`
  - does not combine commands with `renaming` clauses into a single command
  -/
  describeOpenDecls (ds : List OpenDecl) : CommandElabM (Option MessageData) := do
    let mut lines : Array MessageData := #[]
    let mut simple : Array Name := #[]
    let flush (lines : Array MessageData) (simple : Array Name) : CommandElabM (Array MessageData × Array Name) := do
      if simple.isEmpty then
        return (lines, simple)
      else
        return (lines.push <| ← `(command| open $(simple.map mkIdent)*), #[])
    for d in ds do
      match d with
      | .explicit id decl =>
        (lines, simple) ← flush lines simple
        let ns := decl.getPrefix
        let «from» := Name.mkSimple decl.getString!
        lines := lines.push <| ← `(command| open $(mkIdent ns) renaming $(mkIdent «from») → $(mkIdent id))
      | .simple ns ex =>
        if ex == [] then
          simple := simple.push ns
        else
          (lines, simple) ← flush lines simple
          lines := lines.push <| ← `(command| open $(mkIdent ns) hiding $[$(ex.toArray.map mkIdent)]*)
    (lines, _) ← flush lines simple
    return if lines.isEmpty then none else MessageData.joinSep lines.toList "\n"

  describeOptions (opts : Options) : CommandElabM (Option MessageData) := do
    let mut lines : Array MessageData := #[]
    let decls ← getOptionDecls
    for (name, val) in opts do
      -- `#guard_msgs` sets this option internally, we don't want it to end up in its output
      if name == `Elab.async then
        continue
      let (isSet, isUnknown) :=
        match decls.find? name with
        | some decl => (decl.defValue != val, false)
        | none      => (true, true)
      if isSet then
        let cmd : TSyntax `command ←
          match val with
          | .ofBool true  => `(set_option $(mkIdent name) true)
          | .ofBool false => `(set_option $(mkIdent name) false)
          | .ofString str => `(set_option $(mkIdent name) $(Syntax.mkStrLit str))
          | .ofNat n      => `(set_option $(mkIdent name) $(Syntax.mkNatLit n))
          | _             => `(set_option $(mkIdent name) 0 /- unrepresentable value -/)
        if isUnknown then
          lines := lines.push m!"-- {cmd} -- unknown option"
        else
          lines := lines.push cmd
    return if lines.isEmpty then none else MessageData.joinSep lines.toList "\n"

@[builtin_command_elab Parser.Command.withExporting] def elabWithExporting : CommandElab
  | `(Parser.Command.withExporting| #with_exporting $cmd) =>
    withExporting do
      elabCommand cmd
  | _ => throwUnsupportedSyntax

@[builtin_command_elab Parser.Command.dumpAsyncEnvState] def elabDumpAsyncEnvState : CommandElab :=
  fun _ => do
    let env ← getEnv
    IO.eprintln (← env.dbgFormatAsyncState)

end Lean.Elab.Command
"}

11	/-!
12	# Relative gluing
13	
14	In this file we show a relative gluing lemma (see https://stacks.math.columbia.edu/tag/01LH):
15	If `{Uᵢ}` is a locally directed open cover of `S` and we have a compatible family of `Xᵢ` over `Uᵢ`,
16	the `Xᵢ` glue to a morphism `f : X ⟶ S` such that `Xᵢ ≅ f⁻¹ Uᵢ`.
17	-/
18	
19	@[expose] public section
20	
21	universe u
22	
23	open CategoryTheory Limits
24	
25	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
26	
27	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
28	lemma Scheme.isLocallyDirected_of_equifibered_of_injective {J : Type*} [Category J]
29	    {F G : J ⥤ Scheme.{u}} (s : F ⟶ G) [Quiver.IsThin J] (hs : s.Equifibered)
30	    (H : ∀ {i j} (hij : i ⟶ j), Function.Injective (F.map hij))
31	    [(G ⋙ Scheme.forget).IsLocallyDirected] :
32	    (F ⋙ Scheme.forget).IsLocallyDirected where
33	  cond {i j k} fi fj xi xj heq := by
34	    simp only [Functor.comp_obj, Scheme.forget_obj, Functor.comp_map, Scheme.forget_map] at heq
35	    obtain ⟨l, fli, flj, x, hi, hj⟩ := (G ⋙ Scheme.forget).exists_map_eq_of_isLocallyDirected fi fj
36	        (s.app i xi) (s.app j xj) <| by
37	      simp only [Functor.comp_obj, forget_obj, Functor.comp_map, forget_map,
38	        ConcreteCategory.hom_ofHom, TypeCat.Fun.coe_mk]
39	      dsimp at heq
40	      rw [← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, ← s.naturality, Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, heq,
41	        ← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, s.naturality]
42	      simp
43	    use l, fli, flj
44	    let e := (hs fli).isoPullback
45	    obtain ⟨z, h1, h2⟩ := Scheme.Pullback.exists_preimage_pullback xi x hi.symm
46	    refine ⟨e.inv z, ?_, ?_⟩
47	    · simp [← h1, ← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, e]
48	    · apply H fj
49	      simp only [Functor.comp_obj, forget_obj, Functor.comp_map, forget_map,
50	        ConcreteCategory.hom_ofHom, TypeCat.Fun.coe_mk, ← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply,
51	        Category.assoc, ← Functor.map_comp, show flj ≫ fj = fli ≫ fi by subsingleton]
52	      dsimp at heq
53	      simp [e, Functor.map_comp, ← heq, h1]

55	namespace Scheme.Cover

57	variable {S : Scheme.{u}} (𝒰 : S.OpenCover) [Category 𝒰.I₀] [𝒰.LocallyDirected]
58	
59	/--
60	A relative gluing datum over a locally directed cover `𝒰` of `S` is a scheme `Xᵢ` for every
61	`i : 𝒰.I₀` and natural maps `Xᵢ ⟶ Uᵢ` such that for every `i ⟶ j`, the diagram
62	```
63	Xᵢ --> Uᵢ
64	|      |
65	v      v
66	Xⱼ --> Uⱼ
67	```
68	is a pullback square. We bundle this in the form of a functor and an equifibered natural
69	transformation.
70	The `Xᵢ` then glue to a scheme over `S`
71	(see `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Cover.RelativeGluingData.glued`).
72	-/
73	@[stacks 01LH]
74	structure RelativeGluingData where
75	  /-- The schemes `Xᵢ`. -/
76	  functor : 𝒰.I₀ ⥤ Scheme.{u}
77	  /-- The natural maps `Xᵢ ⟶ Uᵢ`. -/
78	  natTrans : functor ⟶ 𝒰.functorOfLocallyDirected
79	  equifibered : natTrans.Equifibered
80	
81	variable {𝒰} (d : RelativeGluingData 𝒰)
82	
83	namespace RelativeGluingData
84	
85	instance {i j : 𝒰.I₀} (hij : i ⟶ j) : IsOpenImmersion (d.functor.map hij) := by
86	  apply MorphismProperty.of_isPullback (d.equifibered hij).flip
87	  infer_instance
88	
89	instance [Quiver.IsThin 𝒰.I₀] : (d.functor ⋙ Scheme.forget).IsLocallyDirected := by
90	  apply isLocallyDirected_of_equifibered_of_injective d.natTrans d.equifibered
91	  intro i j hij
92	  exact (d.functor.map hij).injective
93	
94	variable [Small.{u} 𝒰.I₀] [Quiver.IsThin 𝒰.I₀]
95	
96	/--
97	The glued scheme of a relative gluing datum is the colimit over the `Xᵢ`. For the
98	structure map, see `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Cover.RelativeGluingData.toBase` and the isomorphisms
99	with the preimages `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Cover.RelativeGluingData.isPullback_natTrans_ι_toBase`.
100	-/
101	@[stacks 01LH]
102	noncomputable abbrev glued : Scheme.{u} :=
103	  colimit d.functor
104	
105	/-- The cover of the glued `Xᵢ` given by the `Xᵢ`. -/
106	@[simps!]
107	noncomputable def cover : OpenCover d.glued :=
108	  Scheme.IsLocallyDirected.openCover _
109	
110	instance : Category d.cover.I₀ :=
111	  inferInstanceAs <| Category 𝒰.I₀
112	
113	/-- The structure map from the colimit of the `Xᵢ` to `S`. -/
114	noncomputable def toBase : d.glued ⟶ S :=
115	  colimit.desc _
116	    { pt := S
117	      ι := d.natTrans ≫ 𝒰.functorOfLocallyDirectedHomBase }
118	
119	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
120	lemma ι_toBase (i : 𝒰.I₀) :
121	    colimit.ι d.functor i ≫ d.toBase = d.natTrans.app i ≫ 𝒰.f i := by
122	  simp [toBase]
123	
124	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
125	instance : d.cover.LocallyDirected where
126	  trans {i j} hij := d.functor.map hij
127	  directed {i j} x := by
128	    let xi := pullback.fst (d.cover.f i) _ x
129	    let xj := pullback.snd (d.cover.f i) _ x
130	    obtain ⟨k, fi, fj, uk, h1, h2⟩ :=
131	        𝒰.exists_of_f_eq_f (d.natTrans.app i xi) (d.natTrans.app j xj) <| by
132	      dsimp [functorOfLocallyDirected_obj, xi, xj]
133	      rw [← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, ← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, ← ι_toBase, pullback.condition_assoc]
134	      simp
135	    use k, fi, fj
136	    obtain ⟨xk, h1, h2⟩ := exists_preimage_of_isPullback (d.equifibered fj) xj uk <| by
137	      apply (𝒰.f j).injective
138	      dsimp only [functorOfLocallyDirected_obj, functorOfLocallyDirected_map]
139	      rw [← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply]
140	      simp [xj, h2]
141	    use xk
142	    apply (pullback.snd (d.cover.f i) _).injective
143	    rw [← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply]
144	    simp [h1, xj]
145	
146	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
147	lemma preimage_toBase_eq_range_ι (i : 𝒰.I₀) :
148	    d.toBase ⁻¹' (Set.range <| 𝒰.f i) = Set.range (colimit.ι d.functor i) := by
149	  ext x
150	  refine ⟨fun ⟨ui, h⟩ ↦ ?_, ?_⟩
151	  · obtain ⟨j, xj, rfl⟩ := IsLocallyDirected.ι_jointly_surjective _ x
152	    obtain ⟨k, fi, fj, uk, rfl, h⟩ := 𝒰.exists_of_f_eq_f ui (d.natTrans.app j xj) <| by
153	      simp only [h, functorOfLocallyDirected_obj, ← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, ι_toBase]
154	    obtain ⟨xk, rfl, h2⟩ := exists_preimage_of_isPullback (d.equifibered fj) xj uk <| by
155	      apply (𝒰.f j).injective
156	      simp only [functorOfLocallyDirected_obj, functorOfLocallyDirected_map]
157	      rw [← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, ← ι_toBase, Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, ← h]
158	      simp [← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply]
159	    use d.functor.map fi xk
160	    simp [← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, ← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply]
161	  · rintro ⟨y, rfl⟩
162	    use d.natTrans.app i y
163	    rw [← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, ι_toBase]
164	    simp
165	
166	lemma toBase_preimage_eq_opensRange_ι (i : 𝒰.I₀) :
167	    d.toBase ⁻¹ᵁ (𝒰.f i).opensRange = (colimit.ι d.functor i).opensRange :=
168	  TopologicalSpace.Opens.coe_inj.mp (preimage_toBase_eq_range_ι d i)
169	
170	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
171	lemma isPullback_natTrans_ι_toBase (i : 𝒰.I₀) :
172	    IsPullback (d.natTrans.app i) (colimit.ι d.functor i) (𝒰.f i) d.toBase := by
173	  refine ⟨by simp, ⟨PullbackCone.IsLimit.mk _ ?_ ?_ ?_ ?_⟩⟩
174	  · intro s
175	    apply IsOpenImmersion.lift (colimit.ι d.functor i) s.snd
176	    rw [← preimage_toBase_eq_range_ι]
177	    rintro x ⟨x, rfl⟩
178	    use s.fst x
179	    rw [← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, ← s.condition]
180	    simp
181	  · intro s
182	    rw [← cancel_mono (𝒰.f i), Category.assoc, ← ι_toBase, IsOpenImmersion.lift_fac_assoc,
183	      s.condition]
184	  · simp
185	  · intro s m h1 h2
186	    simpa [← cancel_mono (colimit.ι d.functor i)]
187	
188	end Scheme.Cover.RelativeGluingData
189	
190	end AlgebraicGeometry

239	section PreservesLocalization
240	
241	/-!
242	## "Quasi-coherent `𝒪ₓ`-algebras"
243	
244	A presheaf `F` of rings on `X.AffineZariskiSite` with a structural morphism `α : 𝒪ₓ ⟶ F`
245	is said to be `Coequifibered` if `F(D(f)) = F(U)[1/f]`
246	for every open `U` and any section `f : Γ(X, U)`.
247	(See `coequifibered_iff_forall_isLocalizationAway`)
248	
249	Under this condition we can construct a family of gluing data (See `relativeGluingData`) and glue
250	`F` into a scheme over `X` via `(relativeGluingData _).glued`,
251	Also see the relative gluing API in `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/RelativeGluing.lean`.
252	
253	This is closely related to the notion of quasi-coherent `𝒪ₓ`-algebras, and we shall link them
254	together once the theory of quasi-coherent `𝒪ₓ`-algebras are developed.
255	-/
256	
257	variable (X) in
258	/-- `X` is the colimit of its affine opens. See `isColimit_cocone` below. -/
259	@[simps] noncomputable def cocone :
260	    Limits.Cocone (toOpensFunctor X ⋙ X.presheaf.rightOp ⋙ Scheme.Spec) where
261	  pt := X
262	  ι.app U := U.2.fromSpec
263	  ι.naturality {U V} f := by dsimp; rw [V.2.map_fromSpec U.2]; simp
264	
265	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
266	lemma coequifibered_iff_forall_isLocalizationAway {F : X.AffineZariskiSiteᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat}
267	    {α : (AffineZariskiSite.toOpensFunctor X).op ⋙ X.presheaf ⟶ F} :
268	    α.Coequifibered ↔ ∀ (U : X.AffineZariskiSite) (f : Γ(X, U.1)),
269	      letI := (F.map (homOfLE (U.basicOpen_le f)).op).hom.toAlgebra
270	      IsLocalization.Away (α.app (.op U) f) (F.obj (.op (U.basicOpen f))) := by
271	  trans ∀ (U : X.AffineZariskiSite) (f : Γ(X, U.1)),
272	    IsPushout (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le f)).op)
273	      (α.app _) (α.app (.op (U.basicOpen f))) (F.map (homOfLE (U.basicOpen_le f)).op)
274	  · refine ⟨fun H U f ↦ H (homOfLE (U.basicOpen_le f)).op, fun H ⟨V⟩ ⟨U⟩ ⟨f, hf⟩ ↦ ?_⟩
275	    obtain rfl : V.basicOpen f = U := Subtype.ext hf
276	    exact H V f
277	  refine forall₂_congr fun U f ↦ ?_
278	  set αU : Γ(X, U.toOpens) ⟶ F.obj (.op U) := α.app (.op U)
279	  set αUf : Γ(X, X.basicOpen f) ⟶ F.obj (.op (U.basicOpen f)) := α.app (.op (U.basicOpen f))
280	  algebraize [(X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le f)).op).hom, αU.hom, αUf.hom,
281	    (F.map (U.basicOpen_le f).hom.op).hom, (F.map (U.basicOpen_le f).hom.op).hom.comp αU.hom]
282	  have : IsScalarTower Γ(X, U.toOpens) Γ(X, X.basicOpen f) (F.obj (.op (U.basicOpen f))) :=
283	    .of_algebraMap_eq' congr($(α.naturality (U.basicOpen_le f).hom.op).hom).symm
284	  have : IsLocalization.Away f Γ(X, X.basicOpen f) := U.2.isLocalization_basicOpen _
285	  refine (CommRingCat.isPushout_iff_isPushout ..).trans ?_
286	  rw [Algebra.IsPushout.comm]
287	  refine (Algebra.isLocalization_iff_isPushout (.powers f) Γ(X, X.basicOpen f)).symm.trans ?_
288	  simp [RingHom.algebraMap_toAlgebra]

290	@[deprecated (since := "2026-02-01")] alias PreservesLocalization := NatTrans.Coequifibered

292	/-- The relative gluing data associated to a quasi-coherent `𝒪ₓ` algebra. -/
293	def relativeGluingData {F : X.AffineZariskiSiteᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat}
294	    {α : (AffineZariskiSite.toOpensFunctor X).op ⋙ X.presheaf ⟶ F}
295	    (H : α.Coequifibered) :
296	    (AffineZariskiSite.directedCover X).RelativeGluingData where
297	  functor := F.rightOp ⋙ Scheme.Spec
298	  natTrans := Functor.whiskerRight α.rightOp Scheme.Spec ≫ (restrictIsoSpec X).inv
299	  equifibered := (H.rightOp.whiskerRight _).comp (.of_isIso _)
300

302	lemma PreservesLocalization.isLocallyDirected (F : X.AffineZariskiSiteᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat)
303	    (α : (AffineZariskiSite.toOpensFunctor X).op ⋙ X.presheaf ⟶ F)
304	    (H : α.Coequifibered) :
305	    ((F.rightOp ⋙ Scheme.Spec) ⋙ Scheme.forget).IsLocallyDirected :=
306	  (relativeGluingData H).instIsLocallyDirectedI₀CompFunctorForgetOfIsThin

309	lemma PreservesLocalization.isOpenImmersion (F : X.AffineZariskiSiteᵒᵖ ⥤ CommRingCat)
310	    (α : (AffineZariskiSite.toOpensFunctor X).op ⋙ X.presheaf ⟶ F)
311	    (H : α.Coequifibered) :
312	    ∀ ⦃U V⦄ (f : U ⟶ V), IsOpenImmersion ((F.rightOp ⋙ Scheme.Spec).map f) := by

1	/-

6	module
7	
8	public import Mathlib.CategoryTheory.LocallyDirected
9	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.PullbackCarrier
10	public import Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Gluing
11	
12	/-!
13	# Locally directed covers
14	
15	A locally directed `P`-cover of a scheme `X` is a cover `𝒰` with an ordering
16	on the indices and compatible transition maps `𝒰ᵢ ⟶ 𝒰ⱼ` for `i ≤ j` such that
17	every `x : 𝒰ᵢ ×[X] 𝒰ⱼ` comes from some `𝒰ₖ` for a `k ≤ i` and `k ≤ j`.
18	
19	Gluing along directed covers is easier, because the intersections `𝒰ᵢ ×[X] 𝒰ⱼ` can
20	be covered by a subcover of `𝒰`. In particular, if `𝒰` is a Zariski cover,
21	`X` naturally is the colimit of the `𝒰ᵢ`.
22	
23	Many natural covers are naturally directed, most importantly the cover of all affine
24	opens of a scheme.
25	-/
26	
27	@[expose] public section
28	
29	universe u
30	
31	noncomputable section
32	
33	open CategoryTheory Limits
34	
35	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
36	
37	variable {P : MorphismProperty Scheme.{u}} {X : Scheme.{u}}
38	
39	namespace Cover
40	
41	/-- A directed `P`-cover of a scheme `X` is a cover `𝒰` with an ordering
42	on the indices and compatible transition maps `𝒰ᵢ ⟶ 𝒰ⱼ` for `i ≤ j` such that
43	every `x : 𝒰ᵢ ×[X] 𝒰ⱼ` comes from some `𝒰ₖ` for a `k ≤ i` and `k ≤ j`. -/
44	class LocallyDirected (𝒰 : X.Cover (precoverage P)) [Category* 𝒰.I₀] where
45	  /-- The transition map `𝒰ᵢ ⟶ 𝒰ⱼ` for `i ≤ j`. -/
46	  trans {i j : 𝒰.I₀} (hij : i ⟶ j) : 𝒰.X i ⟶ 𝒰.X j
47	  trans_id (i : 𝒰.I₀) : trans (𝟙 i) = 𝟙 (𝒰.X i) := by cat_disch
48	  trans_comp {i j k : 𝒰.I₀} (hij : i ⟶ j) (hjk : j ⟶ k) :
49	    trans (hij ≫ hjk) = trans hij ≫ trans hjk := by cat_disch
50	  w {i j : 𝒰.I₀} (hij : i ⟶ j) : trans hij ≫ 𝒰.f j = 𝒰.f i := by cat_disch
51	  directed {i j : 𝒰.I₀} (x : (pullback (𝒰.f i) (𝒰.f j)).carrier) :
52	    ∃ (k : 𝒰.I₀) (hki : k ⟶ i) (hkj : k ⟶ j) (y : 𝒰.X k),
53	      pullback.lift (trans hki) (trans hkj) (by simp [w]) y = x
54	  property_trans {i j : 𝒰.I₀} (hij : i ⟶ j) : P (trans hij) := by infer_instance
55	
56	variable (𝒰 : X.Cover (precoverage P)) [Category* 𝒰.I₀] [𝒰.LocallyDirected]
57	
58	/-- The transition maps of a directed cover. -/
59	def trans {i j : 𝒰.I₀} (hij : i ⟶ j) : 𝒰.X i ⟶ 𝒰.X j := LocallyDirected.trans hij
60	
61	@[simp]
62	lemma trans_map {i j : 𝒰.I₀} (hij : i ⟶ j) : 𝒰.trans hij ≫ 𝒰.f j = 𝒰.f i :=
63	  LocallyDirected.w hij
64	
65	@[simp]
66	lemma trans_id (i : 𝒰.I₀) : 𝒰.trans (𝟙 i) = 𝟙 (𝒰.X i) := LocallyDirected.trans_id i
67	
68	@[simp]
69	lemma trans_comp {i j k : 𝒰.I₀} (hij : i ⟶ j) (hjk : j ⟶ k) :
70	    𝒰.trans (hij ≫ hjk) = 𝒰.trans hij ≫ 𝒰.trans hjk := LocallyDirected.trans_comp hij hjk
71	
72	lemma exists_lift_trans_eq {i j : 𝒰.I₀} (x : (pullback (𝒰.f i) (𝒰.f j)).carrier) :
73	    ∃ (k : 𝒰.I₀) (hki : k ⟶ i) (hkj : k ⟶ j) (y : 𝒰.X k),
74	      pullback.lift (𝒰.trans hki) (𝒰.trans hkj) (by simp) y = x :=
75	  LocallyDirected.directed x
76	
77	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
78	lemma exists_of_f_eq_f {i j : 𝒰.I₀} (xi : 𝒰.X i) (xj : 𝒰.X j) (h : 𝒰.f i xi = 𝒰.f j xj) :
79	    ∃ (k : 𝒰.I₀) (fi : k ⟶ i) (fj : k ⟶ j) (xk : 𝒰.X k),
80	      𝒰.trans fi xk = xi ∧ 𝒰.trans fj xk = xj := by
81	  obtain ⟨z, rfl, rfl⟩ := Scheme.Pullback.exists_preimage_pullback xi xj h
82	  obtain ⟨k, fi, fj, xk, rfl⟩ := 𝒰.exists_lift_trans_eq z
83	  use k, fi, fj, xk
84	  simp [← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply]

411	/-! ### Helper for `base_change` (iter-179 Lane B)
412	
413	The base-change theorem packages the canonical iso content into a named helper

416	Together they fill the iter-179 helper budget = 2; `base_change` itself is a
417	1-line existential witness. -/
418	
419	/-- **Canonical base-change isomorphism.** The pullback
420	`pullback g (structureMorphism 𝒜)` (formed in `Scheme`) is canonically
421	isomorphic to the relative spectrum of the pulled-back qcoh algebra
422	`QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜`. The iso descends from the universal property of
423	`Cover.RelativeGluingData.glued` applied to the directed affine cover of
424	`T`: each affine open `U ⊆ T` has preimage `q⁻¹U` affine (since `q` is the
425	projection from the relative-spec pullback and is affine), and these
426	affine pieces are Mathlib-isomorphic to `Spec(g^*𝒜)(U)` by construction.
427	Iter-180+ work: assemble the cocone explicitly and invoke the colimit
428	universal property. -/
429	theorem pullback_iso {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
430	    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
431	    Nonempty (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) ≅
432	              T.RelativeSpec (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜)) :=
433	  sorry
434	
435	/-- **Base change of the relative spectrum.**
436	
437	For a morphism `g : T → X` and a quasi-coherent `O_X`-algebra `𝒜`, there
438	exists a quasi-coherent `O_T`-algebra `𝒜' = g^* 𝒜` and a canonical isomorphism
439	of `T`-schemes `T ×_X Spec_X(𝒜) ≅ Spec_T(g^* 𝒜)`.
440	
441	iter-179 body (Block B): witnessed by the named helpers `QcohAlgebra.pullback`
442	and `pullback_iso`. -/
443	theorem base_change {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X) (_𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
444	    ∃ (𝒜' : T.QcohAlgebra),
445	      Nonempty (pullback g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism _𝒜) ≅
446	                  T.RelativeSpec 𝒜') :=
447	  ⟨QcohAlgebra.pullback g _𝒜, pullback_iso g _𝒜⟩
448	
449	/-! ## §6. Functoriality
450	
451	The construction `𝒜 ↦ Spec_X(𝒜)` extends to a contravariant functor
452	`Spec_X : QcohAlg(X)^op ⥤ Over X`. The iter-173 file-skeleton encodes the
453	object-level functorial assignment as `X.QcohAlgebra → Over X`; the
454	morphism-level action and full `Functor` packaging are iter-174+ work after
455	`QcohAlgebra` is unpacked to a category.
456	
457	Blueprint reference: `thm:relative_spec_functorial` (Stacks 01LR
458	definition-relative-spec + lemma-glueing-gives-functor-spec). -/
459	
460	/-- **The relative-spectrum functor (object level).**
461	
462	The object-level functorial action `𝒜 ↦ Over.mk (π_𝒜) : Over X`, packaging
463	the relative spectrum together with its structure morphism over `X`.
464	
465	iter-174+: the body is concrete via `Over.mk (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)`
466	but is left as `sorry` here because `RelativeSpec.structureMorphism` is itself
467	a typed `sorry`; once the structure morphism lands the body collapses to
468	`fun 𝒜 => Over.mk (structureMorphism 𝒜)`. The full categorical functor
469	`X.QcohAlgebra ⥤ Over X` (with `map` action induced by the universal property)
470	becomes expressible once `QcohAlgebra` carries its category structure. -/
471	noncomputable def functor (X : Scheme.{u}) :
472	    X.QcohAlgebra → Over X :=
473	  fun 𝒜 => Over.mk (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)
474	
475	end RelativeSpec
476	
477	end Scheme
478	
479	end AlgebraicGeometry

input.new_string:
/-! ### Helpers for `base_change` (iter-179 Lane B + iter-181 Lane D)

The base-change theorem packages the canonical iso content into named helpers
(`pullback_iso_affine_piece`, `pullback_iso_construction`, and `pullback_iso`,
below) and consumes the file-level `Scheme.QcohAlgebra.pullback` constructor
declared just above the namespace. -/

/-- **Per-affine-open identification for the base-change iso (iter-181 Lane D
helper 1, axiom-clean).**

For an affine open `U : T.AffineZariskiSite`, the preimage
`q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1` under `q := pullback.fst g (structureMorphism 𝒜)` is itself affine
(since `q` is `IsAffineHom` by `QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom`). Its
canonical iso with `Spec(Γ(pullback, q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1))` (`IsAffineOpen.isoSpec`)
matches `Spec((QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).sheaf.val(.op U.1))`
**by definitional unfolding** of the pushforward sheaf
(`(QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).sheaf := (pushforward q.base).obj (pullback _).sheaf`,
so its value at `U` is `(pullback _).sheaf.val(.op (q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1))` defeq).

This is the per-affine-piece iso feeding into the global `pullback_iso`
construction. Body is a single `(U.2.preimage q).isoSpec` invocation. -/
noncomputable def pullback_iso_affine_piece {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) (U : T.AffineZariskiSite) :
    ((CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
        (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)) ⁻¹ᵁ U.1).toScheme ≅
      Scheme.Spec.obj (.op ((QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).sheaf.val.obj (.op U.1))) :=
  haveI : IsAffineHom (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
      (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)) :=
    QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom g 𝒜
  (U.2.preimage _).isoSpec

/-- **Canonical base-change iso construction (iter-181 Lane D helper 2,
substantive sorry).**

Builds the iso `pullback g (structureMorphism 𝒜) ≅ T.RelativeSpec(g^*𝒜)` via
the colimit universal property of `Cover.RelativeGluingData.glued` applied to
`AffineZariskiSite.directedCover T`.

**Strategy** (Stacks 01LS, lemma-spec-base-change):

1. By construction, `T.RelativeSpec(g^*𝒜) = (relativeGluingData _).glued =
   colimit ((relativeGluingData _).functor)` where
   `(relativeGluingData _).functor = ((toOpensFunctor T).op ⋙
     (g^*𝒜).sheaf.val).rightOp ⋙ Scheme.Spec`. At an object
   `U : T.AffineZariskiSite` this functor evaluates to
   `Scheme.Spec.obj (.op ((g^*𝒜).sheaf.val.obj (.op U.1)))`.
2. By `pullback_iso_affine_piece g 𝒜 U`, this is canonically isomorphic to
   `(q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1).toScheme` (the affine subscheme of `pullback g _` cut out by
   `q ⁻¹ U.1`), which has an open immersion
   `(q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1).ι : ⟶ pullback g _`.
3. The composite morphisms `... ⟶ pullback g _` form a cocone on
   `(relativeGluingData _).functor` whose point is `pullback g _`. Compatibility
   with transition maps comes from `IsAffineOpen.map_fromSpec` applied to the
   pullback's affine opens (mirroring the `cocone X` construction in
   `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Sites/SmallAffineZariski.lean` at L257-263).
4. This cocone is colimiting: the `{q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1 : U : T.AffineZariskiSite}` form a
   directed affine open cover of `pullback g _` (preimage of an affine cover of
   `T` under an affine morphism gives an affine cover; the directedness comes
   from `T.AffineZariskiSite`'s directedness), so by
   `Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.isColimitCocone` (applied to a suitable
   precomposition or directly to `pullback g _`'s affine cover), `pullback g _`
   is the colimit.
5. The iso then follows from `IsColimit.coconePointUniqueUpToIso`.

**Acceptable PARTIAL**: the strategy above describes the construction but the
Mathlib API to actually run it requires (a) a functor
`T.AffineZariskiSite ⥤ (pullback g _).AffineZariskiSite` sending `U ↦ q⁻¹U`
and showing it factors `pullback g _`'s colimit cocone through the
`relativeGluingData _`'s cocone via a natural iso (Helper 1 as the component);
(b) `coconePointUniqueUpToIso` to extract the iso. Sub-bridges (a) and (b) are
each ~30-60 LOC of careful API plumbing; left as a single substantive sorry
this iter, with `pullback_iso_affine_piece` (Helper 1) already discharged
axiom-clean. -/
noncomputable def pullback_iso_construction {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
    CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) ≅
      T.RelativeSpec (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜) :=
  -- Per-affine-piece iso supplied by `pullback_iso_affine_piece`; the global
  -- iso requires the cocone-transfer machinery described in the docstring.
  sorry

/-- **Canonical base-change isomorphism (Nonempty form).** The pullback
`pullback g (structureMorphism 𝒜)` (formed in `Scheme`) is canonically
isomorphic to the relative spectrum of the pulled-back qcoh algebra
`QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜`. Witnessed by `pullback_iso_construction`. -/
theorem pullback_iso {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
    Nonempty (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) ≅
              T.RelativeSpec (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜)) :=
  ⟨pullback_iso_construction g 𝒜⟩

401	/-! ## §5. Base change
402	
403	The relative spectrum commutes with base change: for a morphism `g : T → X`
404	and a quasi-coherent `O_X`-algebra `𝒜`, the pullback `g^* 𝒜` is a
405	quasi-coherent `O_T`-algebra and
406	`T ×_X Spec_X(𝒜) ≅ Spec_T(g^* 𝒜)`.
407	
408	Blueprint reference: `thm:relative_spec_base_change` (Stacks 01LS
409	lemma-spec-base-change). -/

412

414	(`pullback_iso_affine_piece`, `pullback_iso_construction`, and `pullback_iso`,
415	below) and consumes the file-level `Scheme.QcohAlgebra.pullback` constructor
416	declared just above the namespace. -/

    CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) ≅
      T.RelativeSpec (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜) :=

input.new_string:
noncomputable def pullback_iso_construction {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
    CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) ≅
      T.RelativeSpec (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜) :=
  haveI : IsAffineHom (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
      (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)) :=
    QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom g 𝒜
  -- Concrete forward / backward map construction skeleton.  Each of the four
  -- internal sorries below is a single-direction calculation; the structural
  -- decomposition makes the chase explicit for next-iter pickup.
  --
  -- Forward map: glue affine pieces over the pulled-back affine cover of `T`.
  -- `Scheme.OpenCover.glueMorphismsOfLocallyDirected` packages the gluing
  -- on `((AffineZariskiSite.directedCover T).pullback₁ q)`, sending each
  -- piece `pullback q U.1.ι ≅ q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1 ≅ Spec((g^*𝒜).sheaf U)` into the
  -- colimit `T.RelativeSpec(g^*𝒜)` via `colimit.ι`. Compatibility (`fwd_compat`)
  -- is a naturality chase through `pullbackRestrictIsoRestrict`,
  -- `pullback_iso_affine_piece`, and `colimit.ι.naturality`.
  --
  -- Backward map: `colimit.desc` on the cocone whose ι.app U is the affine
  -- inclusion `Spec((g^*𝒜).sheaf U) ≅ q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1 ↪ pullback g (sm 𝒜)`.
  -- Naturality (`bwd_natural`) is the dual chase.
  --
  -- Inverse laws: the two cover-locally-trivial calculations
  -- `forward ≫ backward = 𝟙` (check on each affine piece — both routes
  -- factor through `colimit.ι ≫ colimit.desc = ι.app`) and the dual.
  let q := CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)
  let d := AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData
    (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).coequifibered
  let on_piece : ∀ (U : T.AffineZariskiSite),
      ((Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.directedCover T).pullback₁ q).X U ⟶
      T.RelativeSpec (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜) := fun U =>
    (pullbackRestrictIsoRestrict q U.1).hom ≫
    (pullback_iso_affine_piece g 𝒜 U).hom ≫
    colimit.ι d.functor U
  -- The forward map uses cover-gluing; compatibility is the substantive sorry.
  let fwd : CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) ⟶
      T.RelativeSpec (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜) :=
    Scheme.OpenCover.glueMorphismsOfLocallyDirected
      ((Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.directedCover T).pullback₁ q) on_piece
      (fun {_ _} _ => by
        -- COCONE COMPATIBILITY: chase
        --   trans hUV ≫ (pullbackRestrict ≫ pullback_iso ≫ ι V) = (...) U
        -- through `pullback.map` naturality, `pullback_iso_affine_piece`
        -- being natural in `U`, and `colimit.ι.naturality` on `d.functor`.
        -- The needed identity is shape-analogous to
        -- `f.toNormalization` compat at `Normalization.lean:143–155`.
        sorry)
  -- The backward map uses colimit.desc on the cocone built from `on_piece`'s
  -- inverse direction. Naturality is the substantive sorry.
  let bwd : T.RelativeSpec (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜) ⟶
      CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) :=
    colimit.desc d.functor
      { pt := CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)
        ι := { app := fun U => (pullback_iso_affine_piece g 𝒜 U).inv ≫ (q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1).ι
               naturality := fun {_ _} _ => by
                  -- DUAL COCONE NATURALITY: chase
                  --   d.functor.map hUV ≫ (pullback_iso.inv V ≫ (q⁻¹V).ι)
                  --   = (pullback_iso.inv U ≫ (q⁻¹U).ι) ≫ 𝟙
                  -- The first factor uses how `d.functor.map` acts on the
                  -- Spec of the sheaf restriction; the second uses
                  -- `IsAffineOpen.map_fromSpec` (Mathlib `AffineScheme.lean`
                  -- L451–460) to identify the affine restrictions across
                  -- inclusion `q ⁻¹ᵁ V ⊆ q ⁻¹ᵁ U`.
                  sorry } }
  { hom := fwd
    inv := bwd
    -- Both inverse laws are cover-locally checks: it suffices to verify on
    -- each piece `pullback q U.1.ι` of the forward cover (resp.
    -- `colimit.ι _ U` on the colimit side).
    hom_inv_id := by
      -- Check `fwd ≫ bwd = 𝟙` after composing with each `((directedCover T).pullback₁ q).f U`.
      -- Reduces to: on each piece, the round-trip factors through
      -- `(pullbackRestrict iso).hom ≫ ... ≫ colimit.ι ≫ colimit.desc cocone = ι.app cocone U`
      -- which then identifies to `(pullbackRestrict iso).hom ≫ (q⁻¹U).ι = (((directedCover T).pullback₁ q).f U)`
      -- via `pullbackRestrictIsoRestrict_hom_ι`.
      sorry
    inv_hom_id := by
      -- Check `bwd ≫ fwd = 𝟙` after composing with each `colimit.ι d.functor U`.
      -- Reduces to: on each piece, the round-trip
      -- `colimit.ι ≫ colimit.desc ≫ Scheme.OpenCover.glueMorphisms... = ι.app cocone U ≫ fwd`
      -- becomes `(pullback_iso.inv U) ≫ (q⁻¹U).ι ≫ fwd` and
      -- `(q⁻¹U).ι ≫ fwd` simplifies via `map_glueMorphismsOfLocallyDirected` to
      -- `(pullback_iso.hom U) ≫ colimit.ι _ U`, giving the identity after
      -- cancelling `pullback_iso.{inv,hom}`.
      sorry }

  -- Naturality (`bwd_natural`) is the dual chase.

  -- Inverse laws: the two cover-locally-trivial calculations
  -- `forward ≫ backward = 𝟙` (check on each affine piece — both routes
  -- factor through `colimit.ι ≫ colimit.desc = ι.app`) and the dual.
  let q := CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)
  letI d := AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData
    (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).coequifibered

noncomputable def pullback_iso_construction {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
    CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) ≅
      T.RelativeSpec (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜) := by
  haveI : IsAffineHom (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
      (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)) :=
    QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom g 𝒜
  -- **Concrete construction plan** (next iter pickup):
  --
  -- Forward map (uses `Scheme.OpenCover.glueMorphismsOfLocallyDirected`):
  --   Glue affine pieces over the pulled-back affine cover
  --   `((AffineZariskiSite.directedCover T).pullback₁ q) : P.OpenCover`,
  --   sending each piece `pullback q U.1.ι` through
  --   `pullbackRestrictIsoRestrict q U.1` to `q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1`, then through
  --   `pullback_iso_affine_piece g 𝒜 U` (Helper 1) to
  --   `Spec((g^*𝒜).sheaf U)`, then via `colimit.ι _ U` into
  --   `T.RelativeSpec(g^*𝒜)`. Compatibility with transition maps is a
  --   pullback.map + colimit.ι naturality chase (shape-analogous to
  --   `f.toNormalization` at `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Normalization.lean:136–155`).
  --
  -- Backward map (uses `colimit.desc`):
  --   The cocone on `(relativeGluingData _).functor` with point
  --   `pullback g (structureMorphism 𝒜)` whose ι.app U is
  --   `(pullback_iso_affine_piece g 𝒜 U).inv ≫ (q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1).ι`. Naturality is
  --   the dual chase using `IsAffineOpen.map_fromSpec`
  --   (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/AffineScheme.lean:451–460`).
  --
  -- Inverse laws: cover-locally; each direction reduces via
  -- `map_glueMorphismsOfLocallyDirected` / `colimit.ι_desc` to identities
  -- of `pullback_iso_affine_piece` round-trips composed with affine
  -- restriction ι's.
  --
  -- The construction is left as a single substantive `sorry` because the
  -- instance-resolution interplay between `let`-bound `d := relativeGluingData ...`
  -- and `HasColimit d.functor` requires extracting `d.functor` to a top-level
  -- definition (e.g. as a `@[reducible] noncomputable def pullback_iso_functor`)
  -- before the `colimit.ι` / `colimit.desc` calls fire. That hoisting (one
  -- additional named declaration) lifts the lane beyond the helper budget = 2,
  -- so the substantive sorry is correctly retained this iter.
  exact sorry

# AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelativeSpec.lean

## Lane D — `pullback_iso` body close

### Attempt 1 — structural decomposition into 2 helpers
- **Approach**: Replace the bare sorry of `pullback_iso` with a 2-helper
  decomposition per the lane briefing:
  1. **Helper 1** `pullback_iso_affine_piece` (per-affine-open iso).
  2. **Helper 2** `pullback_iso_construction` (global iso via colimit transfer).
- **Result**: **PARTIAL** — Helper 1 closed kernel-clean; Helper 2 retains a
  single substantive `sorry` with detailed strategy commentary; the main
  `pullback_iso` is now `⟨pullback_iso_construction g 𝒜⟩`, replacing the
  bare sorry with a structurally decomposed proof.
- **Sorry count delta**: 1 → 1 (helper 2 carries the sorry, helper 1 is new
  axiom-clean export, main is sorry-free locally).

### Helper 1: `pullback_iso_affine_piece` — KERNEL-CLEAN (this body)

```lean
noncomputable def pullback_iso_affine_piece {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) (U : T.AffineZariskiSite) :
    ((CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
        (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)) ⁻¹ᵁ U.1).toScheme ≅
      Scheme.Spec.obj (.op ((QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).sheaf.obj.obj (.op U.1))) :=
  haveI : IsAffineHom (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback.fst g
      (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜)) :=
    QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom g 𝒜
  (U.2.preimage _).isoSpec
```

`#print axioms`:
`'AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec.pullback_iso_affine_piece' depends on axioms:
 [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]` — **kernel baseline only**.

**Key insight**: The pushforward sheaf
`(QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).sheaf = (pushforward q.base).obj (pullback _).sheaf`
unfolds DEFINITIONALLY so that
`(QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).sheaf.obj.obj (.op U.1) = (pullback _).sheaf.obj.obj (.op (q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1))`,
i.e. `Γ(pullback, q⁻¹U.1)`. So `IsAffineOpen.isoSpec` on `U.2.preimage q`
(itself derived from `pullback_fst_isAffineHom` iter-180) IS the desired iso —
no glue code, no transport.

### Helper 2: `pullback_iso_construction` — KERNEL-CLEAN MODULO own body `sorry`

```lean
noncomputable def pullback_iso_construction {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
    CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) ≅
      T.RelativeSpec (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜) := by
  haveI : IsAffineHom ... := QcohAlgebra.pullback_fst_isAffineHom g 𝒜
  -- detailed strategy comments (~30 lines) covering:
  --   * forward map via `Scheme.OpenCover.glueMorphismsOfLocallyDirected`
  --     on `((AffineZariskiSite.directedCover T).pullback₁ q)`
  --   * backward map via `colimit.desc` on the cocone built from Helper 1
  --   * inverse laws cover-locally via `map_glueMorphismsOfLocallyDirected`
  --     / `colimit.ι_desc` round-trip identities
  --   * documented blocker on the inline `Iso.mk` form (HasColimit synthesis
  --     fails through `let`-bound `d := relativeGluingData _`)
  exact sorry
```

`#print axioms`:
`'AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec.pullback_iso_construction' depends on axioms:
 [propext, sorryAx, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]` — `sorryAx` is the
internally-used `sorry`.

### Main: `pullback_iso` — KERNEL-CLEAN MODULO upstream `pullback_iso_construction`

```lean
theorem pullback_iso {X T : Scheme.{u}} (g : T ⟶ X)
    (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra) :
    Nonempty (CategoryTheory.Limits.pullback g (RelativeSpec.structureMorphism 𝒜) ≅
              T.RelativeSpec (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜)) :=
  ⟨pullback_iso_construction g 𝒜⟩
```

`#print axioms`:
`'AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec.pullback_iso' depends on axioms:
 [propext, sorryAx, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]` — `sorryAx` inherited
from `pullback_iso_construction`.

### Downstream: `base_change` — KERNEL-CLEAN MODULO upstream `pullback_iso_construction` (transitively via `pullback_iso`)

Unchanged from iter-180. `'AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RelativeSpec.base_change' depends on axioms:
 [propext, sorryAx, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`.

## Attempted but reverted: explicit `Iso.mk` body with 4 internal sorries

I attempted a more ambitious version that built the forward and backward maps
explicitly as `let`-bindings, then assembled them into `Iso.mk fwd bwd ...`,
leaving 4 internal sorries (forward-compat, backward-naturality, hom_inv_id,
inv_hom_id). This form would have made the construction more concrete for the
next iter to pick up.

**Blocker**: With `letI d := AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData ...`,
typeclass synthesis fails for `HasColimit d.functor` and
`(d.functor ⋙ Scheme.forget).IsLocallyDirected` — Lean can't resolve the
instance through the `let`-bound name even though `d.functor` unfolds defeq.

Working around requires either:
(a) extracting `d.functor` to a top-level `noncomputable abbrev pullback_iso_functor`
   — this would be Helper 3, **exceeding the helper budget = 2**;
(b) inlining `(AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData _).functor` at every
   `colimit.ι` / `colimit.desc` callsite — feasible but introduces severe
   ergonomic friction at every callsite (~15 lines per).

Both routes are documented in the `pullback_iso_construction` docstring as
deferred to iter-182 Lane B planning.

## Lemmas / instances found relevant (for next-iter pickup)

- `Scheme.OpenCover.glueMorphismsOfLocallyDirected` (Mathlib
  `AlgebraicGeometry/Cover/Directed.lean:213`) — for forward map.
- `Scheme.Cover.locallyDirectedPullbackCover` (`Directed.lean:156`) —
  makes `((directedCover T).pullback₁ q)` locally directed.
- `pullbackRestrictIsoRestrict` (`Restrict.lean:497`) — bridges
  `pullback q U.1.ι ≅ q ⁻¹ᵁ U.1` for the affine pieces.
- `Cover.RelativeGluingData.ι_toBase` / `isPullback_natTrans_ι_toBase`
  (`RelativeGluing.lean:120, 171`) — relates `colimit.ι` to `toBase`.
- `IsAffineOpen.map_fromSpec` (`AffineScheme.lean:451`) — needed for
  backward-naturality chase.
- `Scheme.AffineZariskiSite.isColimitCocone` (`SmallAffineZariski.lean:343`) —
  `X` is the colimit of its affine opens; could be used to prove the
  backward cocone is colimiting via a different cofinality argument.
- **Template** for the full structural proof: `f.toNormalization` at
  `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Normalization.lean:136–155` uses the
  same `glueMorphismsOfLocallyDirected ((directedCover Y).pullback₁ f)`
  idiom with substantially analogous compatibility checks.

## Dead ends / negative results

- **`HasColimit` instance through `let`/`letI`**: typeclass synthesis fails;
  cf. the Iso.mk-body attempt reverted above. Mathlib's own
  `RelativeGluingData.glued = colimit d.functor` works because `d` is a
  field projection, not a `let`-bound name.

## Blueprint alignment

Blueprint chapter: `chapters/Picard_RelativeSpec.tex`. The
`thm:relative_spec_base_change` block (statement L334–371, proof L379–402)
covers `base_change` (which witnesses via `pullback_iso`). The
iter-180 lean-vs-blueprint-checker iter180-relativespec report
(L73–88) flagged the chapter as under-specified on the proof strategy
for `pullback_iso` — that gap is now ADDRESSED inside the Lean docstrings
of `pullback_iso_construction` (which cite the Normalization template and
the specific Mathlib API). The blueprint prose still under-specifies the
strategy; a blueprint-writing subagent could lift the Lean docstring into
a dedicated `\lemma{pullback_iso_construction}` block in iter-182's
plan-phase.

The two new helpers (`pullback_iso_affine_piece`, `pullback_iso_construction`)
are unblueprinted — they are auxiliary lemmas, not chapter pins, so no
`\lean{...}` block is owed.

`\leanok` status (managed by `sync_leanok`):
- `thm:relative_spec_base_change` statement: present (statement-level).
- `thm:relative_spec_base_change` proof: NOT `\leanok` (the `pullback_iso`
  body it depends on transitively carries `sorryAx`).

## Helper budget audit

- Helpers introduced this lane: **2** (`pullback_iso_affine_piece`,
  `pullback_iso_construction`).
- Helper budget per iter-181 Lane D briefing: **2**.
- Status: ON BUDGET.

## Iter-182 pickup

The cleanest next-iter approach to close `pullback_iso_construction`:

1. **Helper 3 (one-time hoist)**: introduce
   `noncomputable abbrev pullback_iso_functor (g : T ⟶ X) (𝒜 : X.QcohAlgebra)
   : T.AffineZariskiSite ⥤ Scheme.{u} :=
   (AffineZariskiSite.relativeGluingData (QcohAlgebra.pullback g 𝒜).coequifibered).functor`.
   This exposes `HasColimit` synthesis to fire normally.

4. **Helper 6**: assemble into `Iso.mk` with inverse-law sorries.

This is ~150–250 LOC total, well within a 5-helper iter budget. Lane D will
likely require 2 iters to fully close, possibly 3 if the inverse laws
require deeper Mathlib gap work.

Alternative shortcut: explore using `IsColimit.coconePointUniqueUpToIso`
with the cocone of `pullback g _` from `AffineZariskiSite.isColimitCocone`
